Hehe, the word "soup" wasn't meant to mean anything , and I think the domains whereby resonant attraction of 2 or more entities span different mediums, from the tightly-packed cell, to the relatively dilute blood plasma.

I'm still reading Mae-Wan Ho's 'Living Rainbow H2O' -- [http://www.amazon.com/Living-Rainbow-H2O-Mae-Wan-Ho/dp/9814390895](http://www.amazon.com/Living-Rainbow-H2O-Mae-Wan-Ho/dp/9814390895) , which has immediate relevance to this topic. Will probably be reading it for the next 2-3 weeks though, and then I'll need to synthesize the info.

I'll upload chapter 18 from the book to my site. I encourage anyone to buy the book at Amazon or at -- [http://www.i-sis.org.uk/Living\_Rainbow\_H2O.php](http://www.i-sis.org.uk/Living_Rainbow_H2O.php)

Link: [http://tanyewwei.com/static/living-rainbow-h2o-18.pdf](http://tanyewwei.com/static/living-rainbow-h2o-18.pdf)

In that chapter, she describes the nature of the cell, and how the "cell cytosol" is actually a hydrophillic cytoskeleton made up of a network of actin, tubulin, and intermediate filaments, that span the entire cell.

She also introduces us to the notion of a percolation cluster, which I quote:

A percolation cluster is the ensemble of holes or sites in a lattice connected to a chosen centre to which a fluid is injected, so that the fluid will percolate to those sites.

The most remarkable feature of percolation processes is the existence of a percolation threshold, below which the spreading process is confined to a finite region.

The percolation probability P(p) is the probability that **a fluid injected at a site chosen at random will wet infinitely many sites**
​

This will scale to electron and proton transport as well. Meaning that a seemingly-non-local event like an enzymatic reaction somewhere else in the cell, can trigger an energetic and information cascade that reaches some other connected part of the cell, without the enzyme having to be there, provided that the appropriate cluster lattice is present.

And again, the fractal nature of this structure, and the presence of actins and integrins throughout the entire body, also mean that the result of say enzymatic activity inside of a particular cell can have "seemingly-non-local" effects someplace outside the cell that is connected by this network.

How significant an effect this is I do not yet know, but I think the answers are in the book that I'm reading

\----

Higher nn-EMF harmonics will definitely change the resonant information carried within water. No clue about the implications.

Some of my random speculations pertaining to the SCN receiving information from water in the third ventricle -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/january-2015-webinar.12595/page-3#post-152928](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/january-2015-webinar.12595/page-3#post-152928)

### CindyB — 2015-01-23T17:15:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-9-is-live-welcome-comments-here.12199/post-153701>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=153659)
>
> "Molecules in the cell cannot move into any random x,y,z location because of the exclusion zone around all that polyelectrolyte strand material. It's mainly crowded in the interstices between gel blobs." Molecules can't but electrons and protons can and do.......and they change proteins and gels to innovate life.

I think there is some electron and proton concentration gradients going on wrt structured water around polyelectrolyte strands as evidenced by Pollack's work with nafion and pH-driven color change dyes. There are more protons at the edges of the exclusion zone while the EZ-generated electron density is spread across the psudo-pi cloud generated by the structured water-- H1.5O. These protons are where other potential redox reactants can happen in the interstices. Agree, that since most of the cell water is structured and the extra (accounting-based) 1/2 electron is supported by the area of the structured water, electrons can pretty much be any x, y, z location inside a structured water area. Mobile protons can be in the interstices since generating an exclusion zone pushes them there.

Agree the Grotthaus mechanism certainly gives both very mobile charged particles lots of ability to enable getting an electron or proton to more of the interior or edges of the cell "instantly". Much like holes/electrons in the source/drain region of an NMOS semiconductor, an electron can get instantly across the band gap (without appearing inside the tunnel-ox) to get trapped in the gate but it can't go to any random location. The dopant has to be right. In the cell, the dopant regions are created by losing 1/2 a proton per structured water molecule in the structured water areas and by the subsequent increase in proton density in the interstices between blobs.

At least, that's the way I think about it.

Remember DHA Pi coud abuts CSF which is 99.8% water by volume.
**Pi electron clouds of DHA in the brain form giant dielectric clouds of electrons. On a scale much larger than the inter atomic distance a solid can be viewed as an aggregate of a negatively charged plasma of the free electron gas and a positively charged background of atomic cores. The background is the rather stiff and massive background of atomic nuclei and core electrons which we will consider to be infinitely massive and fixed in space. A magnetic field further “stiffens” the underlying atomic core of DHA. The negatively charged plasma of electrons is formed by the valence electrons of the free electron model that are uniformly distributed over the interior of the solid atomic core. If an oscillating electric field is applied to the solid, the negatively charged plasma tends to move a distance "x" apart from the positively charged background. As a result the sample is polarized and there will be an excess charge at the opposite surfaces of the sample. This is the basics of how the pi electron cloud is used in the electromagnetic brain.**

### CindyB — 2015-01-24T05:08:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-9-is-live-welcome-comments-here.12199/post-153737>

> > **Pi electron clouds of DHA in the brain form giant dielectric clouds of electrons. The background is the rather stiff and massive background of atomic nuclei and core electrons which we will consider to be infinitely massive and fixed in space. A magnetic field further “stiffens” the underlying atomic core of DHA. The negatively charged plasma of electrons is formed by the valence electrons of the free electron model that are uniformly distributed over the interior of the solid atomic core. That is \_exactly\_ how a doped silicon semiconductor works except I would modify this phrase:
"**On a scale much larger than the inter atomic distance a solid can be viewed as an aggregate\[...\]"**
to
"**On a scale much larger than the inter atomic distance a metallic or semiconducting solid can be viewed as an aggregate\[...\]**"

> ^^^^^And there is another loss.........of magnetic sense on cell membranes we have yet to probe. Topologic insulators are inherently chaotic on their surface and naturally show extreme disorder of the surface electrons on cell membranes. In plasma research individual electrons act as massless electrons, but groups of these electrons form a quasi-like crystalline structure on the surface that is chaotic but has a definite mass. This is known as the "Dirac mass." The mass of a sub atomic particles are a measure of the energy is absorbed in orbital deformation of that particle. This Dirac mass of cell membranes is interesting for biophysics because of how it can interact with magnetic fields.
>
> Dirac mass results from surface particles' interactions with magnetic fields. The environment’s magnetic fields can impart order on the chaos at the surface. These fields are created by the presence of magnetic atoms substituted into the cell or membranes crystal lattice to convert it into a ferromagnetic topological insulator. The magnetic chaos eventually destroys the normal exotic surface state.......................
>
> Now consider the implications of non native EMF signals to the Dirac mass built into cell membranes. It can affect electrons or protons and alter their masses in a big way to alter their abilities to transfer energy or information. In fact, the effects of pulsed non native irradiation may also be transmitted chemically in the blood plasma. It affects the ion balance on colloidal particles suspended in the plasma (why TG rise in non native exposures) such as those in blood plasma, giving them the ability to remove calcium ions from surfaces of arteries and cells and can make cell go from hydrophilic to hydrophobic. Non native EMF exposures also dehydrates cells, so it makes sense a cell would become more hydrophobic as you get dehydrated. Hydrophobicity means the cell has less energy within it, therefore its orbital circumference changes and it enlarges. The effect on intracellular water is to lower the size of the EZ and the result is a higher blood pressure because the PVN is stimulated abnormally. This is the real mechanism involved in the generation of adrenal fatigue.

# Thread: Can a proton really capture an electron to form a neutron?  Widom-Larsen Theory and "Cold Fusion"
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-a-proton-really-capture-an-electron-to-form-a-neutron-widom-larsen-theory-and-cold-fusion.12208/>

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NeilBB — 2014-11-22T00:50:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-a-proton-really-capture-an-electron-to-form-a-neutron-widom-larsen-theory-and-cold-fusion.12208/post-148123>

Okay Jack. Thoughts on next blog... Protons and neutrons are very similar. The only difference is that one of the "up quarks" of the proton would become a "down quark." Mass is very similar as well.

[http://discovermagazine.com/2012/nov/27-big-idea-bring-back-the-cold-fusion-dream](http://discovermagazine.com/2012/nov/27-big-idea-bring-back-the-cold-fusion-dream)

> Widom and Larsen theorized that the same neutron production process could happen in nature. Recently, scientists found one piece of supporting evidence for that. In March [a study in _Physical Review Letters_](http://prl.aps.org/abstract/PRL/v108/i12/e125001) described a large flux of neutrons during thunderstorms—perhaps, Larsen says, the result of LENRs in the atmosphere sparked by electricity from lightning.

# Thread: Drop back or tough it out
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drop-back-or-tough-it-out.12210/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### david weiss — 2014-11-22T16:15:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drop-back-or-tough-it-out.12210/post-148165>

I've just started cold bathes after 2 weeks of face dunking. I skipped the ice on chest stage and I'm wondering if that was a mistake. The face dunks were easy, so I jumped right to bathes. Five days of 20 minutes at 55 degrees, but each dayis getting harder. Today I was shivering after only 3 or 4 minutes. by 15 minutes I was shaking.
Was it a mistake to skip the ice on chest step? Should I drop back and do that method for a while, or if I push on will I have a breakthrough with the baths? I don't mind being uncomfortable, but today was borderline impossible.
Background: Sex hormones and cortisol very low, 20 pounds underweight, low functioning thyroid, mild anemia, low body temperature, 5 weeks into Leptin Reset.
Thanks,
David Weiss

### Jack Kruse — 2014-11-22T18:17:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drop-back-or-tough-it-out.12210/post-148170>

follow the protocol.

### david weiss — 2014-11-22T18:55:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drop-back-or-tough-it-out.12210/post-148173>

Thanks for the reply. Ok, ice on the chest.

### nonchalant — 2014-11-25T22:00:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drop-back-or-tough-it-out.12210/post-148468>

Hi David. You went straight to 55F baths? It took me months to get down to those temps. I started in the 70-80F range and slowly went down.
55F was the goal for me, not the starting point.

### Jude — 2014-11-25T22:56:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drop-back-or-tough-it-out.12210/post-148478>

@[freesia](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1614/)........where have you been? Are you still having success with your CT?
Do fill us in

### freesia — 2014-11-25T23:40:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drop-back-or-tough-it-out.12210/post-148489>

Hi Jude I just responded to yr PM.
I spent the best part of Winter blue light free - apart from getting caught out by the light in the fridge a few times.
I stopped CT at colder temps, just temporarily 'til next March or sooner if we get a bigger deep freeze before then, in September when the weather warmed up.

I'm WELL cold adapted though. I went to an early morning cricket practice early in the season with my boys in a t-shirt and sandals. There was a brisk wind off the Sth Pole and the grass was wet. Literally every single other parent was wearing a puffer jacket, and some had woollen rugs around them as well. (I took off my sandals, put my feet on the wet grass and they looked at me like I'm nuts. I know people here will understand.)

I've been travelling a bit too - long haul flights (horrible!) but have managed it OK....drank lots, bare feet on metal (always did that anyway, instinctually), and extra CT either end.

David (OP) - It took me months to get down to 55 F too. 57 F was a major milestone I remember.  I experienced mild hypothermia once somewhere in the 60's and various discomforts until I started rebuilding my internal warming mechanisms.
Don't push yourself at that level if it's unpleasant. You might need to build up a bit of cold tolerance/warming ability fitness. Consistency will get you there.

### david weiss — 2014-11-26T00:29:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drop-back-or-tough-it-out.12210/post-148495>

Thanks for your thoughts. Guess I'm impatient. I went back to ice on the chest and will go slowly.

# Thread: Stephen Hawking
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephen-hawking.12211/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2014-11-23T02:10:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephen-hawking.12211/post-148200>

Anyone out here have a theory on how this amazing man could beat the odds of ALS for 50 years?

I really wonder....

### Sue-UK — 2014-11-23T16:21:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephen-hawking.12211/post-148227>

Not a theory, just a couple of thoughts ........

Perhaps he is manipulating time like the yogi in the senses thread

_"action, rest, action, rest .......Pulses, standing waves, vibrations etc, but action .... rest ...... It may not be the best example but I find it easier to think of time like a pendulum ..... as it swings it eventually reaches a point of rest ..... action, rest, action, rest ....... Slowing as it comes to rest ......

The rest may be too subtle for the observer to notice, but it is there...... What happens if the observer leaves when the pendulum is slowing down, or at the exact moment of rest? Does it stay still or slow down (time stops or slows down) until the observer returns? (The tree doesn't fall.....)"_

I would suspect SH's observer leaves the building during his thought experiments, so that may be a factor. I wonder how many electrons is he collecting on his mental journeys, in addition to possibly slowing time in his cells?

Another could be him not collapsing the "conventional wisdom" waveform about his prognosis. A "defying the odds" waveform maybe? Although the environment continues to act on him, if there is even a 1 in a billion chance of living more than 50 years after diagnosis, the waveform exists (or even create one ....I can be the first...)....if you are also surrounded by loved ones or supporters to help collapse the waveform and keep it collapsed ..... Who knows the possibilities...... And even randomly someone may just be that one in a billion, even if they dont actively collapse the waveform.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-11-23T16:30:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephen-hawking.12211/post-148230>

Not all bad protein folds are deadly.......the ones that cause ALS are...........

### NeilBB — 2014-11-23T16:56:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephen-hawking.12211/post-148231>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=148200)
>
> Anyone out here have a theory on how this amazing man could beat the odds of ALS for 50 years?
>
> I really wonder....

There is actually wide disparity in how long patients with ALS and ALS-like illnesses can survive. As you can imagine, it's likely a lot more complicated than is generally thought. At my institution, which sees a lot of ALS/PLS, a fair number of patients have been followed in clinic for many years.

Given Jack's description of some of the processes of degeneration involving protein folding and calcium efflux, it's actually not too hard to visualize a situation in which a positive change in some aspect of environment may slow down such a pathological process for certain people...

### Hope — 2014-11-24T17:15:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephen-hawking.12211/post-148325>

Thanks for your thoughts guys. I do wonder about this. Also read about more ALS folks beating the odds....via self healing. The power of the mind is....astonishing.

# Thread: Electrical Fields
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-fields.12212/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martin — 2014-11-23T17:51:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-fields.12212/post-148233>

I've been engrossed in the "Thunderbolts Project" lately and got blown away by what has been researched since I strayed from keeping up with my love of cosmology. When I saw "electric universe", I wondered about how AC electrical fields affects humans... and my dog.

From Tensegrity 9: _"The spins of electrons are not only manipulated by magnetic fields, but also by **electrical fields.** Both exist in mitochondria, and both are powerful."_

I'm finding that electrical fields in my environment are more pervasive than magnetic ones. I've been working to reduce the magnetic fields, and I'm trying to see a corelation between the two. A review of what goes on at a cellular level when **external electrical fields** are present is needed for me.

From Oscillitorium website:
"Electricity powering our homes produces both alternating magnetic and electric fields. These together are termed AC electromagnetic fields or EMF. Magnetic fields are present when current flows to power appliances and lights. More often than not, magnetic fields are not a sleeping place problem since circuits are shut off at night. However, **Electric fields are present at all times** and are produced by the wiring in the walls, floors and ceilings, wiring to appliances, etc. Most people cannot directly sense such fields so **measurements must be made**."

Still searching for cellular effects of Electrical Fields from a reputable source, so any suggestions would be appreciated.

Mud hut, anyone?

NSH

### Josh — 2014-11-23T17:55:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-fields.12212/post-148234>

Robert O. Becker's books, the webinar series on EMF available here with Michael Neuert, some of the blogs, etc..

### Jack Kruse — 2014-11-23T18:13:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-fields.12212/post-148235>

Magnetism is the father of electric induction..........

### Jonathin — 2014-11-23T23:08:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-fields.12212/post-148280>

> [Martin said:](/goto/post?id=148233)
>
> Mud hut, anyone?

I've been think about this for awhile - a sleeping cottage separate from the house. We have plenty of land to do this. Minimize metal. Natural or masonry materials. A couple of feet of basaltic rocks under the adobe floor finish. No AC electric-maybe some low voltage DC from battery for amber/red leds, charcoal in the walls for ambient emf absorption. Keep it simple.

Were you thinking wattle and dab?

### Martin — 2014-11-23T23:15:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-fields.12212/post-148282>

Yes, it could work. I've got plenty of land and lots of tools/experience. I could build it myself because I can build anything, but the exertion would put me at or near the grave. My post-exertional malaise is nasty these years. Still, one dab at a time...

But Mama would be none too happy. I'd miss the ol' girl for sure. Still, you have me thinking about something along those lines again... along with all the other aspects of getting to a clean space. Thanks, Jonathin! Great ideas you have there!

NSH

# Thread: Eggs a mismatch in the winter.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs-a-mismatch-in-the-winter.12216/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Danco3636 — 2014-11-25T00:31:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs-a-mismatch-in-the-winter.12216/post-148349>

Perhaps eggs aren't the right choice in the winter.....
My natural craving for them has gone down.

Chickens require about 14 total hours of sunlight per day to produce eggs. An article on Backyard Chickens does an excellent job of explaining why:

_“Chickens are ‘told’ to produce eggs by their endocrine system, a system of different glands and organs that produce hormones. As the daylight hours shorten in winter, changes in these hormones shut down egg production. Adding additional light triggers the endocrine system into action, causing them to produce more eggs. Continuously giving chickens light in the winter fools their bodies into thinking that the days aren’t getting shorter at all.”_

Another cold-weather factor impacts egg production is (no surprise here) temperature. [Chickens lay best when the ambient temperature is between 52 and 79 degrees Fahrenheit](http://animals.pawnation.com/minimum-temperature-hens-need-lay-eggs-8611.html). When temperatures fall outside of those parameters, egg production slows or stops. Also sudden, extreme changes in temperature can trigger an equally sudden stop to egg laying.

Perhaps in winter eggs just wouldn't be available.

What is your thoughts?

[http://smithmeadows.com/farm/why-dont-chickens-lay-eggs-in-the-winter/](http://smithmeadows.com/farm/why-dont-chickens-lay-eggs-in-the-winter/)

### Jack Kruse — 2014-11-25T00:39:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs-a-mismatch-in-the-winter.12216/post-148350>

I said this in a podcast 2 yrs ago....so it is true. Eggs have lots of sulfur so they provide the sulfur for the sun too.......but i do eat eggs in winter

### Jonathin — 2014-11-25T05:38:43+0200

I have an RF meter (10Mhz - 8Ghz) and I measured around the smart meter that they installed on our house. Outside there was some radiation pulsating off of it but I was more concerned about INSIDE the house. I could not get any reading from the smart meter inside our house but I also wrapped the meter with a few layers of tin foil and just left a small opening in the front. I did that several months back - before the meter came in so I don't really know how much radiation was transmitted before the tin foil.
The biggest ones in our house (now that I disabled the wifi) is the cordless phone and the microwave. My cellphone will pick up a few wifi signals from our neighbours - about 3 - 5 signals, but my meter doesn't pick them up (It's a good quality meter, one that Michael Neuert recommends)
Nevertheless, I still have a 20 gauss Magnetico on order and I'm going to put a main power switch in our bedroom because it's just going to get worse, not better!

### Psyche — 2015-09-22T23:20:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-protection.12217/post-177617>

I called my electric company and asked about the type of meter I have. They told me it is hard wired and uses power line communication (PLC). From what I've read:

**_Electrical meters using PLC technology communicate by transmitting signals via the electrical wires. Communication is normally transmitted on cables designed for it-power lines along the streets are not designed to carry communication signals. So the PLC signals are "dirty electricity" which turns the household wiring and the power lines in the streets into giant transmitting antennas. This is caused by the modification of the electrical and/ or magnetic fields created by the PLC signals._**
From reading about PLC meters, it seems mitigation is much more difficult with this type- mostly used in rural areas.. Opt out doesn't help, because PLC signals still come in to the house from the outside. The signals get inside on the utility line and also as direct radiation from outside the power line near the house. Apparently, you can't shield PLC technology in the US, only Europe, due to the use of lower frequencies (i.e. unaffordable, not practical).

My fix- most nights, I am sleeping on the property in a tent , approximately 200 feet away from the meter on the house and any power line. I'm also at a lower line of sight from the house (down a small hill).

Smart meters- really SUCK.

### persistence — 2014-11-25T07:31:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-illustration-of-arginine-pathways.12219/post-148400>

Does anyone have a good illustration showing pathways of arginine to nitric oxide? Apparently this pathway is often disturbed by too-high levels of Asymmetric dimethylarginine (ADMA):

[http://www.proactivehearthealth.net/ADMA.html](http://www.proactivehearthealth.net/ADMA.html)

ADMA can be metabolized to a lower level by the enzyme dimethylarginine dimethylaminohydrolase (DDAH).
I have very low nitric oxide levels, and I'm trying to learn enough about this pathway to see if I can get my nitric oxide higher. I supplement a lot of arginine, but my high ADMA levels apparently interfere with the enzyme needed to convert the arginine to nitric oxide. I'm trying to research if there are good strategies to lower ADMA or raise DDAH, to address this imbalance and allow my arginine to convert over to nitric oxide.

He even talked about the pathway on the recent podcast he did with two anti aging doctors in which he talked about CT. I will listen to it again and write up briefly what he discussed.

I just posted the podcast in this thread in which Jack talks about arginine. I believe NO is made from tyrosine and aspartic acid. Am I right? I am saying that off the top of my head.

### persistence — 2014-12-09T05:36:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-illustration-of-arginine-pathways.12219/post-149580>

> [Melanie Procter said:](/goto/post?id=149505)
>
> I just posted the podcast in this thread in which Jack talks about arginine. I believe NO is made from tyrosine and aspartic acid. Am I right? I am saying that off the top of my head.

Nitric Oxide should be made from Arginine and enzyme nitric oxide synthase. As people age, a chemical named ADMA rises which competes for that enzyme. So even though you have enough Arginine around to generate adequate nitric oxide, your body can't use it.

If you take Arginine alone, most of that ends up combining with Arginase in the gut and liver to make ornithine.

If you take Citrulline, that goes to kidneys where Arginine is made from it and then sent straight to the blood. So even though it is less energy efficient, Citrulline will create more bioavailable Arginine.

In my case, I cannot budge nitric oxide levels with either Arginine or Citrulline.

### persistence — 2014-12-09T09:04:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-illustration-of-arginine-pathways.12219/post-149588>

I heard the section on nitric oxide. I think he is discussing a different angle, and he didn't get into much detail.

I'm using test strips from berkeleytest.com. The interesting comment about all of these test strips for nitric oxide you hear many people make is that they must be defective because they register near zero levels of NO. They did the same for me, but now after starting to eat nitrate rich foods I get near target levels.

This is the problem with 90% of nutritional supplementation. Unless you have a way to test the end result frequently, I get the feeling that most of the supplements we take do nothing or do something unexpected (sometimes harmful). In my case I was taking megadoses of arginine and citrulline and getting no benefit.

Stay ketogenic, use CT and realize that people with NO deficiencies during summer have a severe nitrogen and sulfur deficit in their skin in the summer which you must fix during the long light cycle. When you eat a lot of green, leafy veg, and colors of rainbow that Terry Wahls talks about, you can replenish sulfur and oxygen in the skin. Take advantage of that in the summer. When it is cold, you can then upregulate the NO Pathway which works in cold temps. When does hydrogen sulfide work, in summer? What links them together water chemistry. Every 2% you’re dehydrated, your metabolic rate drops by almost 10%. Follow your BUN/Creatine levels when doing CT, as it is important then.

The problems with the above:

1) There are no double blind studies proving any of this.

2) To implement an experiment is extremely complex, involves complete lifestyle changes, etc. At the end of the day you might become ketogenic, use cold therapy, eat sulfur (something I cannot do because of a mercury toxicity issue I am fighting), etc, and you might STILL have zero nitric oxide.

Maybe he is right, but isn't it a whole lot easier to just eat five or six foods that are very high in nitrates?

I listened to the Youtube video. Mercury comes in different forms. Mercury amalgams give you an organic form that is disposed primarily through kidneys. Food gives you methylmercury that is primarily disposed through liver. Quicksilver has a nice test called the Mercury Tri-Test that is very precise and shows exact levels of each species of mercury in hair, blood, and urine. They are then able to do calculations on how well your kidneys and liver dispose of whatever load you have.

Some people react to a high constant load more than other people do. In some cases, you don't dispose of either type correctly and you just constantly back up amounts held in tissues. There is no one size fits all.

Jack makes the point in the video that the people who have problems with mercury have shot mitochondria. Well, mine are shot. Some people would say that mercury binds to the cell membranes and that helps to do that mitochondrial damage. I have no idea what the sequence of events was in my case.

### persistence — 2014-12-13T02:47:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-illustration-of-arginine-pathways.12219/post-149970>

I have definite circadian rhythm issues. Not only do I tend to stay up late naturally, but now that osteopaths have me on high dose B vitamins, these seem to further stimulate me to stay up.

But even taking this into account, on the days when I do get lots of great sleep at appropriate hours, you cannot compare me today to how I was prior to Dec 2013 (or thereabouts). I had sudden, pronounced, and almost shocking changes in energy level, cardiac rhythm, brain function, response to aerobic exercise (I form unbearable amounts of acid, so I am probably stuck in glycolysis), auditory symptoms, etc. The sudden onset and profound nature of these symptoms suggest to me that this is not about aging and not about lifestyle, but something external influenced my biology profoundly and quickly.

The best evidence so far suggests that I took high doses of alpha lipoic acid (ALA) starting in Nov 2013 and unknown to me this is a great heavy metal chelator that crosses the blood brain barrier. I may have managed to move mercury that was stable in body tissues into my brain, where it is a lot harder to get back out. It could be I have bad or inefficient disposal systems, and it may also be that I have a low tolerance for metals. I make slow progress towards reversing all of this.

### persistence — 2014-12-14T00:46:11+0200

> Back in 2003, someone on an internet thread shareed his experience with the anarchy stack which consists of alcar, ala , egcg and cla, but you can get results only with the combo ala/alcar or simple ala (the best being Na-rala , correct me if i am wrong but this form of ala is not widely avalaible online than simple ala ) & indeed, it is used in alternative medicine as chelation therapy.

I am still mercury toxic at this point, so taking ALA would be extremely dangerous. Anyone still having dental mercury amalgams and taking ALA may be moving mercury into their brain, because ALA is a heavy metal chelator that has the unusual property of being able to pass through the blood brain barrier. I started having my health crisis around Dec 2013 / Jan 2014, and that was approximately one month after starting high dose ALA. It wasn't until summer 2014 that I finally got in labs that showed mercury above 95% percentile in blood, urine, and hair, and then I connected dots and realized the symptoms came on after my experiments with ALA. I stopped the ALA cold around that time and started to investigate protocols for mercury removal. It's a very tricky business. I'm still extremely sick, but I have at least stopped the most acute part of the crisis. Prior to June I literally woke each day feeling like I was going to be dead within a week. I had a combination of cardiac abnormalities, extreme dehydration, abnormal breathing, auditory disturbances, visual disturbances, energy collapse, and brain fog that seemed like end of the world.

In Dr Andrew Cutler's chelation protocol, after about three months of clearing mercury from the blood and superficial tissues, then you begin an ALA chelation protocol. But he use ultra-low doses every three hours, to maintain a constant level of chelator for three straight days. Otherwise what happens is that each dose of ALA will mobilize the mercury without guaranteeing any trip out of the body. The mercury resettles as the ALA levels wear off, and you get more symptoms.

I know Jack doesn't believe in mercury toxicity, or at least he feels if your systems are working well you are disposing of a non-acute load. But there is tremendous individual variability in these things. I am a slow methylator, and maybe I am just less tolerant to mercury in general. I also appear to have a lead toxicity (less pronounced than mercury), and lead and mercury have an interesting synergy together. In the research literature, when they give 5% of lethal dose of mercury and 5% of a lethal dose of lead together to mice, they die.

I also may have mistakes in my notes that should be accounted for. However, I am a very methodical note taker. With that caveat, I will paste the notes I have on the Mitochondria RX webinar and the Mitochondria RX blog. I also may have added a comment or two based on my own findings outside of this website. I did make a comment on aspartic acid and tryptophan, but it was not from Jack. I welcome anyone to correct any mistakes I have made or comment on additional notes I could add. So, here are my cliff notes. Hopefully, they will take a life of there on here and people will contribute.

Nutrition

1\. Apply Body Electric Protocol: Sunshine, Walking Barefoot, a raw/marine based low carb diet, high intensity interval training, yoga, and ice bathing.

2\. Perfect Day of Eating (low carb/marine sourced food diet).

3\. Rebuild the gut flora to be diverse in species and numbers. Take probiotics and follow a good gut building protocol.

Oxygenate!

4\. Improve your supply of oxygen such being in nature, especially pine forests. Oxygenate your blood i.e. Blood Building Protocol

5\. Reduce your exposure to EMF

Ice

6\. Ice bathing and even spot ice therapy with ice packs, bags of ice or ice fasts

7\. You can increase electron flow to a body part wearing an ice vest and sitting in a hot bath (fournier effect).

Magnetize

8\. Wear grounding shoes!

9\. Strong magnetic field under body as much as possible. Sit on ground or get Magnetico Sleep pad.

10\. Walk or sit on ground before bed at night.

11\. Camp on a regular basis to improve health.

Technology

12\. Improve your body’s ability to hold energy by far infrared therapy or being in the sunset or sunrise.

13\. Ozone treatments or TENS units improve body’s ability to hold energy.

14\. Transcranial magnetic stimulation or neurofeedback.

Body Work and Collagen Protection

15\. Acupuncture, massage and rolfing improve tissues’ ability to hold energy.

16\. Improve collagen conductivity 1. reducing exposure to electronics 2. Eating foods high in copper, zinc, iron, iodine\*

seaweed

spirulina

grape leaves

spinach

avocados

eggs

small seeds: sesame, pumpkin, cashew,flax

oysters

Mind and Body

16\. Sexual activity. Face to face relations put you in an ideal situation to benefit from the magnetic field that comes from your partner’s heart.

17\. Meditation improves flow of electrons.

18\. Yoga, hot yoga and especially, acro yoga, helps tighten your collagen improving your ability to hold and flow energy.

Clothes

19\. Use of compression shirts, socks, or gear to make you more energy efficient and clothes with magnet or far infrared fabric.

Foods and Supplements

20\. Keep your blood sugar levels as low as possible by eating a low carb diet.

21\. Three plate fulls of leafy greens a day (a la Terry Wahls)

22\. organ meats or co Q 10/ ubiquinol

23\. PQQ

### Martha Ray — 2019-12-30T02:53:02+0200

> [persistence said:](/goto/post?id=149580)
>
> Nitric Oxide should be made from Arginine and enzyme nitric oxide synthase. As people age, a chemical named ADMA rises which competes for that enzyme. So even though you have enough Arginine around to generate adequate nitric oxide, your body can't use it.
> If you take Arginine alone, most of that ends up combining with Arginase in the gut and liver to make ornithine.
> If you take Citrulline, that goes to kidneys where Arginine is made from it and then sent straight to the blood. So even though it is less energy efficient, Citrulline will create more bioavailable Arginine.
> In my case, I cannot budge nitric oxide levels with either Arginine or Citrulline.

**Do you think Nitric Oxide Dump exercises would increase or deplete Nitric Oxide levels?**

# Thread: Kids and DHA

**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JMO — 2014-11-26T03:11:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-and-dha.12228/post-148522>

I posted a few weeks ago abt how my DD has asked for 3 raw oysters a day since trying them and noticing her acne getting better (her words). She's been eating them almost everyday along with other seafood meals for several weeks now. Interesting to hear her say that she thinks she has ESP. I didn't ask her anything --she just said this out of the blue. I take this as a good sign along with some of her actions and comments she has made. The DHA is having a positive effect in a fairly short timeframe. Hope she can keep it up.... I will send that thought to her.

### kovita — 2014-12-03T21:06:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-and-dha.12228/post-149127>

I think kids know what they need. I "forced" my young children into marine diet and even sometimes on the beginning I was a tough mother, there are coming now moments of rewards. This morning my 3 y old opened the fridge and said he is having sardines, olives and "sea asparagus" for the breakfast and for the dinner he wants the "pulpo" (octopus) and chorizo. They often say kids want to live on pizza and fries. I am pretty sure they do not want as long as they are allowed to sample full variety of "adult foods".

### nicld — 2014-12-04T02:31:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-and-dha.12228/post-149168>

How long did it take to get the kids to start eating more seafood?

My mother has two foster boys that so desperately need DHA but the one only like sugar and right now seems to be calmer on it. I am trying to get my mother to change their diet but it is hard.

### JMO — 2014-12-04T02:56:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-and-dha.12228/post-149171>

Hi Nicole....I told my DD that she could 'work' for some of the extras she wanted (she likes clothes). So just started telling her she could have 3 oysters, 15 min CT, or 5 bottles of water for every xx dollars. She did this for a couple months during the summer. Then she just started asking for 3 oysters every night before bed. Surprised the heck out of me (just hope she doesn't have her calculator going..lol).

# Thread: Deep CT required for Skin Flushing (and how this relates to the Menstrual Cycle)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deep-ct-required-for-skin-flushing-and-how-this-relates-to-the-menstrual-cycle.12231/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### yewwei.tan — 2014-11-26T09:47:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deep-ct-required-for-skin-flushing-and-how-this-relates-to-the-menstrual-cycle.12231/post-148533>

I want to move a question originally posted by Gretchen in the Quantum Health Facebook Group because I think it's important, and because searching on Facebook seems to be impossible  (I need the power of Google to look up older but still relevant threads )

Original Question by Gretchen (@[Shijin13](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/969/)) -- [https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/911038668909028/](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/911038668909028/)

[Jack](https://www.facebook.com/jack.kruse) - Question for you - why doesn't sitting in 50 deg water activate my alpha MSH as effectively as colder water? I get the most alpha MSH Activation in water below 45 degs. what mechanism is going on? is this good/bad?
​

Jack's initial reply -- [https://www.facebook.com/groups/872...id=911067665572795&offset=0&total\_comments=25](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/911038668909028/?comment_id=911067665572795&offset=0&total_comments=25)

[https://www.facebook.com/groups/872...id=911067665572795&offset=0&total\_comments=25](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/911038668909028/?comment_id=911067665572795&offset=0&total_comments=25)
​

My initial reply -- [https://www.facebook.com/groups/872...id=911203462225882&offset=0&total\_comments=25](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/911038668909028/?comment_id=911203462225882&offset=0&total_comments=25)

Hmm, I always thought that flushing was a sign of eNOS upregulation more than anything else --[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php...](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads%2Fniacin-flushing.6849%2F#post-84654)

The linked thread is about Niacin flushing, and the CPC#9 blog post actually talks about how Niacin is useless in people with low redox, which is also where -- [http://jackkruse.com/hashimotos-and-melasma/](http://jackkruse.com/hashimotos-and-melasma/)

I personally think that Niacin's action (more NAD+) is mediated by cAMP and AMPk, and Jack has talked about the mechanisms of how those 2 pathways suck when you are Leptin Resistent:

\- [http://jackkruse.com/how-to-find-your-inner-masterpiece/](http://jackkruse.com/how-to-find-your-inner-masterpiece/)
\- [http://jackkruse.com/intermittent-fasting-and-leptin/](http://jackkruse.com/intermittent-fasting-and-leptin/)

Peter on Hyperlipid mentions how AMPk can generate superoxide --[http://high-fat-nutrition.blogspot.com/.../the-crabtree...](http://high-fat-nutrition.blogspot.com/2014/10/the-crabtree-effect-and-superoxide-in.html)

Quote: "But the central link, the activation of AMKP, is mitochondrial derived superoxide. And, oddly enough, one of the functions of AMPK activation is the generation of mitochondrial superoxide. A self sustaining loop."

Another quote: "Let's be very specific: Glucose, under the Crabtree effect, triggers a cascade which ends up with failure to generate superoxide and this maintains mitochondrial shutdown"

My guess as to why colder CT works, is that you need the glycogen depleting and magnetism increasing effects of the colder temps to stimulate the proper hormone cascade that allows for alpha MSH, eNOS, etc .... Essentially more superoxide and better cellular signalling as [Jack Kruse](https://www.facebook.com/jack.kruse) mentioned.

**Stuff to Possibly Biohack**

[https://www.facebook.com/groups/872...id=911204875559074&offset=0&total\_comments=25](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/911038668909028/?comment_id=911204875559074&offset=0&total_comments=25)

\- If I'm not wrong, Chromium helps diabetics generate superoxide using the same mechanism which would be helpful to you.

\- HIIT before CT to further deplete glycogen before cold exposure. Timed to circadian cycles if possible, say 5 mins of HIIT during the last 2 hours of sunlight, then CT immediately after. (then maybe a meal containing at least 25g of protein after)

\- Nicotine before CT for BAT upregulation. I think 1mg is enough for the effect, though up to 4mg is useful.

\- Spot CT while sitting in warmer water. Example: Ice bag around your neck while sitting in 50F water. I personally found this very helpful, and was as simple as putting a big ice block next to my neck while in the tub. Definitely a quicker way to induce a shivering response in myself.
​

Jack referenced Chromium in Tensegrity #9 -- [https://www.facebook.com/groups/872...id=911266945552867&offset=0&total\_comments=25](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/911038668909028/?comment_id=911266945552867&offset=0&total_comments=25)

Why does Chromium help diabetics? It is the only transition metal that is antiferromagnetic. Antimagnetic materials can cause a net magnetic effect to manifest when mitochondria have lost their normal ability to generate a magnetic field. Antiferromagnets can couple to ferromagnets, through a mechanism known as exchange bias. This stabilizes a bad magnet and slows ubiquination rates in diabetics. The Fe-S clusters in T2D function poorly therefore they stimulate signaling to increase ubiquination rates in protein polymers and protein synthesis. Anytime we increase protein synthesis we are increasing the times our cells have to measure the quantum state of its local environment as its redox potential changes. When we measure things in QED it alters the experiment.

(from Tensegrity #9 section '**More Metal Magic**' -- [http://jackkruse.com/tensegrity-9-magnetic-mitochondria-memory-creates-coherence/](http://jackkruse.com/tensegrity-9-magnetic-mitochondria-memory-creates-coherence/))
​

Reply by Gretchen -- [https://www.facebook.com/groups/872...id=911270635552498&offset=0&total\_comments=25](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/911038668909028/?comment_id=911270635552498&offset=0&total_comments=25)

I think that in my case superoxide and alpha MSH decrease across my cycle, which shows a slower response at the mitochondria b/c of the PCOS/Estrogen dominance thing, especially since estrogen is hydrophobic. all of this is a result of a disconnection from magnetism. women are sensitive to the environment for a reason..... its all about the magnetism at the mitochondrial level...​

\=====

I had a post talking about Estrogen and Ketosis awhile ago -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/todays-webinar-with-dave-asprey.10486/#post-120686](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/todays-webinar-with-dave-asprey.10486/#post-120686)

Strong encourage a re-read of the section '**Iodine, Estrogen and Ketosis: The Wake-Up Call for Menopausal Women**' in EE7 -- [http://jackkruse.com/energy-epigenetics-7/](http://jackkruse.com/energy-epigenetics-7/)

There was a thread about Tensegrity #5 and Hashi's, in which we talked about the need for T3 as the seasons change -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/tensegrity-5-and-thyroid-hashi-question.11842/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/tensegrity-5-and-thyroid-hashi-question.11842/)

Summary by Gretchen -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...yroid-hashi-question.11842/page-2#post-143162](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/tensegrity-5-and-thyroid-hashi-question.11842/page-2#post-143162)

So. If you're eating a seafood rich diet and you're in ketosis. YOU DON'T Need T3 to assist with ETC across Cytochrome 1. BOOM! When you add Seasonally appropriate carbs, T3 is you little helper to leverage cytochrome 1 during High Light cycles.

So what happens when you live in Artificial light? you mimic summer. the problem is when you eat like summer when its winter, your magnetic sense can't function properly to support ETC.
​

Gretchen is currently in winter when she posted the question.

\----

**Random
**
ECT was mentioned in the latest podcast with Daniel Stickler as a possible CT biohack that Jack may embark on in the future -- [http://www.ephysiologix.com/living-the-optimized-life-episode-33-jack-kruse-cold-thermogenesis/](http://www.ephysiologix.com/living-the-optimized-life-episode-33-jack-kruse-cold-thermogenesis/)

Obviously this podcast is very relevant to discussion, but I only listened to it once. I'll probably listen to it again tomorrow.

\----

I mentioned in my reply to Gretchen above that putting an Ice Block around my neck while in the cold tub caused me to shiver a lot more despite relatively warm water. So for example, I wouldn't shiver in 12C (54F) water normally, but if I put the ice block close to my neck, I'd start shivering, and my skin would also start to flush within a lesser amount of time.

Ben Greenfield's latest podcast on freediving made me think about how the mammalian dive reflex is a huge deal -- [http://www.bengreenfieldfitness.com/2014/11/how-to-start-freediving/](http://www.bengreenfieldfitness.com/2014/11/how-to-start-freediving/)

This could be a bit of the effect of Jack's fournier effect setup, where we keeps his head and chest in the freezing wind, but keeps his lower body cold.

Of course, I'm thinking about what if you actively used water to induce the dive reflex. For some reason, the first idea that came to me was grabbing a cotton (not wool) balaclava, stuffing it full of ice, making it wet with cold water, and then wearing the thing during CT (possibly exposed to the wind), would cool the face pretty well and induce this dive reflex. No idea if that works of course, and I don't have the resources to try it on myself right now ....

\----

Why is "Skin Flushing" good anyway?

@[Josh (Paleo Osteo)](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9815/) made this comment -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...s-over-exposure-to-the-sun.11796/#post-142231](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/sunscreen-allowing-hazardous-over-exposure-to-the-sun.11796/#post-142231)

when skin goes red it has absorbed enough blue light #optics​

I posted a sun flushing thread as it pertains to the sun awhile ago -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/sun-exposure-and-skin-blushing.10569/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/sun-exposure-and-skin-blushing.10569/)

Now we know that repelling excess sun and repelling excess cold makes use of the same mechanisms -- either enough NO or Sulfur in the skin to create enough electric current.

So what is skin flushing anyway? Is the redness an indicator that the skin is reflecting red and infrared light, and thus "reflecting excess heat" (hot case), and "preventing loss of heat" (cold case)?

\=====

I'll let these topics sit in my head on the plane ride tomorrow and see what comes up.

why is the brain pink, it is absorbing blue light...

skin flushing i reckon can have many contexts

Women are more sensitive to the environment - the abundance or lack of magnetism, and that fluctuates through out a woman's cycle as does superoxide. These environmental impacts directly influence how women's mitochondria function to directly support gestation, or preclude it. This directly influences if a women gains/looses mass, becomes diseased, or becomes pregnant.

Obviously I'm still trying to figure this out.

One thing that keeps rolling around my head is the possibility that if you pay attn to circadian cycles, embrace cold, and are eating a seafood based ketotic diet is it possible a woman's cycle would naturally shorten in the leutal phase to preclude pregnancy, if she's not already pregnant going into "Winter" thus limiting a woman's chance of getting pregnant when there's limited access the food, similar to how hibernating animals gestate. My thinking on this is that it would support conception during high light cycles, with greater food availability and limiting it during low light cycles/cold just like our hibernating ancestors. This would also support natural child spacing associated with breastfeeding that extended until the child was 5-6yo. yet this probably changed as we evolved and began implementing farming, food storage techniques, as well as living indoors with some semblance of controlled heat to keep temps elevated in the living space. This wouldn't have eliminated the circadian signals from shorter days, but would epigenetically impact the cold signals - possibly allow women to get pregnant during the winter due to the "perceived" summer due to spending the night next to all that IR heat from the fire.

I look at an apple tree and see something more amazing. I a see fruit created in a tree made of carbon from the air. Trees don't come from the ground.........they take root in the ground but he wood comes from CO2. Sunlight uses a golf club swing to knock out oxygen leaving carbon and water to make a tree..........and at its weakest limb is where the apple grows out of the carbon water and light and magnetic field.........and that apple repels the effect of gravity quite amazingly for its entire life. The forces that made an apple repels gravity naturally.
> -
>     No one seems to see that aspect of life. What else do I see? Your mind determines, judges, and creates meme's that you operate under. Gravity vs the electromagnetic force. I divorced my beliefs from a gravity based world ten years ago because of what I learned about electric and magnetic fields. The land and seas of planet Earth are alive with an endless and constantly replenished supply of electrons. The specific conditions on the surface of the earth make it unique to other anodes in the solar system. Life has to organize around the conditions here and the electric and magnetic condition set forth by the sun. Since all life forms make some kind of direct contact with the surface of the planet—the skin of our bodies touching the skin of the Earth—our conductive bodies naturally equalize with the Earth and sun's charges. Your mitochondria is that sensor in the system of interaction. It senses both parts of the system with specificity and sensitivity. Figuratively speaking, life is designed by evolution to refill the electron level in our tanks that have become low. DHA is the main supercapacitor in the system because it turns light to a DC electric current in both plants and animals.
>
>     How do we know that the body absorbs those electrons? In the 2005 study I spoke about in EMF 1, it was proved with a simple experiment. Mr. Applewhite showed a voltage drop across an in-line resistor, measured with an oscilloscope, provided ample evidence of an exchange of electrons between the Earth and the body during grounding. Today, the physics of plasma cosmology has shown that sunlight is a cathode ray. The sun is positively charge cathode and the source of all life's forces. How it gets spliced, divided, and used are all quantized in the subsystems of our body.
>
>     The Earth is a negatively charged anode in this circuit. It has an endless supply of negative-charged free electrons. The Birkeland currents of both objects connect them via the solar wind bi-directionally. Anytime you have two conductive objects and they make contact, such as your bare feet and the ground, electrons will flow from the place where they are abundant to the place where there are fewer of them. The more disconnected your mind is from the light and magnetism that are formalized in circadian signaling, the more altered your perceptions of reality become. If you're well connected to nature, inflammation levels are low and your body acts in coherent fashion. In this state, you create thoughts and intuitions of what life looks like and feels like is acceptable for you. Conversely in sickness, the opposite happens and the minds thoughts become disconnected from the operations in your cells. The irony most are unaware of is that the body doesn’t give a hoot what you think about this process; it goes on about the business of survival. All of our goal's in life should be to learn what we need to know to coherently link our thoughts to how our mitochondria fundamentally work in quantum fashion. When we do, we begin to realize that our body conforms to what our mind thinks is optimal based upon the conditions we allow. Once we learn who the master of our body really is, then and only then will we come to know how the quantum world manifests in everyday life. > -   [Dan Ordoins](https://www.facebook.com/dordoins?fref=ufi)Here is my experience:
> -   I usually fast until 12-1:00pm. Morning time my energy is through the roof. Once I break my fast it is with the largest meal of the day loaded with seafood fats & proteins. Once done eating I get a bit tired. I may even take a 10-15 min nap. After about an hour or so my energy again goes way up. I then at time have a small epi paleo ketosis meal or just wait to have my last meal around 6-7:00 pm. - again ketosis seafood based. After again I get a little tired, the energy stabilizes until about 9:00ish where I start to get supper drowsy. Hit the sack around 10. Repeat by waking up at 5-6:00 am full of energy and ready to hit my fasted CT session and later workout or movement. The above FB thread, as well as Christi's comment has been rolling around in my head for the past several weeks. If you allow yourself to follow circadian cycles - regardless of sex, you should be naturally drawn to the cold. and that cold will directly influence you heath, as well as your ability to become pregnant. If you ignore the cold, your body gets the incorrect signal thus turning on/off epigenetics.

### cantweight — 2014-11-27T03:37:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deep-ct-required-for-skin-flushing-and-how-this-relates-to-the-menstrual-cycle.12231/post-148619>

I was in a bad way last winter. Extreme fatigue, could not walk up a flight of stairs without my leg muscles seizing. Looking back I can see a lot of mismatch.

Was still pushing 180mg Armour, high fat extreme keto, and I lived in front of a burning fire all winter long, little to no CT. I stayed in my family room with a roaring fire daily as I studied....the temp was high and exposure to IR light constant.

When we ran out of wood early spring I slowly began to come around. I blamed the moldy wood, the IR light/heat never even occurred to me until reading Tristans thread. SMH.

### Shijin13 — 2014-11-27T17:50:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deep-ct-required-for-skin-flushing-and-how-this-relates-to-the-menstrual-cycle.12231/post-148646>

It took me reading Tristan's comments, talking to you @[cantweight](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1283/) to understand how even small things can establish a mismatch. blows my mind I really don't think we truly understand the magnitude disconnection to seasonal and circadian cycles can eff us up, especially as women given we're passing the mitochondria on to the next generation....

### kovita — 2014-11-27T18:05:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deep-ct-required-for-skin-flushing-and-how-this-relates-to-the-menstrual-cycle.12231/post-148648>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=148570)
>
> Thanks for posting @[yewwei.tan](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6824/) ! I'm still puzzling this out. Women are more sensitive to the environment - the abundance or lack of magnetism, and that fluctuates through out a woman's cycle as does superoxide. These environmental impacts directly influence how women's mitochondria function to directly support gestation, or preclude it. This directly influences if a women gains/looses mass, becomes diseased, or becomes pregnant.
>
> Obviously I'm still trying to figure this out.
>
> One thing that keeps rolling around my head is the possibility that if you pay attn to circadian cycles, embrace cold, and are eating a seafood based ketotic diet is it possible a woman's cycle would naturally shorten in the leutal phase to preclude pregnancy, if she's not already pregnant going into "Winter" thus limiting a woman's chance of getting pregnant when there's limited access the food, similar to how hibernating animals gestate. My thinking on this is that it would support conception during high light cycles, with greater food availability and limiting it during low light cycles/cold just like our hibernating ancestors. This would also support natural child spacing associated with breastfeeding that extended until the child was 5-6yo. yet this probably changed as we evolved and began implementing farming, food storage techniques, as well as living indoors with some semblance of controlled heat to keep temps elevated in the living space. This wouldn't have eliminated the circadian signals from shorter days, but would epigenetically impact the cold signals - possibly allow women to get pregnant during the winter due to the "perceived" summer due to spending the night next to all that IR heat from the fire.

interesting ideas. Well I sort of fit into this model. I was cold and zero carbs the whole winter. It did some harm to my hormones, but the summer and summer carbs fixed this slowly and I got pregnant in impossible way (first random accident). It is interesting to note what was the behaviour of DHEAs and IGF1. IGF1 got very high during the ketotic and cold winter, top range, DHEAs was good around 300 (I am 36). In the summer the IGF1 went to the upper 1/3 rd of the range and DHEAs to the absolute top range. Estrogen went rather lower in the summer, lower third of the range and
progesterone upper third range and I got pregnant. I do not represent complete health at all, this is what I have witnessed this year.

Kovita - I think if more women tracked their cycles, paid attention to seasonal eating, light, and circadian cycles - we'd see a quick shift in pregnancy when women would become pregnant easily. Where most women today - are in some state of STRESS - thus precluding their ability to get pregnant, and its why we're now seeing a reliance on intervention to get pregnant.

The interesting thing is our dog breeder (Labrador Retrievers) is having a hard time getting her bitches to come into heat. She's mentioned this several times, as well as other breeders in other breeds in other areas of the country also having similar issues. So this problem isn't just happening with humans it's happening with other species as well....

If we don't start paying attention, mother nature is going to solve our problem for us.... and we're not going to get a say in it...

### Haley — 2015-01-15T00:16:12+0200

Is the flushing a good thing?

# Thread: Headphones at night responsible for bad sleep?

### Butters — 2014-11-26T18:30:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/headphones-at-night-responsible-for-bad-sleep.12234/post-148566>

Last night I had a horrible sleep. This is untypical for me. I used headphones for quite a time this night and before go asleep too.
Today I thought about that Michael Jackson post in the EMF-Series and the sleep apnea...
Could it be that a exposure over just a evening is responsible for a bad sleep?

I loaded a EMF-Meter app for my mobile phone and checked between the speakers of my headphones. There were fields up to 11000 mG!

Please give me a wink if this could be.

### fitness@home — 2014-11-26T18:33:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/headphones-at-night-responsible-for-bad-sleep.12234/post-148567>

Frying your brains .

Seriously best not to use the headphones, especially if you are falling asleep in them.

### Martin — 2014-11-26T18:46:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/headphones-at-night-responsible-for-bad-sleep.12234/post-148571>

Bad idea. Headphones are electro-mechanically constructed and your measurement is telling you to avoid them.

NSH

### Butters — 2014-11-26T18:50:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/headphones-at-night-responsible-for-bad-sleep.12234/post-148575>

Yeah, i know now. Was really shocked. That is the strongest field i ever measured and so close to my head. But i want to know if it can be that this One Event disturbed my sleep so much.

### NeilBB — 2014-11-26T21:21:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/headphones-at-night-responsible-for-bad-sleep.12234/post-148584>

> [Butters said:](/goto/post?id=148575)
>
> Yeah, i know now. Was really shocked. That is the strongest field i ever measured and so close to my head. But i want to know if it can be that this One Event disturbed my sleep so much.

Light and this kind of nn-EMF suppress melatonin production immediately. You have to have melatonin at night to sleep properly.
If you had been trying to sleep with a bright flashlight shining in your face, would it then surprise you that "one event" disturbed your sleep that much?
This is no different. You just can't see it so you have a harder time believing it. But your meter gave you the answer.

# Thread: Can one wear blublockers to soon at night?

**Subforum:** The EMF Rx

### Butters — 2014-11-26T18:35:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-one-wear-blublockers-to-soon-at-night.12235/post-148568>

Hey I'm currently putting my blublockers on at sunset. But till bedtime there are often 4-5 hrs usually between 10-11pm.
Can one put blublockers to soon on when going to bed later?

And don't say I should go earlier to bed

### Danco3636 — 2014-11-30T19:57:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-one-wear-blublockers-to-soon-at-night.12235/post-148851>

All I can say is what I do and works.
After sunset 1-2 hours lights start to go dim gradually, start to use candles and yellow lights.
Computer and cell phone goes off.
2 hours before bed (bed time is around 10) I put my blue blockers (a yellow lighter pair).
Wifi and other electrical appliances go off. (They are on timers)
We sometimes watch a bit of TV.
About an hour before bed I put in a darker pair of blue blockers and perhaps an ice pack on my back or neck..... More candles my be lit to replace any lighting.
My bedroom starts to get cooled down and black out shades drawn.
1/2 hour or so before bed I start to get real groggy and get the nods.
At 10 I crawl into my cold ass sheets, blow out the candles and fall asleep in 5-10 minutes on my magnetico.
My record to falling asleep when head hits the pillow is a count down to 10 seconds of deep breaths.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-11-30T20:21:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-one-wear-blublockers-to-soon-at-night.12235/post-148856>

you can.........but I dont think it is a huge issue unless you do it pathologically kinda like we use blue light.

### nicld — 2014-11-30T21:24:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-one-wear-blublockers-to-soon-at-night.12235/post-148859>

I try to put mine on as soon as I get home around 6 pm and turn all the lights down much to my husbands dislike. By 9 I am so ready for bed.

### Valerie — 2014-12-01T00:04:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-one-wear-blublockers-to-soon-at-night.12235/post-148872>

Living in LA I commute early morning...I wear blue blockers while driving (and at night if I drive home late) to limit blue light from car lights. It seems to soothe my eyes

### Shijin13 — 2014-12-01T00:29:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-one-wear-blublockers-to-soon-at-night.12235/post-148878>

I have worn mine all day - but then that's when I spend all day in a fluorcesent lighting environment. The come off when I take my breaks to go out side to walk, and have lunch. I also wear them when I drive home and its dark out...

### Dali Dula — 2014-12-01T02:03:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-one-wear-blublockers-to-soon-at-night.12235/post-148890>

When sun comes up blue blockers come off, when sun goes down blue blockers go on. Sometimes I forget, doh!

### Seamus Ryan — 2015-01-28T10:08:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-one-wear-blublockers-to-soon-at-night.12235/post-154072>

I've just bought a pair on ebay and am waiting for them to arrive. Being in Melbourne Australia at the moment it's bright till about 8.45pm at the moment. I go to bed about 10pm to 10.30pm. I will probably start to wear them about 8.30pm and see how I go. I don't have a magnetico mattress (although I think this would be a big benefit for me) but will be interested to see if the glasses help me sleep deeper and longer. I don't remember the last time I slept deeply and woke up feeling really refreshed. Generally I go asleep within 15 minutes (not always if something is on my mind) but I do wake up quite a lot, once to twice a night, occasionally more. Even when I sleep right through I still don't wake up refreshed! I have no doubt that EMF is a factor here, I also sleep about 2 to 3 metres from where the smart meter is located outside. If I had things my way I would switch off the electricity box every night, but my wife doesn't buy into what we believe on this site. If you were me, what other changes would you make? P.S. The wifi gets switched off every night (wife not happy but has put up with it). Having said this I still see a wifi signal form the next door neighbour, therefore this is still having an effect! There is no escape is there?

### caroline — 2015-01-28T11:42:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-one-wear-blublockers-to-soon-at-night.12235/post-154081>

can you sleep in another room? away from smart meter? basement? shield the smart meter! pretty simple to do.
Has your wife listened to Dr. Charlie Teo? Have you read Dr. Marino's book? Going somewhere : truth about a life in science

### notsoperfick — 2014-11-28T12:02:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/techy-issue.12242/post-148686>

Whenever I post to a thread, it automatically watches it for me without my selecting it. It's not a huge problem as I don't post very much but I wondered if there was a way to switch it off? Also, does this site not allow pms to other members, I can't see the facility anywhere?

### caroline — 2014-12-01T02:04:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/techy-issue.12242/post-148891>

Have you contacted mission control?

you should be able to pm other members and I don't think the "watch" thing is automatic - it isn't for me anyway.

Have you tried clicking on someones pic? and then it shows you how to start a discussion ...
try it with me....

### Lahelada — 2014-12-01T02:50:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/techy-issue.12242/post-148893>

Have a look in your account for the heading "preferences" there is a tick box that says "automatically watch when.." Un-tick it and all will be well.

### Da-mo — 2014-12-01T11:16:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/techy-issue.12242/post-148920>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=148891)

> try it with me....

Alternatively, click on your inbox at top right and then on start a conversation.

### notsoperfick — 2014-12-02T14:22:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/techy-issue.12242/post-149012>

It's not important just that once or twice I've wanted to send a message that I'm not sure should be public. I can live without it lol.

### Lahelada — 2014-12-02T14:30:11+0200

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/techy-issue.12242/post-149098>

The Electric Universe gang summarized the weekend with this image and the probability that it is a Berkeland Current....

Also the "Helium" reference to Feynman's work to understand Helium 3 vs. 4 behavior:

> A **[superfluid](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Superfluid)** is a [state of matter](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/State_of_matter) in which the matter behaves like a [fluid](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fluid) with zero [viscosity](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Viscosity) and zero [entropy](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Entropy). The substance, which looks like a normal liquid, will flow without friction past any surface, which allows it to continue to circulate over obstructions and through pores in containers which hold it, subject only to its own [inertia](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inertia).
>
> Known as a major facet in the study of [quantum hydrodynamics](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_hydrodynamics) and [macroscopic quantum phenomena](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Macroscopic_quantum_phenomena), the [superfluidity](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Superfluidity) effect was discovered by [Pyotr Kapitsa](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pyotr_Kapitsa)[\[1\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Superfluid_helium-4#cite_note-1) and[John F. Allen](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_F._Allen), and [Don Misener](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Don_Misener)[\[2\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Superfluid_helium-4#cite_note-2) in 1937. It has since been described through [phenomenological](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Phenomenology_\(science\)) and microscopic theories. The formation of the superfluid is known to be related to the formation of a [Bose–Einstein condensate](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bose%E2%80%93Einstein_condensate). This is made obvious by the fact that superfluidity occurs in liquid [helium-4](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Helium-4) at far higher temperatures than it does in [helium-3](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Helium-3). Each atom of helium-4 is a [boson](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boson) particle, by virtue of its zero spin. Helium-3, however, is a [fermion](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fermion) particle, which can form bosons only by pairing with itself at much lower temperatures, in a process similar to the electron pairing in [superconductivity](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Superconductivity).
>
> In the 1950s, Hall and Vinen performed experiments establishing the existence of [quantized vortex](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantized_vortex) lines in superfluid helium.[\[3\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Superfluid_helium-4#cite_note-3) In the 1960s, Rayfield and Reif established the existence of quantized vortex rings.[\[4\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Superfluid_helium-4#cite_note-4) Packard has observed the intersection of vortex lines with the free surface of the fluid,[\[5\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Superfluid_helium-4#cite_note-5) and Avenel and Varoquaux have studied the [Josephson effect](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Josephson_effect) in superfluid helium-4.[\[6\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Superfluid_helium-4#cite_note-6) In 2006 a group at the University of Maryland visualized quantized vortices by using small tracer particles of solid hydrogen.[\[7\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Superfluid_helium-4#cite_note-7)

> **Vortex ring model\[[edit](http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Superfluid_helium-4&action=edit&section=8)\]**
> Landau thought that vorticity entered superfluid helium-4 by vortex sheets, but such sheets have since been shown to be unstable. [Lars Onsager](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lars_Onsager) and, later independently, Feynman showed that vorticity enters by quantized vortex lines. They also developed the idea of [quantum vortex](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_vortex) rings. [Hendrik van der Bijl](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hendrik_van_der_Bijl) in the 1940s,[\[23\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Superfluid_helium-4#cite_note-23) and [Richard Feynman](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Richard_Feynman) around 1955,[\[24\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Superfluid_helium-4#cite_note-24) developed microscopic theories for the roton, which was shortly observed with inelastic neutron experiments by Palevsky. Later on, Feynman admitted that his model gives only qualitative agreement with experiment.[\[25\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Superfluid_helium-4#cite_note-25)[\[26\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Superfluid_helium-4#cite_note-26)

Don Scott (EU Birkeland Currents) recreated Onsager's mathematics in his work on Birkeland currents only to discover that Onsager had worked it out earlier...
