### Inger — 2015-02-09T08:43:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cannibinoid-curiosity.12247/post-155109>

bio-fractal-soul-self,
how are you going to cultivate your veggies etc.? I think as long as you do not use a green house it will always be somehow seasonal. In Finland many uses greenhouses in their garden to be able to grow more stuff and longer.... but I would never use that. I think it is stupid. The plants need real sunlight to be real healthy. That way they will be more seasonal too. I have read it makes quite a difference if a plant gets real light or filtered through glass/fake light.....

I am a fan of wild foods... I think it is way more practical to pick and eat wild plants, berries and mushrooms and eat, than have to do lots of work with gardening
Somehow the wild stuff makes me feel amazing too. I love the whole procedure of searching for it... eating it... being in the wild..... I prefer that over gardening anytime... I sometimes think, why do people pick away the weeds from their gardens.... all those healthy wild edibles.... full of nutrients... that are maybe way healthier than the carrot they grow? So much work. I never understood this. Then comes a deer.. and eats the veggies.. all the hard work was for nothing. or other issues..... too dry.. too wet.. you cant control the weather. But the wild stuff is so hard core it always survives. Here are wild greens that survive under the snow! Amazing! Must be full of life force

That said... I do love to munch on some home grown cucumbers or dirty carrots....  I just would always grow them naturally to get natural lights and temps, no greenhouses

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-02-10T06:12:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cannibinoid-curiosity.12247/post-155181>

Well, I will firstly have a garden, as I have some minor experience gardening and feel like picking up a few new skills as it is; it would be great for CT, grounding, light, active meditation, etc. as well. I know things are different in different parts of the world, but in the Midwest people often plant carrots in the winter, often in sand, and they continue to grow quite easily. There are also berries, edible acorns, grasses and shrubs, and much more, in the dead of winter, just under the fresh snow. If I chose to eat these foods alone as sources of carbs, it'd be easy to get around 70 grams of carbs each day, even in the coldest days of winter. The coastal inuit tribes did very similar - they never ate under 50 grams of carbs, and often ate 70+ in addition to hundreds of grams of protein everyday - not even close to a ketogenic diet. Technically speaking, dairy isn't so hard to find in winter, either (I'm speaking of local, grass-fed/raw dairy). If I ate a more 'Optimal Diet' like Peter at HyperLipid, who prefers saturated fats especially from dairy, well, I'd easily be able to get 70-100+ carbs a day in the winter if consuming full fat raw milk, fermented cream and yogurt, matsoni, etc. There is strong evidence that plant foods were readily available during the ice ages in winter as well, such as tiger nuts, and various shrubs and _fatty_ plants. So, I'm not necessarily worried about eating certain foods that someone does or doesn't think is appropriate for winter, so much as I'm concerned with optimal beta oxidation, mitophagy, and so on, especially during winter. CT and a high fat diet alone can accomplish this; DHA, AA, and tons of saturated fat, iodine, selenium, and sulfur from eggs, sardines, etc. alongside some PQQ and resistance training can enhance these adaptations via transcription factors such as PGC-1a and increased activity of some PPAR's.

Further, it's very easy to grow herbs, spices, and other plants indoors. Oregano, ginger, avocados, and more, can be easily nurtured inside even in the winter. Spirulina and mushrooms seem rather easy to grow, too, all year round. This isn't even including things such as aqua-culturing and utilizing the work of Viktor Schauberger and others to drastically increase plant growth sans additional food (using water and minerals alone), which can completely alter what is "normal" in any given environment. There is a show called Bizarre Foods with Andrew Zimmern, where he travels the world eating, well, "bizarre" foods. Interestingly, they're often just traditional/indigenous dishes. Recently I saw his episode of the Farroe Islands in Europe where things like fermented sheeps head, blood pancakes and blood pudding are frequent dishes. What else did they eat? Massive amounts of raw parsnips, potatoes, and winter fruits, in the dead of winter; and these weren't imported foods... they were naturally occurring for people in small towns in the hills with almost no access to the main cities nearby. So, seasonal foods = relative to what you are looking at, and also, what you aren't looking at.

This isn't to decline the validity of your approach or ideas - someone as youthful as yourself, well, I can't argue with those results, and I definitely think fat burning pretty much all year round is optimal, and in Winter, I see no reason to not be in pretty deep ketosis (30 or less carbs, 100 or less grams of protein, maybe 50-70 if dealing with neurological/psychiatric issues); but in the event that my various experiments work out (with food production), there is no way I'd decline some fresh berries, shrooms, home grown avocados, etc. just because there is some trace evidence that in some remote location some time in history those foods weren't available. **Oh, also, I plan to mineralize all my soil with sea veggies and the shells of shellfish**

I just started back on the CBD oil. And I notice a difference immediately. Its so odd to me because other members of my family as well as friends have tried higher dosages and gotten no reaction (noticeable anyway).

Immediately I experience decreased appetite, energetic almost caffeinated feeling, increased thirst. Sorry if this is tmi but also a diuretic effect frequent urination and extremely loose bowels....this happened before as well and rights itself after day one.

So I decided to see what would happen if I tried CBD oil. ughh.. lets just say I'm not doing that again. 15 drops of CBD oil sublingual ramped up my migraine beyond the point where I was functioning, yet as a mom, I don't have a choice, and must function anyway.

So... wondering how CBD oil makes migraines worse for me? I'm guessing it degrades my ECT? or maybe it needs to be taken at onset.

Any thoughts?

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=159039)
>
> One more question for those thinking outside the box on this
>
> @[Josh](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/8791/) @[Josh (Paleo Osteo)](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9815/) @[yewwei.tan](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6824/)
>
> How would COMT, MTRR and other dopamine/serotonin snps impact the THC/CBD response, as well as Pregnenolone levels?

As usual, I am not 100% certain on the mechanisms of CBD oil

Regarding migraines, I personally subscribe to this mechanism -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...-csf-semiconductors-in-brain.7564/#post-89605](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/migraines-a-deficiency-in-current-of-dha-csf-semiconductors-in-brain.7564/#post-89605)

So basically, I think migraines are an upset in the water balance within the brain, and depending on which part is upset, you'll get more water into some parts of the brain more so than the others. Malfunction of the water balance system (AQP4 gates) is likely the root cause, so the next question is why do AQP4 gates fail?

A quick review is recommended of some of the notes I made on the May 2014 Webinar -- [http://tanyewwei.com/notes/jk-webinar-may-2014](http://tanyewwei.com/notes/jk-webinar-may-2014)

Some extra technical notes on AQP4 function -- [http://tanyewwei.com/notes/grotthuss-aquaporin-4-conductance](http://tanyewwei.com/notes/grotthuss-aquaporin-4-conductance) . Note really needed for this discussion though​

When it comes to CBD oil and migraines, we've got to look at the CB1 receptor and its effects on the Central Nervous System ([http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cannabinoid\_receptor\_type\_1](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cannabinoid_receptor_type_1)). Activating this receptor does a lot of downstream ACTIVATION of the neurotransmitters in the brain. Both CBD and THC will activate this receptor pretty strongly.

Where I think the COMT SNPs come into play, is that COMT is needed for DE-ACTIVATION of several neuronal pathways. So having a COMT defect basically means that you cannot de-activate overly-excited pathways very well. This is purely based of theoretical speculation, but if that holds true, then CBD/THC dosing in people with such defects will have to be much lower; You'll still get the benefits of CBD/THC, but the required dose will be lower than in people with these defects.

With migraines, I still the best treatment is going to be to restore AQP4 function, aka: re-zip collagen. Red light to the brain seems like the best solution. Intra-nasal red light therapy, or possibly red-light LLLT is probably the best bet. (In the operating room, doctors would pump saline solution into the brain to get the same effect )

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=159043)
>
> Another follow up. how does cycloset, when coupled with DHA and Sunlight upon rising, bind to the same receptor sites? or is it just the stimulation of Dompamine that affects the SCN.

IMO yeah, it's just the stimulation of the D2 receptor, which happens to mimic a lot of the downstream effects of Leptin. Both the D1 and D2 receptors will have good downstream effects on NPY and CRH (this guy is mentioned heavily in CT4 and CT6 -- "bypass the thyroid and make CRH directly").

Leptin affects both the D1 and D2 receptors, while Cycloset will only affect D2, and actually reduce activation of D1 slightly (though not enough to reduce the overall effect).

So I basically view it as a boost to the Leptin Reset protocol.

....

### Josh — 2015-03-20T23:16:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cannibinoid-curiosity.12247/post-159074>

The relative health of the methylation pathways/reliable proton tunneling due to mass equivalence, water quality, etc. will affect the pharmacokinetics and persistence in the systems/half-life. There may be persistent intermediate effects along the detoxification/biotransformation pathways that have their own effects as well, otherwise known as a "hangover". etc...

Consider some of the factors involved in reliable H+ tunneling that effect enzyme function/pharmacokintics by factors of up to 10 to the 25th power.

All medicine is like that for me. I can take 9 Grams of Magnesium by IV - that's enough to kill the average person - but for me it does nothing. No clue why. ### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-03-21T08:46:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cannibinoid-curiosity.12247/post-159096>

As I alluded to before, one also has to be careful with what "CBD oil" they're getting. The 'legal' stuff made from hemp is crap and doesn't work, and there is in fact a large group on facebook that has been calling out differen companies selling "CBD oil" for quite some time. Not only is there often barely any CBD in the oil, it is typically filled with potentially allergenic impurities. That alone can cause migraines, body aches, etc.

It seems interesting that most people on various forums get NO beneficial effects - and sometimes adverse effects - from CBD oil, yet, when people go and see a MD who then prescribes them medical marijuana and they get medical grade CBD made from cannabis, not hemp, they respond positively.
'

>
> So I basically view it as a boost to the Leptin Reset protocol.
>
> ....

@[yewwei.tan](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6824/)

I think one of the key things to addressing the AQP4 is the proper Pg:E2 ratio. You can be rocking everything perfectly, etc but if at any point in the hormonal cycle across both the 24-hour, monthly, and seasonal that ratio is off, the gates don't work.

Usually for me that's two points in my cycle - day 5/6 or day 26. these are the days I usually have issues with migraines (thought not every time)

This cycle - I got hit with a killer migraine on cd3. not normal. I think the geomagnetic storm induced the migraine.

I also think that based upon what Jack has commented about women having a more sensitive epigenetic antenna - that its possible that if we lived in a world with just native EMF, the balance issue at AQP4 is required to inform egg release as well as influence fertilization and pregnancy. If the balance is off, AQP4 might actually result in the inability to sustain a pregnancy given environmental factors.

Yet when you live in a world flooded with nnEMF and you have balance issues at AQP4 - that epigenetic antenna sets you up for failure as system faster. the only signals might actually be the migraine, or the inability to get pregnant or carry a child to term w/o medical intervention.

As for the COMT I've never thought about its inability to turn off the neural pathways. but that would make total sense. Especially given someone who has PTSD. I've always looked at COMT from my perspective recently of not generating enough Dopamine and supporting the Liver detox pathways of P450. but it makes sense. Especially given my COMT status as +/- . I should be able to make enough dopamine, and turn off the hyper-activation of the neural pathways, but I get stuck. courtesy of the good old bipolar issues. when the switch flips, its hard for me to turn it off... when the switch is off its hard for me to turn it on... I would assume that something similar is happening in individuals with PTSD/TBI as well. @[Josh](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/8791/) & @[QuantumRadical](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/165/) alert.

much more to ponder here.... I really need to get signed up for my anatomy and Physiology and biochem class.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-03-29T01:30:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cannibinoid-curiosity.12247/post-159690>

Posting here some of the stuff that I posted in this QH Facebook Group thread: [https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/978212452191649/](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/978212452191649/)

\----
(**excerpts commence**)
\----

Rubin linked to this paper: [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/9472423](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/9472423)

Important to note that the study involved psychoactive THC, introduced into the body after it has been heated. Non-heated THC is non-psychoactive, which probably means it doesn't excite particular neuronal pathways.

\----

Whether or not CBD or TCH this is useful for something like PTSD will IMO depend heavily on the mechanism being used when taking something like medical marijuana.

My opinion is to do it raw (never heated above 40C)

PTSD is basically over-excitation of the brain's emotional centres. I personally don't think exciting it further with psychoactive THC is helpful

Non-psychoactive CBD in combination with Non-psychoactive (non-heated) THC, could potentially be a good way to re-establish cellular signalling. Binding to the cannabinoid receptors also help with the downstream dopamine pathways, which ultimately feedback to Leptin function (improving it).

\----

IMO cannabis works by producing Reactive Oxygen Species (ROS) in the appropriate cells to re-establish cellular signalling, while decreasing ROS production in other cells (see papers below). This would normalise the ROS response to "tune the system to work together".

Chemical reduction of ROS induces biophoton emission at the site of reaction, which transmits information both at this site of reaction, and at the original ROS generation site (due to quantum entanglement)

\- Control over Macrophage ROS production -- [http://cardiovascres.oxfordjournals.org/content/early/2009/07/31/cvr.cvp240.short](http://cardiovascres.oxfordjournals.org/content/early/2009/07/31/cvr.cvp240.short)

\- Reduction of Oxidative Load on rat PC12 neural cells -- [http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/j.1471-4159.2003.02327.x/full](http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/j.1471-4159.2003.02327.x/full)

\- Killing malfunctioning cells with ROS over-production -- [http://molpharm.aspetjournals.org/content/70/3/897.short](http://molpharm.aspetjournals.org/content/70/3/897.short)

IMO cannabis was designed by Nature as a plant to aid with this ROS signalling during the Autumn transition period, when light levels are decreasing, and it isn't cold enough for matter to be condensed.

The body will need to find more ways of generating signalling molecules, and cannabis is one of those ways.

Overview -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/reverse-electron-flow.12744/#post-154292](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads%2Freverse-electron-flow.12744%2F#post-154292)

\----

^^ Implication of the previous section is that I do not think cannabis is useful for people who already have good cellular signalling, other than to have a good time  (which I fully support)

It is designed as a stress mitigation strategy, and can be useful acutely for that purpose (again, I believe raw is best), or chronically in sick populations until health is restored.

Don't forget that the stressor still needs to be removed  Using cannabis as a band-aid is a losing strategy.

....

# Thread: Experiences with Fish Oil
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/experiences-with-fish-oil.12251/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2014-11-29T21:17:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/experiences-with-fish-oil.12251/post-148780>

So I've been taking Green Pastures fermented Cod Liver Oil and I'd like to know I'm not just wasting my money. I don't have much..working at Walgreens 10.90 an hour after 3 raises and I've been working 3 years so about 1 a year.

It feels like I've been spending tons on supplements...and I'm not even sure of their efficacy.

[http://www.walgreens.com/store/c/fi...ry-supplement-softgels/ID=prod6108946-product](http://www.walgreens.com/store/c/finest-nutrition-fish-oil-pearls-dietary-supplement-softgels/ID=prod6108946-product)

14 cents a serving for these easy to swallow pills that use Wild Caught fish.

[http://www.greenpasture.org/public/Products/CodLiverOil/index.cfm](http://www.greenpasture.org/public/Products/CodLiverOil/index.cfm)

37 cents for the liquid and 50 cents for the capsules.

The price per serving amounts aren't that much different but it adds up in money, and I'm wondering if it really makes a big difference. Has anyone had any experience trying different kinds and actually noticing differences either in lab results or even just how you feel?

Part of me wants to take the Green pasture capsules because its easier, but its still supposed to be high quality fermented cod liver oil which I've heard so many good things about. Are they just as good as the liquid though...they basically are just putting the liquid in a capsule right? The ingredients in the capsules don't sound bad.

Also how much better is it if at all than the Fish Oil pearls...they are supposedly also wild caught fish so is it any different. I don't need the pearls for their size I have no problem swallowing large pills, but the cost difference though seemingly small when you look at the per serving cost when you look at the cost as a whole its quite a big difference.

9.99 and thats not even taking into account that its buy one get one free so its actually 4 cents a serving. That doesn't actually matter if its not absorbed and used as well, but if it is than I'm just paying for nothing getting the Green Pastures.

Of course there is also the issue of product quality..how much nutrient capacity..how it varies between products and I would think there was a higher amount in the more expensive one, but I have no clue on that..

If anyone has any experience taking these different types etc..or any advice please let me know or any advice on supplements in general. I don't currently do the shopping for my house and I currently have several different supplements I've bought to fill the gaps I may have in nutrition.

I'll post all my supplements in a bit.

Thanks

### CTforlife — 2014-11-29T22:15:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/experiences-with-fish-oil.12251/post-148788>

Food is your supplement. Not a pill! Food is your supplement, rewiring this into your brain will take time, but we have an awful lot of that here ! Don't ever look at a multivitamin and think that's how you get nutrients vitamins etc.... Food is the supplement package , and WE all know supplements are additions , not the source.
Look at the definition of a supplement I'm being quite literal throughout this post .

You will begin to see why I call food a supplement, because it helps, but it's not the end all be all. (Read emf 2!)

Right now you're thinking about what you can add to food, that's wrong .
Think about what food you can supplement to your new lifestyle to improve your n=1 game. (Becoming optimal)

I'm talking about emf 2, food is the supplement not the answer. Meaning it helps the new lifestyle you are seeking. See why I said and why I said it?

You are trying to supplement your food.

No!!! Use food to supplement your lifestyle!!!!!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/experiences-with-fish-oil.12251/post-149870>

> [Raiken3712 said:](/goto/post?id=148864)
>

I took green pastures for some time and results were mixed and it hurt the wallet. I also have tried many other supplements to fight a lifestyle that I was paying a heavy metabolic price for. I spent a lot.... a lot of money on supplements over the years, thousands! In october I did an experiment where I ate about 400 oysters over the course of a month. I felt better than I had on **any** supps I had tried. Now you may say, that is a lot but I proved a point to myself, I can't outsmart mother nature. These foods already have DHA, iodine, magnesium, niacin and other cofactors.

The food gets the DHA in the right places because of it being in the SN2 position. Be smart about it, eat oysters, buy whole fish and save the bones and make broths and eat some of the organ meats. Ask your local fishmonger for some fish heads, sprinkle some paprika in there go to town.

Do some CT to optimize uptake and avoid blue light to avoid breakdown. Think about everytime you cheat you are tossing money down drain. You have gotta look at your resources and innovate...

# Thread: Reaction from Betaine HCL
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reaction-from-betaine-hcl.12255/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2014-11-30T22:15:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reaction-from-betaine-hcl.12255/post-148863>

Hi,

I have been taking betaine hcl (with pepsin) again over the last week. Anyway I've been taking it in higher amounts without burning. I have found it helps with some of my symptoms.

Anyway today I took 20 x 500mg for breakfast and the same for tea. About 1.5 hours after taking the last dose I came up in something similar to a niacin flush reaction. My body got really warm and itchy, I could feel lots of blood pumping to my head and it felt sun burned. At no point have I felt burning or hotness in my stomach. I also came up in little almost like spots.

What is the cause of this reaction?

### seanb4 — 2014-12-01T17:00:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reaction-from-betaine-hcl.12255/post-148935>

It seemed very similar to a niacin flush but my throat is still tight and it is painful to breath deep. Wonder if it is a reaction to the sheer amount of acid or some of the ingredients... Betaine Hydrochloride, Pepsin (Porcine), Hypromellose capsule, Leucine, Silicon Dioxide.

### kovita — 2014-12-01T19:00:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reaction-from-betaine-hcl.12255/post-148944>

have you changed brand, lot, package, for how long have you been using this product etc...if you can exclude reaction to components, you can start to look into what happened internally. It sounds like massive histamin release. Remember it could happen even with a substance you have been taking regulary for years. I remember last time you were mentioning helicobacter p. as being probably culprit in your story. Maybe look into whether there are any cases, situations where this bug could be related to histamin release? I am just trying to give ideas.

### seanb4 — 2014-12-01T20:06:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reaction-from-betaine-hcl.12255/post-148948>

Thanks for the advice kovita.

It seems like a milder version of Anaphylaxis. Probably something to do with histamine like you said.

Anyway it seems that histamine is released when the stomach wants to secrete acid. I was taking high amounts of acid so I doubt this is the case.

If I do have h pylori then maybe it was unhappy with the higher acid levels and released histamine to somehow defend itself. Apparently it reduces diamine oxidase which helps detoxify histamine so that could have influenced it.

Thing is if it was due to h pylori being irratated, I thought I would have had a smaller reaction when taking less dose....

# Thread: CONFIRMED EBOLA CASE TREATED AND QUICKLY RESOLVED DIRECT IV OZONE

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ColinGorham — 2014-12-01T17:41:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confirmed-ebola-case-treated-and-quickly-resolved-direct-iv-ozone.12262/post-148937>

[https://www.facebook.com/DrRobertJRowen?fref=ts](https://www.facebook.com/DrRobertJRowen?fref=ts)

CONFIRMED EBOLA CASE TREATED AND QUICKLY RESOLVED
Dear Facebook Family,
You all have been devoted to our mission, and therefore you all should be the first to know the most fabulous news you will hear regarding the Ebola crisis. Dr. Robert Rowen and Dr. Howard Robins now openly announce to the world the first CONFIRMED case of Ebola which has been apparently cured with the Rowen-Robins Ebola protocol, which features the Robins method of DIV ozone administration.
If you’ve been reading the news, Dr. Komba Songu M’Briwa, a senior military physician in Sierra Leone, and one who had been with the now deceased Dr. Salia, contracted the disease, likely from embracing Dr. Salia when Salia’s first test came back negative. Dr. M’Briwa made international news on November 27, 2014 with the announcement that he contracted Ebola.
[http://www.washingtonpost.com/world...1fbd54-7627-11e4-a755-e32227229e7b\_story.html](http://www.washingtonpost.com/world/sierra-leone-physician-who-treated-martin-salia-diagnosed-with-ebola/2014/11/27/af1fbd54-7627-11e4-a755-e32227229e7b_story.html)
Dr. M’Briwa was presented with the offer of our ozone protocol, and signed the informed consent. This is the email Rowen got on November 27, 2014, from Dr. M. Jalloh, who became the managing physician of this case:
“Hello Dr. Rowen, This is Dr. M I Jalloh the guy who brought you the tennis shoes at Hastings \[Ebola treatment center\]. Hope you are fine.
I am not the one directly treating Dr. Songu M'brewa but I coordinate his treatment. He is presently stable with no diarrhea or vomiting. He is receiving the ozone (55 mls) twice a day. He is also receiving Advance Cellular Silver, Vitamin C and Glutathione. The management is promising. They will probably run another Ebola test after ten doses of ozone to check if he is still positive and the viral load…Will keep you updated.”

Dr. Jalloh has indeed kept us updated. Today, November 30, only 3 days later, Dr. Jalloh has informed Dr. Rowen that Dr. M’Briwa promptly resolved his fever and had no further development of the usual Ebola symptoms of horror. In fact, he’s already been moved out of the Ebola sick unit into the “recovery” unit. Furthermore, a second test (for viral load) after treatment commenced was negative for Ebola, and a third test will be performed on Monday, December 1. You will be sure the naysayers will, like they often do when a confirmed cancer case “mysteriously” recovers at the hands of unconventional doctors, that they might say the same here. But it will be hard to do on this one. He had direct contact with Dr. Salia, he was personally treating Ebola, and he was housed in the Ebola treatment unit after testing positive. No one will be able to deny this one. And, he recovered exactly as we predicted would be the course of ozone-treated Ebola.
Now, here is an interesting twist to the story. You will remember our announcement of the first doctor we reported who jabbed himself with an infected needle? That was Dr. Sekou Kanneh, who was scared for his livelihood and life should he get a positive test, be quarantined, and then be denied ozone. Dr. Kanneh was the hands-on treating physician for Dr. M’Briwa, and was supervised in the program by Dr. Jalloh.
And finally, today, Dr. Rowen spontaneously got a delightful email from someone he didn’t recognize. It said, “Hi Dr . Its been a while since u left sierra Leone. How are u doing? We miss d ozone therapy.” When an email was sent inquiring as to the identity of the sender, this returned: “Am d head nurse nurse at Hastings ebola treatment centre. U gave me your email d last time u went there. Actually, we are taking ozone but my colleagues at other treatment centres around d country are not .
Sent from my BlackBerry® smartphone from Airtel Sierra Leone.
This appears to be the most promising advance in the Ebola crisis, and we are hopeful that everything we are being told from our sources is accurate. We are anxiously awaiting additional information regarding Dr. M'Briwa (and Dr. Kanneh). Folks, we wish to thank you for your support, your prayers and your love. We are bringing this case to the world now, and you can certainly help by getting it to news media, friends and family.
Sincerely, Robert Rowen, MD and Howard Robins, DPM

### ColinGorham — 2014-12-01T17:46:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confirmed-ebola-case-treated-and-quickly-resolved-direct-iv-ozone.12262/post-148938>

From Dr. Robert Rowen's FACEBOOK page
Ebola vaccine is in the works:
[http://www.washingtonpost.com/natio...b63c42-75d6-11e4-8893-97bf0c02cc5f\_story.html](http://www.washingtonpost.com/national/health-science/ebola-vaccine-seems-safe-in-first-stage-testing/2014/11/26/6bb63c42-75d6-11e4-8893-97bf0c02cc5f_story.html)

Now I want you all to consider the press this piece is getting compared to the silence we have gotten. Here is an international story on an Ebola vaccine under way. Yes, it was done on human volunteers. And yes, it showed promise in that antibodies developed in the subjects. Now consider the flack Howard and I are taking for not actually having in hand, as yet, a blood test on our first patient. This vaccine story makes international news even though we will NEVER EVER see proper scientific testing of the effectiveness of this vaccine, in that we can NEVER EVER tempt fate by exposing those vaccinated to real Ebola. Why? They could still get infected. The vaccine might not provide full immunity, or it might provide immunity to a different strain than what the virus has morphed to. So, no proof of anything here with relation to a real case, but the story makes huge news as a promising approach.

You might ask, “Why?” I’ll tell you the same answer as always. Follow the money trail!. The vaccine will make bazillions for the maker in Africa, and perhaps even more here from people scared out of their minds about Ebola, or if/when we see cases coming up from bioterrorism. If there is bioterrorism, and Ebola starts making the rounds where it should not, watch the pundits run for this product. In the meantime, Dr. Robins and I have shown a MOST promising therapy to treat the actual disease which deserves attention and formal study, which no one will profit from since you cannot patent ozone.

[Newsscience.org](http://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2FNewsscience.org%2F&h=6AQFs4rac&s=1) tells us: “The driver \[of the vaccine process\] is really the productivity of the cell line,” Ballou says. He puts the most optimistic timeline for 100,000 to 500,000 doses at 9 months—and the cost at $25 million.

Do the math, one dose of the vaccine now underway by PHARMA giant GSK, will cost, at best, $50 and at worst $250. And, it’s a crap-shoot. Even if it works, it could cover this Ebola outbreak only. Not others, and not offer a scrap of protection against other plagues that will certainly come.

So, let’s round the cost figure off to just $100. If just one million doses are prepared for Sierra Leone, that’s $100,000,000 cost for just that country. And, if Ebola becomes a bioterrorist phenomenon, well, consider taking out a mortgage on the entire world. On the other hand, if there is a $40 risk free treatment for just those who get the disease, and you don’t need to take the risk of the vaccine, and the $40 treatment can also treat all the potential strains that might emerge, that might put a crimp into the profiteering on this malady, and take the Heavenly reward away from the terrorists who won’t be able to kill people with a cure down the street.

Oh, sorry. I don’t mean to be jumping to conclusions. Just want to present the facts.
Stay tuned. I think I’ll have some more hot information for you soon.

Hope you all have a wonderful and SAFE holiday weekend.

### ColinGorham — 2014-12-02T20:38:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confirmed-ebola-case-treated-and-quickly-resolved-direct-iv-ozone.12262/post-149028>

From Robert Rowen's FB page:
“Suppose they gave a cure for Ebola, and nobody came.” I think that might be the name of the movie that comes out of the adventure Dr. Robins and I had in Sierra Leone. Based on the information we are able to get on our two cases, the Ebola virus, as horrifically lethal as it is left alone in the human body (about 60% mortality) in the current epidemic, this virus appears to have an Achilles heel so vulnerable, but yet so simple, that scientists won’t consider it, and the press ignores it.
Mind you, the Rowen-Robins protocol called for treatment of active Ebola at least 4 times daily with the powerful ozone treatment of direct intravenous gas (DIV) of the Robins method. Dr. Robins and I believed the virus so lethal that it would take several days of frequent administration to cure it, but that we would see improvement in as little as 48-72 hours. It appears, based on the feedback from Sierra Leone, that we were wrong. Dr. Kanneh was better almost immediately and did continue his treatments for at least the 10 days we recommended. But Dr. M’Briwa, who had a confirming test for Ebola, was 100% better after just 2-3 days (possibly less) of ozone therapy, and after a posting was made on Facebook in Sierra Leone which was attempting to raise money for ZMAPP to save his life. Furthermore, a follow up test for Ebola was negative in just 2 days.

Remember the War of the Worlds by H.G. Wells? In that tale, Martians with invincible power came to the planet and went on an unstoppable roll. It was so ghastly that people who listened to the fictional story on radio (broadcast by Orson Wells) actually committed suicide. Those who killed themselves might have survived if they listened just a bit longer. As the story unfolded, within just a few days of the invasion, the Martians were stopped! And suddenly! How? By the simplest of all things on the planet – microbes, to which they had no immunity, but we do!

We are just two cases into Ebola. The unstoppable march of symptoms virtually instantly stopped in both these cases. And in the one documented positive case, the patient became negative on a second test just a few days after the first. The Facebook page calling for donations for ZMAPP to save the life of Dr. M’Briwa announced his personal recovery. Additionally, I got an email from the chief nurse at the Hastings Ebola unit telling me they were rejoicing. And yes, he did get ozone, but not nearly what we thought was necessary.
So, could Ebola be the equivalent of “invincible” Martians? Does it really have a target painted on it that ozone or other oxidants might move on like a “smart bomb”? We won’t know unless and until those responsible for the lives of others take note – including organizations wedded to Pharma, like governments bought by Pharma, like the WHO, whose Ebola officer (Theo Grace) emailed me that the use of ozone on humans is CRIMINAL, like news media fully biased by Pharma bought ads, etc. Zmapp treatment may cost many hundreds of dollars, perhaps more, and it takes many months to grow it in a GMO tobacco plant. While thousands die, Theo Grace, presented with ozone as a definite safe, and highly possible cure for Ebola deemed ozone use to be criminal, yet permits a 60% death rate to rampage.

Ozone? It’s here right now. It’s in the atmosphere. It’s in many city water purification systems. In Sierra Leone the material cost to administer treatment is about $0.75 per session. Based on two doctors recoveries and the limited information we have at hand from their cases, the material cost for cures for them cost less than $10 (not including supplements).
I guess ozone is just not sexy enough like “ZMAPP” for the Pharma owned/controlled authorities/press to consider. Hmmm, maybe because its simply not costly enough or patentable either. Perhaps because unknown “nobodies” like Rowen and Robins can do it absent their control?
Honestly, while it appears we have two full quick cures under our belt, the treatment deserves study. Until then, we don’t know ABSOLUTELY for sure/ And, our line of communication from Sierra Leone is about as good as a satellite transmission from Neptune. But, with the WHO in charge, and huge Profit motivated interests at large, and millions coming in to incinerate bodies and bury the dead, getting ozone into use to actually SAVE lives has been painfully slow in coming, even as more people die, children orphaned and families broken.
However, while hundreds are still dying of Ebola, you can be sure that when a government official gets scared, as exactly happened when I was on the ground there in October (see my previous postings), he will come running for ozone to save his xxx. Then “there will be a cure for Ebola and “he” will have come”.

Finally, I close by referring you to the solicitations for donations for the military doctor for the expensive and scarcely available ZMAPP and the doctor's own announcement that he has recovered. Interesting that the ONLY note about ozone on that page came from me.
[https://www.facebook.com/…/ZMAPP-for-a-life…/754066924678683](https://www.facebook.com/pages/ZMAPP-for-a-life-saver-Captain-Dr-Komba-Songu-Mbriwa/754066924678683)

I solicit your comments, please. And, I will post the extraordinarily hostile comments of WHO Ebola official Theo Grace in the very near future.

# Thread: Pebbles Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pebbles-journal.12264/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Pebbles — 2014-12-01T23:15:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pebbles-journal.12264/post-148959>

Hi! So lets start my journal......

About myself - 49 yo and struggling since 10 years with weight issues, before being 50 kg at 164cm.
2003 had an abortion due to shrinking embrio at 10 weeks, later never again pregnant....
2005-2006 Went up to 60kg, than followed by 3 x 1 month sibutramine ( I know was stupid, but I worked there) which ended up -5kg/+10kg x 3 kinda yo-yo-effect.
2007 had an accident- falling on wet stairs, ending up with fissures all over my pelvis, sacrum, pubis, ... and dislocated coccyx - which did not help with my weight and took me 1,5 years recovery
HBP started beyond some half side kinda migraines which could not be alleviated - no meds helped - appeared regularly with wether changes - cold and warm fronts as well. Full check - hormonally neg, ORL- neg prolactinoma neg, migraines may be due to TMJ, so extracted wise teeth, made a root resection ( in wain), but my ear pain still accompanies my migraines ( similar to otitis media). Lost half of my hair- due to hormone imbalance or due to many X-rays or due to beta-blockers- I will never find out.
2009 turned to paleo and started a daily 1,5 hrs training with my GSD, lost 20kg in 5 month than the weight loss stopped. As a plus, migraines are more rare and bearable - instead of 2/week, max 1-2/month.
2011-12 due to work- business travels- my training was reduced to only 2-3x/week, which ended up in additional +10kg's. ( or could be related to neurotransmitters type?)
2012 Nov my father deceased and I got an LBBB
2014 Sept- by a routine turned out a thyroid nodule, so full endo check.....
Started CT but ended up with cystitis than nephritis - 4 doses of antibiotics and now recovering my gut flora.... struggling with low iron and sleepy mid days ( between 12-15:00)
I never had circadian cycle issues - all my life waking up at 6:00 and sleeping at 10:00 - wherever I am - home/bed, or sitting in a restaurant ( sometimes painful).
Tried magnetic bed lining ( European brand) but I am getting more tired, all my muscles and joints are aching. Same happened when trying magnetic shoe insoles or a magnetic bracelet on the time being.
Have no EMF in the bedroom and have flux on my laptop.

As related to food, need far more mollusc.

Will come back soon, with my labs, to have the final verdict for Leptin RX
My 23andme are on the way as well.

Good night!

### caroline — 2014-12-02T00:25:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pebbles-journal.12264/post-148965>

Hi pebbles and welcome to journal land! That isn't too long at all!

a few questions.....

where do you work? non native EMF at home, work? power lines??? smart meters etc?
Have you read thru the leptin RX?
leave at least four hours between meals and before bed
carbs?

what kind of water do you drink?

do you have blue blocker glasses?

grounding? do you get outside and hug a tree or two???

health history of your Mom? grandma?

your magnetic pad and insoles could be a problem ...... drawing dirty electricity TO you instead of away from you.

### fitness@home — 2014-12-02T02:34:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pebbles-journal.12264/post-148983>

Great start Pebbles. Time to dig deeper and see what you find.

### Pebbles — 2014-12-02T12:01:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pebbles-journal.12264/post-149007>

Hi Caroline!
Read Leptin RX)
Work place depends... I am a freelancer consultant, so everywhere where I get a contract, sometimes this means sleeping in a hotel during weekdays and returning home only for weekend but sometimes working from home as well.
We live in a house, classical brick construction, mineral isolation, ceramic tiles ( so walking barefoot in house is grounding as well), no smart meters, a little garden with fruit trees ( apricot, fig, sawer cherries, cherries, grapes, mint, lemon balm, parsley and colourful roses all around the fence) and a big 30-40 yrs walnut tree - grounding in the garden)
Summertime we often go to a lake or to a forest nearby. Laying in the sun in the dirt, soaking my legs in the water - and this is an instinct habit, Swimming tog w my GSD.
Water - we have a spring also close to us, we have carbon water filter and buying the cleanest mineral water (won several european eau-oscars). Unfortunately the bathroom water is not filtered, so still getting chlorine while showering.
No, have no blue blocker glasses yet. Living in the suburbs of capital Budapest, the place has a silent village like lights - so almost nothing.....is not a light polluted place.

About my maternal lineage:
My grand-grandma lived 91 yrs, in full physical and mental health - never ever been to a doc, never took meds - she just used a few med plants collected by her own and she had always cold water showers- her own CT). She was small - about 150cm and thin.
My grandma- unfortunately died at age 26- due to a mistake - doc cut an artery during an op, the op was not needed, she had only an uterus/ovaries inflammation due to a cold.
My mom - she is now 71, she had cyst on her kidneys ( last 7 yrs) -PCKD. Urol, said cyst could be due to flu vaccine or pneum vaccines she got a few years ago ( 1x and never ever again). Cyst fluid extracted- cytology neg-, sclerot fluid filled instead. Parallel with the kidney cyst her BP changed from whole life low 90/60 to 130/90, so she takes ACE inhib. Also we do not know when, her blood 0, RHneg changed to 0, RHpos - no blood transfusion, or similar
She has nodules on her thyroid, but all labs are OK and she does not feel anything neg hormonally. She had an uterus myoma and unfortunately ( her erroneous decision) had a full hysterectomy at the age 43, so can not trace back at what age I should expect entering meno..... Due to lack of hormones, she had a starting osteosp- but now is ok with paleo and Vit D3 and K2.
According to blood group, I've inherited my fathers one - A, RH pos and the 'outlook' as well - most probably with some armenian or mediteranian roots: almost black hair, olive/copper skin and green eyes - will turn out soon with my 23and me. And hopefully will not inherit the stroke - my father, all my fathers brothers and sisters and my fathers mom and dad as well are gone due to stroke - this sounds very bad and scary, I know.

Eating: can fast easily, so been eating only 1xday at 6pm, and had a morning coffee w lots of cream. Changed since 3-4 weeks according to Leptin RX, have a piece of protein at 6am - min 50gr, have something at 12:00 and at 6pm. Since 2009 am on paleo, so no grains, no legumes, no sugar. Have vegetables as CH source and some fruits, latest not every day.

### Pebbles — 2016-04-18T17:44:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pebbles-journal.12264/post-192975>

2016 March- April - tropical vacation - followed Jet Lag RX for travel- the EMF-edema gone in 1-2 days.
tanking high UV and IR.
Some days I was out on the open sea, with high adrenaline and with ZERO EMF - had no stuffed ears and no myoclonus, only the ear sizzling stays (its permanent and much weaker than a ringing). Its was such a refreshing feeling! I felt almost like in pre-floxxed times!
Hormonally felt OK as well!
The way back ( E to W), followed Jet Lag RX - While landing - pressure increase - the stuffing of the ear and myoclonus lessens ( lack of NO is compensated by other gases - NOS, H2S, CO, etc). 1 day passed and still some edema in my ankles.
My ears are stuffed, myoclonus again, ..... I wish I could return ASAP to the healing sun and open sea - with no EMF!
Still hope on some longer lasting positive effects......

### Pebbles — 2017-06-29T23:01:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pebbles-journal.12264/post-217519>

After 6 month in the tropics, 3 weeks home and starts again....
In January due to a scratched infected mosquito bite that did not want to heal in the tropics, I had to take Augmentin, which activated EBV - my childhood mumps-, ending with some Frey syndrome. - interrupted ABs after 3 days, apparently the Frey syndrome diminished with time.
Now back home (EU, 49 L) and due to inflamed lymph under chin and flu symptoms ( scratching and painful throat) lasting since a few weeks and fever fluctuating during the day between 36,5-37,5C - not progressive, but chaotic.
I made ultrasound, which showed activated chin lymph node and a 7mmx3mm "lymph node' intraparenchial in parotid.
The fever might be related or unrelated to above - never had any allergy and now turned out that I have dust mite allergy (after my return cleaned a room not touched for 6 month). Endotoxins blocked partially my bile as well, menses stopped. At 51,5yo could be meno, however I doubt stopes so drastically right after my return.
Now I was sent to cytology for the parotid nodule.
1- What labs other than C3 and C4 should I do for toxins? ( I do not have HLA snps, no HLA-DR snps)
2- Cytology for parotid - I have doubt doing it, since heard from you so many times the PSA test/testis situation - what is your opinion about punching parotid?
3- if no punching, blood work can be reliable to exclude cancer?

# Thread: Trying to Find Food Database With Sulfur Content
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-find-food-database-with-sulfur-content.12268/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### persistence — 2014-12-02T08:30:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-find-food-database-with-sulfur-content.12268/post-149002>

I have a list of high and low sulfur foods, but I want to quantify the amount of sulfur in each type of food and then sort each list by that quantity. Does anyone know of a food database that includes the amount of sulfur / sulphur found in each cup (or any unit of measure) for major known foods?

Would also be nice if the same database had a quantification of the amount of nitrates and oxalates in the same foods.

### PaulG — 2014-12-02T14:14:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-find-food-database-with-sulfur-content.12268/post-149010>

[http://apjcn.nhri.org.tw/server/info/books-phds/books/foodfacts/html/data/data5g.html](http://apjcn.nhri.org.tw/server/info/books-phds/books/foodfacts/html/data/data5g.html)

### Lahelada — 2014-12-02T14:21:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-find-food-database-with-sulfur-content.12268/post-149011>

Or this: (see tables)

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC1264524/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC1264524/)

### persistence — 2014-12-03T05:18:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-find-food-database-with-sulfur-content.12268/post-149079>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=149011)
>
> Or this: (see tables)
>
> [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC1264524/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC1264524/)

Thank you for that. As old as that source is, this is the first real lead I have.

That source is dated 1939. You really think it would hold up as accurate?

# Thread: Ketogenic overeating in winter
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketogenic-overeating-in-winter.12272/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2014-12-02T21:40:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketogenic-overeating-in-winter.12272/post-149031>

What happens when you overeat on a ketogenic diet in winter and you're resistance training?

I can watch my hunger disappearing but force myself to eat to build muscles. I try to gorge on fat and have a moderat protein approach with carbs mainly in the evening meal up to 50g max out of vegetables or chocolate (I will ditch some vegetables and chocolate soon because I do not feel to eat so much anymore in the cold times).

### Hansen Kenimer — 2014-12-04T00:39:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketogenic-overeating-in-winter.12272/post-149137>

Butters, I imagine if your redox is good, you'd burn off the excess calories at night when you're sleeping.

As an aside, I'm eating ketogenic and I lift heavy and never force myself to eat. I eat till I'm full. And then I don't eat until I'm hungry again. The better my redox gets and the more I CT, the less I want to eat, really. Starting CT for me really seemed to make building muscle ridiculously easy. Too easy. My legs have suddenly gotten too big and I need to back off.

You'll eventually get past what's been ingrained into our minds about "eating big to get big" and realize it's all about improving your redox and becoming metabolically efficient. The more efficient you are, the less actual food you'll need, and you'll get a whole lot more out of the food you DO consume. Water, however, I have found to become even more critical as you start to creep closer and closer to being a zero entropy system.

### Butters — 2014-12-04T11:55:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketogenic-overeating-in-winter.12272/post-149200>

Thanks for the answer. Thought no one would answer it.

I posted the question, because I read the CT blogpost 6,7 and there Jack mentioned that cold, short light cycles, low carbohydrates and low calories have all to be present to get the most out of CT. Then I thought about my consume behavior and my hunger was declining despite I am working out and make gains like crazy for me. I'm making such gains I haven't for a long time but I also was eating a lot of fat.

Its coming slowly but after rereading some posts I begin to understand.

In Winter protein and fats also are rare, so we are low calorie, dependend of the latitude its more or less.

But in his podcast with Ben Greenfield Jack mentioned also that some kids use CT and eat tons of bacon etc. So that is not fitting in the picture.

# Thread: Where I am at with CT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-i-am-at-with-ct.12273/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2014-12-02T21:48:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-i-am-at-with-ct.12273/post-149033>

How can I find out if I am cold adapted?

What happens when my BF is already low and I am CTing extensivly through the day with less clothing and sessions on the top? My BF through BF caliper is roundabout 12%.

I thought I am adapting very good but as temperature is dropping to -3°C I find myself shivering almost all the time outside when wearing only longsleeve or T-shirt and inside in the unheated room. I can not get the feeling of heating up all the time. Plus my feet and hands are cold almost all the time.

When I use the cool gut buster and fat burner I can wear it all the time on the pure skin and take it off with covered red skin and numb in places. I don't develop cold urticaria. Also most of the time I can gulp on ice water without getting brain freezes.

Also, should I eat a high fat/high protein meal before at my status or better not?

### nonchalant — 2014-12-03T02:45:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-i-am-at-with-ct.12273/post-149069>

I think you are partially adapted.
If you are having difficulty with CT, eating a high fat meal before could help. I don't usually bother now. I CT when ever it is convenient; sometimes just after a meal, sometimes not.
I think you need to be patient with the shivering. Your body will adapt when it can.

### Butters — 2014-12-05T14:28:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-i-am-at-with-ct.12273/post-149301>

Tonight I got very intense heat generation also during the day after a period of fasting I recognized more heat generation.
I thought shivering isn't bad at all because it releases Irisin and this browns WAT fast to BAT.
Maybe I drink to much ice water during the session. Sometime to 2l. Jacks writes about 1l ice water maximum during a session.

# Thread: Brain freeze explanation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-freeze-explanation.12274/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2014-12-02T22:39:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-freeze-explanation.12274/post-149041>

Why One gets brainfreezes with high omega 6 tissue content?

### nonchalant — 2014-12-02T23:52:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-freeze-explanation.12274/post-149047>

The O6 tissue (from artificial oils) is not able to respond correctly to cold temperatures. Skin that has O6 from nuts or other natural source, and O3 from seafood is quite resilient in cold temps. They are like antifreeze for your cell membranes.

### Butters — 2014-12-03T09:14:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-freeze-explanation.12274/post-149084>

Yeah, I know Jack mentioned that. But is there any scientific evidence? I have a friend who wants to take fish-oil. I told him to eat fish but he don't want to. As he wants to know how much he have to take, I said it varies from person to person. But he should tell me if he gets brain freeze when eating/drinking cold things. I would like to send him some study or article about that.

# Thread: Hello, lovely peeps!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-lovely-peeps.12276/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kristi Lambert — 2014-12-03T01:28:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-lovely-peeps.12276/post-149053>

[Hi everyone! I have been following Paleo, Wahls, Keto, LCHF for a while and recently came upon interviews with Dr. Kruse, which brought me here. I have been vlogging on Youtube as Lambiepoo for a few years, most recently, my journey with keto for the past 13 weeks. I have been pouring through the blogs and forums here and trying to soak in all the information. I lost about ten pounds before I started vloggin, and 32 pounds or so over the following 12 weeks. I incorporated alot of testing of different macro rations based on what the "gurus" were recommending and noted how my body reacted. I unofficially started the LeptinRx yesterday (kind of a dry run for two days before offically starting tomorrow - Wednesdays are my Youtube check-in days, generally).](http://www.youtube.com/user/lambiepoovegan/videos)

I am a T2D and found that my FBG stayed in the 80s when I kept my protein below 70, while it would shoot up to 100-120 if I went to about 100g. I found myself being scared off from the BAB by the internet folks who talk about gluconeogenesis, so I was heartened to read Dr. Kruse's comment about gluconeogenesis being the gateway drug to the PPP! I have not used any diabetes meds, just a cinnamon extract called Cinsulin (sp?) and hope that I will not have to do so. My FBG, as expected, has gone up to the 120s since I started the LeptinRX. I am hoping it will start dropping natually, and I also want to not that I have not been entering my food into Cronometer this pas week and have stayed LCHF and Keto but enjoyed a few treats such as pork shoulder, Applegate Farms pepperoni and Kerrygold cheese over that past week. Did not measure ketones or blood sugars over five days, so I may sill be recovering from my indulgences. I had been doing a few five day fat fasts recently so who knows how much I'll weigh now that I am rehydrating (hindsight says probably no more fat fasting).

I feel so much better already, sleeping better, aching muscles subsiding. I have my blue blocker glasses on, got my sleep mask. Been using a Colpak on my trapezius, but I got a snorkel and am gonna start my face dunking.

Dr Kruse and all you lovely peeps are so inspiring and thought-provoking! Thanks for being here!

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/kristis-journal.12278/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/kristis-journal.12278/)

Cheers,

Kristi

### Kristi Lambert — 2014-12-03T01:33:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-lovely-peeps.12276/post-149057>

Thanks so much, Dr Kruse!

### nonchalant — 2014-12-03T01:41:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-lovely-peeps.12276/post-149059>

Welcome Kristi! Glad you are going to start the face dunking. Will you be getting some AM sunshine?

### Kristi Lambert — 2014-12-03T01:46:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-lovely-peeps.12276/post-149062>

Thanks! Yes indeed, found a spot this morning across the street and got a couple minutes on my retinas during the "golden hour", at about 7:15 AM. Sunrise was listed to be 7:06 AM. Any recommendations on the best way to sun gaze? I would be most grateful

### nonchalant — 2014-12-03T02:31:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-lovely-peeps.12276/post-149065>

> [Kristi Lambert said:](/goto/post?id=149062)
>
> Thanks! Yes indeed, found a spot this morning across the street and got a couple minutes on my retinas during the "golden hour", at about 7:15 AM. Sunrise was listed to be 7:06 AM. Any recommendations on the best way to sun gaze? I would be most grateful

Sounds great. I found at first that I couldn't look at the sun, even when it was very low on the horizon. So I would just look in the general direction.
Now my eyes are different, and I can look directly at it (if right after sunrise), or later if it is behind a tree and I only see small bits of the sun. Even if obscured by clouds, I go look in the general direction. And glance at the sun often during the day.

Jack has emphasized that resetting your circadian rhythm is the most important part of the Leptin Reset.

I have been drinking spring water and RO water, but I love ice cubes and have used filtered water cubes from the fridge so I am gonna start sticking RO water in the freezer and fridge going forward.

I am currently in an emf swamp in this house. I use Dropcams to keep track of my mother and wireless routers so I have a bunch of work to do there to come up with ethernet solutions. This house was built in 1980 so I have some challenges.

I have a grounding mat, but had it grounded by the house electric socket (stopped using it - I could feel it was making me sick) so I need to run a wire directly to the earth.

I bought one of those emf ponchos and a face shield - things are tight money-wise so I will keep adding. I have an emf pendant called a rejuvenizer that I hope is helping me, I got it a couple years ago and never take it off. I am currently caretaking for my mother so at least I am no longer in a work emf swamp.

We have Hunter Spring Park in Crystal River which I hope is getting nice and cold. Thought I might start wading into it, will bring a thermometer and see if it will work.

I would be most grateful for any suggestions

Thanks again for the welcome! I am so excited to have been guided here - feels like I am finally home

### Kristi Lambert — 2014-12-04T01:57:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristis-journal.12278/post-149153>

[Well, here we are at Day 1 of the LeptinRx. I made my video for the LCHF Youtubers and here it is: http://youtu.be/bqCpklQRfOI. Here are my stats for the week:](http://youtu.be/bqCpklQRfOI)

FBG 126
Evening BG 113
Morning Ketones 1.2
Weight 237.8
Released this week -8.4
Total released 23.6
Avg release 1.82
Hip/waist/bust 55/49/52.5

I was really happy to see my evening BG. I will get in a Colpak on my upper back tonight and start face dunking tomorrow. Gonna look into compression garments asap. I bought one of those gizmos for the tub listed in Dr K's store to keep the tub from draining. I'd really like to get a stock tub and put it out front (will see if I can have one for Christmas).

My food for the day was:

Breakfast (7:15 AM EST):
3 5 oz cans of pink salmon with a splash of MCT and some mayo
decaf coffee with 2 tb coconut milk (just the cream part from the top)

Lunch (11 AM EST):
2 heads romaine with mct/mustard/ACV dressing
2 TB Bubbie's sauerkraut

Dinner (4:15 PM EST):
2 c mushrooms in coconut oil

### Kristi Lambert — 2014-12-10T22:59:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristis-journal.12278/post-149751>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=149748)
>
> Good morning Kristi .....just on my way to Sydney for some Christmas shopping ...I will be back!
>
> please re read the leptin reset again ...don't over think things! sometimes we miss things the first or second time thru - I know I do ....or I didn't quite understand the importance of something.
>
> It is all about the three legged stool ......
>
> my initial thought is you are getting a lot of protein ......
>
> Are you hungry for lunch still? How much good fat are you having?

Thanks Caroline! Ooh sounds fun, I love to see all the decorations in the city this time of year. I like to watch the young girls take ice skating lessons at the mall to get me into the Christmas spirit.

I went back through the leptin RX, brain gut 6, and the leptin faq and from what I am gathering from three combined at this point is:

-   **Does breakfast need to be just protein, or can there be vegetables or fruit? What about fat?** If you are overweight, avoid fruit. _Veggies are fine as long as they are not nightshades and have a low glycemic index_. If you are underweight, you can eat fruit, and I suggest blueberrries, blackberries, and grapes over all others. Fat is great. The obese can use [coconut oil](http://store.nutiva.com/aff/7563933154C0D4013687DE48F5051500/index.html) as their “number one diesel.” If you don’t like coconut oil or Nutiva coconut manna, you can use heavy cream, but you won’t lose weight as quickly.
-   Try to eat as soon as possible upon rising in the morning, ideally within 30 minutes of waking. _Make sure that breakfast has little to no carbs (less than 50 grams), and has a lot of protein and fat. I use as a general rule 50-75 grams of protein with most patients. Some patients can use less and some need more. The key point of knowing how much is right for you is your hunger later in the day. If you remain ravenous throughout the day, you need to eat more protein in the morning. If you can hold off eating until dinner you probably are at homeostasis for you. If you can skip both meals you likely are overdoing it at breakfast. As for sources, I suggest pastured or organic eggs first, served with left over dinner scraps of grass fed meats, poultry, or fish. A third option, although less ideal, would be whey protein or protein shakes._

-   _Any time I eat carbs_, I use liberal amounts of butter, heavy cream, [coconut or palm oil](http://store.nutiva.com/aff/7563933154C0D4013687DE48F5051500/index.html). I do not recommend other oils initially such as olive oils or industrial seed oils. I would also avoid nut oils at the initial stages. My personal favorite is [coconut oil](http://store.nutiva.com/aff/7563933154C0D4013687DE48F5051500/index.html) because of the great metabolic effects of MCT, and how it helps heal the guts of LR folks.

From these points it sounds like I can optimize my reset by moving what I eat for lunch to breakfast and adjust the protein by first making it 50-75g using eggs (mmm a spinach/mushroom/bacon frittata sounds great to me) as the first priority for breakfast so that lunch will not be needed. I am eating at least 6 TB of coconut and mct oil and 2-4 TB coconut manna daily, but need to move those to breakfast and dinner from what my feeble brain is gathering. I am thinking that will continue to hit the bone broth with arame and kelp in it, probably with dinner.

Cheers

### Kristi Lambert — 2014-12-11T02:47:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristis-journal.12278/post-149779>

Good evening, lovely peeps!

Here is my weekly stats update:

Weeks on keto: 14
Weeks on Leptin Rx: 1
FBG: 98
Evening BG: 88 (major woot woot!)
Morning Ketones: 1.9
Weight: 235.6
Pounds released this week: 2.2
Total released on keto: 25.8
Avg release 1.84
Hip/waist/bust: 55/48.5/52.5 down 0/.5/0 inches respectively

Had to pee at 3 AM. Went back to sleep until around 6 AM. Ate at sunrise and got some good sungazing in. Extended my CT by driving to the Walmart and back with the windows down in 40 degree weather. Did a morning oxy session.

FBG: 98 (Yayyyyyy!)
Evening BG (8:35 PM EST): 88

My food for the day was:

Breakfast (7:15 AM EST):
3 5 oz cans of pink salmon with a splash of coconut oil

Lunch (11 AM EST):
1.5 heads romaine with mct/mustard/ACV dressing
1 can smoked oysters (quite yummy!)
2 TB Bubbie's sauerkraut
decaf coffee with 2 tb coconut oil

Dinner (4:15 PM EST):
2 c mushrooms in coconut oil
1 5 oz can pink salmon
blonde starbucks coffee with 2 tb coconut manna

Did my face dunking after lunch. Was able to do three 3 minute dunks in 51 degree water. Breathing a lot more relaxed now.

Did sungazing after dinner while standing barefoot with my hand on our camphor tree.

Used my Colpak for 45 minutes.

Here is the link to my Youtube video:

Gonna move more food into breakfast tomorrow and see if I can make it to dinner without hunger

Sweet dreams y'all!

### Kristi Lambert — 2015-01-03T18:06:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristis-journal.12278/post-151471>

Howdy lovely peeps!

Here is my update for Friday 1/3/2015:

Still waking a little too early, but sticking to having breakfast at sunrise so that I only eat during daylight. Went to Ft Island Beach (froze my face with AC on the way there for about 30 minutes) and got into that 59.5 degree water for 15 minutes up to my belly button. Wanted to go deeper but things were getting mucky on the bottom and I feared I would lose my balance. I used the IR thermometer to check my skin temperature when I got out and it was 61 degrees. I should have stayed in longer because it actually felt great, but I couldn't wait to check my skin temp, lol! The air was cold and windy and the sun was barely peaking through so I got some good grounding and sungazing in while I sat on the beach for a while. Very peaceful, practically had the place to myself.

FBG: 90

Breakfast:
3 5 oz cans salmon
decaf coffee with coconut oil
1 can smoked oysters

Dinner:
1 5 oz can salmon
1 head romaine with mct/acv/mustard dressing
decaf coffee with coconut oil
2 c mushrooms with 2 tb butter
two forkfuls of Bubbie's sauerkraut plus two swigs of the brine

I am happy to see my FBG come back down. The only visible change is that I stopped the vanilla extract. Will keep working on the sleep. Will post today's entry later tonight. I think I will start setting up my portable tub. Hoping I'll be ready to do it soon, need to do the ice bags this week.

Cheers

### Kristi Lambert — 2015-01-05T12:58:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristis-journal.12278/post-151676>

Maybe this will help, I found it on a forum:

**[brassandzin](https://discussions.apple.com/people/brassandzin)**Jan 29, 2013 11:28 AM
[Re: Can't play YouTube videos on Safariin response to darkhorse85](https://discussions.apple.com/message/18674880#18674880)
I had the same issues!! Here's the fix! Found on the Gooogle/YouTube discussion boards.
The issue is caused by YouTube's HTML5 test not working in Safari.
To fix this go the the [HTML5 test page](http://www.youtube.com/html5), scroll down to the bottom, and click the "Leave test" link.
If this doesn't do it or you're getting this issue in another browser try clearing your cookies and restarting your browser. That usually solves it.

Just a shot in the dark...

### Kristi Lambert — 2015-01-07T01:47:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristis-journal.12278/post-151908>

Had my first session with bagged ice today. Got 2 10 lb bags and started with lying on the floor, but my back gave out so I moved to a sort of reclined porch chair and held the bag in a tall kitchen bag against me for the rest of the time. It was 56 degrees outside which helped. I lasted an hour - makes me so happy! I checked my skin temp with the IR thermometer often. I got some areas to 49 degrees and some to 52 degrees. I have set my new portable tub where I think it will work for me in anticipation of using it perhaps tomorrow. I am in the meantime searching for a "chiller" that I can afford (unless I get really lucky on an aquarium chiiler - it's a very small portable tub). Been looking at peltier, chill plate, immersion wort chillers, counterflow wort chillers, ice probe chillers, etc. trying to see if I might be able to cool my outdoor tap water enough throughout the year. Aaah, but lots of time to figure that out - will search for the cheapest ice bags around here ("Wally World" charges $2 per 10 pound bag). Got my epsom salts, MSM powder and H2O2 ready to go.

### Kristi Lambert — 2015-01-09T05:02:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristis-journal.12278/post-152191>

Howdy y'all! Well, it was 32 degrees with a bitter wind so I did my CT in the bathtub today. Got a 20 lb bag of ice from Publix for about $3.70 and reclined in the tub with my swim cap, dive socks, and compression gear. Started off with the ice on the compression shirt, then decided it was time to do the next step, so I exposed all my belly and iced my belly directly. I believe the whole session was 30 minutes. Got skin temp down to 49 degrees. Interesting, got a couple purple faint bruises. I read in another thread that Dr K mentions perhaps too much omega 3, which actually thrills me since I have been pounding the fish. I have a pork roast warming for tomorrow so I can rebalance O3/O6 a bit, as well as get more cysteine. Looks like that direct icing test was a success because I saw no cold urticaria, so I am thinking I shall move onto the next step with water plus ice with socks, cap and gloves.

On another note, I think I figured out why the weight loss has been lower these past two weeks, or at least a contributing factor - silly me, I added the oysters and forgot to check how many grams of protein I was effectively adding to my BAB, so I have been eating a whopping total of 82 grams total for BAB. So I will drop the salmon down to 2 cans (40 grams) with the 22 grams from the oysters and see if that improves things.

Oh and thanks to Yew's makerel food porn post  I checked out Publix and found makerel in water for $1.99 for a 15 oz can, so I will be shifting to that (the salmon is $1.49 for a 5 oz can).

Just ordered the faraday disposable tent from lessemf.com to try to lessen the smartmeter effect at night.

I hope this has been a blessed day for all of you lovelies

Well, I bought one on amazon and it turned out to be too loose, but I found a cheap spandex generic garment (ClimateRight is the brand - [http://www.walmart.com/search/searc...l0=e&wl1=g&wl2=c&wl3=45038760815&wl4=&veh=sem](http://www.walmart.com/search/search-ng.do?search_query=climate+right&adid=22222222220214754401&wmlspartner=wmtlabs&wl0=e&wl1=g&wl2=c&wl3=45038760815&wl4=&veh=sem) ) at walmart and I got it a couple sizes too small. It's kind of stretchy but seems to hold tight enough and is easy to get on. When I can afford it I plan on getting an Under Armour shirt or a Spanx. Thanks to Caroline's suggestion I pretty much wear it all day long now.

I will absolutely update you on the tent. I am hoping it will buy me some more sleep time

Cheers, Jude

### Kristi Lambert — 2015-01-12T18:56:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristis-journal.12278/post-152559>

Just a quick update on CT. Found myself a nice little spot for CT on Fridays where I can ground and I can kneel to get waist deep and sit to get all the way in. Friday the air temp was 42 degrees and the water as probably in the 60s (hubby's guess) - I forgot the thermometer.

Today I finally used my new portable tub with epsom salts. I did not get enough ice in so the temps was 68 degrees. Got my skin to about 69 degrees after 45 minutes. It was a last minute decision to try it. This was the first time I have put myself in the water up to my neck. Wore my compression shirt and shorts and dive socks. Having the treadmill to hang onto made getting in and out quite easy. This is exciting. I know 68 degrees is wimpy but I am happy to make any progress I can LOL! I may have to keep buying ice bags until I can clear enough room in the freezer for ice jugs and packs.

### Kristi Lambert — 2015-01-14T18:56:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristis-journal.12278/post-152747>

Howdy, lovely peeps! Here is my weekly update:

FBG: 93
Ketones: 2.2
Weight: 225.6
Release for week: 3
Total released 18 weeks keto: 35.8
Keto weekly avg: 1.88
Hips/waist/bust (deltas): 54.5/48/51 (.5/0/.5)
Total released 5 weeks Leptin Reset: 12.2
Leptin Reset weekly avg: 2.03

Now having 62 grams of protein for breakfast (instead of the 82 grams I was mistakenly having) and I am guessing this helped, as well as my progressing some more in my CT.

Here is my video update:

I have been doing my CT in my portable tub and today I got the water temp to around 57 degrees. I stayed in for 30 minutes today. Skin temp on legs was 61 degrees while skin temp under shirt was 64 degrees. Even with 20 lbs of ice I am not quite there. I am hoping to get my water colder with this:

[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000NI8POM/ref=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o00\_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000NI8POM/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o00_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

### Kristi Lambert — 2015-01-14T21:54:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristis-journal.12278/post-152758>

Hi Caroline  My thoughts were that I would immerse this into a cooler packed with ice and hook the garden hose to one side, then another hose from it to the portable tub. This one happened to have the garden hose connectors. I am hoping that since beer home brewers chill their boiling wort down to 70-80 degrees with it, maybe I can get from 70 down to 50 degrees with it  I thought about an aquarium chiller but could not afford it (maybe down the road)

### Kristi Lambert — 2015-01-16T00:58:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristis-journal.12278/post-152921>

Aaaah, much better. Air temp was 40 degrees and water was 56 so I borrowed Mom's living room lamp and used the heat lamp bulb so I could keep hands, nose and head warm as needed. My feet are still freakin' cold even with compression socks, dive socks, and althletic socks layered, but it was okay because I was able to warm my hands when I got too cold. I added my knit cap over my swim cap too. Mom's gloves did the trick for the hands. This cold weather right now is good since I only had to add one bucket of ice to get to 56F (waiting on the immersion chiller). I was happy that the lamp did not heat up the tub at all. I made it 40 minutes this way. Still wimpy, but some day I hope to grow up to be a badass CTer like you lovely peeps!
