I have been told that dissolving tumors is easy, it is preventing them from coming back that is the challenge....

The main component of Guo Lin Qigong is breathing a specific way while walking. It is called xi xi hu breathing (xi = inhale, hu = exhale).

Starting at about 12 seconds, people are in lines doing Guo Lin Qigong. Starting at 20 seconds you can hear the instructor saying xi ("she") xi ("she") hu ("who"). In English this is inhale, inhale, exhale. Two inhales as you swing your arms one way, exhale as you swing them to the other. There are variations on how the arms swing from side to side, but the breathing is always two inhales then one exhale. It's not really important how exactly you do the hand and arm movements (for example, how high your arms go, how far you swing your arms, your hand positions -- are they up or down, etc), but you do want them to be coordinated with the breathing. Breathing is coordinated with moving left or right. Breathing is the most important part of the practice.

Yes I think some of those terms can be problematic to discuss because people are often treating them from different layers of meaning. These layers are not necessarily conflicting, just possibly at different scales that could be nevertheless congruent - i.e. instinctive/intuitive/rational/logical etc. So as my signature suggests, choose a layer you're confortable with.

Take the word dimension - many could take that to mean alternative universes or we could take that to mean changes of scale from macroscopic to quantum. In this way it would lead us to the idea of things being fractal in nature. As above, so below.

Or the word ascension - that implies the aspects of changes in space, time, state, knowledge and has differences in quality whether you treat it as an act, a state or a destination. I could treat ascension as just a change in the perspective of the observer or the ability or act of observing from different perspectives (dimensions/scales).

Words can be such clumsy things .

I do like the images morphing from one to another and I wonder what a computer simulation of a dynamic ampltuhedron moight look like.

The main component of Guo Lin Qigong is breathing a specific way while walking. It is called xi xi hu breathing (xi = inhale, hu = exhale).

> [Josh said:](/goto/post?id=149624)
>
> Starting at about 12 seconds, people are in lines doing Guo Lin Qigong. Starting at 20 seconds you can hear the instructor saying xi ("she") xi ("she") hu ("who"). In English this is inhale, inhale, exhale. Two inhales as you swing your arms one way, exhale as you swing them to the other. There are variations on how the arms swing from side to side, but the breathing is always two inhales then one exhale. It's not really important how exactly you do the hand and arm movements (for example, how high your arms go, how far you swing your arms, your hand positions -- are they up or down, etc), but you do want them to be coordinated with the breathing. Breathing is coordinated with moving left or right. Breathing is the most important part of the practice.

Intersting . . . the assymetrical nature of the breathing suggests to me that they are changing energy balance somehow. The combination with movement gives me the impression of breath and movement being perpendicular to eachother creating a vortex effect - a flow with the left and the reverse flow with the right. I noticed them pausing at some points at the extreme ends of the movement (and breath?) and for some reason and reminded of that moment of weightlessness on a swing, an inflection point or undulation point. I'll have to try it and see what sensations I can experience if any.

In my internal marital arts experience a feeling of lightness was a regular quality - if gravity is an emergent property of electromagnetism then maybe that was the link. Sometimes in training we would use meditation without movement and other times combine the two. Since Dr. Kruse has introduced vibration and oscillation and the vibration only mode of sleep (no movement) I now wonder about how moving meditation differs from non moving meditation in these aspects.

### yewwei.tan — 2014-12-11T01:16:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-11-comments-welcome-here.12294/post-149771>

Going to derail this thread a little with a summary of this article I just saw: [https://medium.com/the-physics-arxi...ecord-smashed-by-sulphur-hydride-c853795079bb](https://medium.com/the-physics-arxiv-blog/high-temperature-superconductivity-record-smashed-by-sulphur-hydride-c853795079bb)

**High Temperature Superconductivity Record Smashed By Sulphur Hydride**

Today, Mikhail Eremets and a couple of pals from the Max Planck Institute for Chemistry in Mainz, Germany, have measured sulphur hydride superconducting at a temperature of 190 Kelvin (-83 degrees Celsius).

**There is a caveat, of course. The material has to be squeezed at pressures greater than 150 gigapascals** — that’s about half the pressure at the centre of the Earth.

Sulphur hydride is not one of these new ceramic high-temperature superconductors. On the contrary, it appears to be a conventional superconductor described by a theory that has been well understood for over 50 years.

Back in 1968, the British physicist Neil Ashcroft predicted that hydrogen could superconduct at high temperatures and pressures. His idea was that because hydrogen is so light, it should form a lattice that is able to vibrate at very high frequencies and therefore superconduct at high temperatures.

Since then, other physicists have shown that hydrogen ought to be up to do this even at room temperature (albeit at pressures greater than those at the centre of the Earth).
​

\-----

Firstly, this is Hydrogen Sulfide superconducting at a higher than expected temperature. We've already heard Jack mention H2S as an important quantum gas in the body, but I'm not going to dwell on that topic in this post because I don't know enough.

Instead, the fact that "high pressure" is needed for higher temperature superconductivity to occur caught my eye. What does "high pressure" mean?

To me, it just means that particles are very close together. If so, then we need to ask what makes particles move closer together. Cold does it, but there must be purely magnetically-induced means of getting that to happen as well.

I stumbled across the Casimir effect while reading Tensegrity #10 -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...elcome-comments-here.12245/page-3#post-148816](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/tensegrity-10-is-live-welcome-comments-here.12245/page-3#post-148816)

The region in between the sheets looks like it could be a "high pressure zone" .....

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Casimir\_effect](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Casimir_effect)

### Da-mo — 2014-12-11T03:12:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-11-comments-welcome-here.12294/post-149783>

and then this . . .

> The cysteine level inside mitochondria is approximately three times that in the cytosol. **Translocation of CSE to mitochondria metabolized cysteine, produced H2S inside mitochondria, and increased ATP production.** Inhibition of CSE activity reversed A23187-stimulated mitochondrial ATP production. H2S improved mitochondrial ATP production in SMCs with hypoxia, which alone decreased ATP production. **These results suggest that translocation of CSE to mitochondria on specific stress stimulations is a unique mechanism to promote H2S production inside mitochondria, which subsequently sustains mitochondrial ATP production under hypoxic conditions.** [http://www.pnas.org/content/109/8/2943.full.pdf html](http://www.pnas.org/content/109/8/2943.full.pdf+html)

So the ingredients might be there for superconductivity.

The above also sheds light on the use of H2S for inducing low oxygen requirements as discussed in this thread [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...about-infrared-and-ct.9675/page-6#post-146991](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/a-little-story-about-infrared-and-ct.9675/page-6#post-146991)

### PaulG — 2014-12-11T03:55:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-11-comments-welcome-here.12294/post-149794>

Good sleuthing Da-Mo and Yew

[http://www.sciencealert.com/physicists-achieve-superconductivity-at-room-temperature](http://www.sciencealert.com/physicists-achieve-superconductivity-at-room-temperature)

> These Cooper pairs of electrons are able to ‘tunnel’ between the alternating layers "like ghosts can pass through walls, figuratively speaking - a typical quantum effect,”[they report](http://phys.org/news/2014-12-superconductivity-cooling.html), but it was thought this could only occur at super-cooled temperatures.
>
> But then the physicists from Max Planck decided to **see what would happen if they irradiated the YBCO ceramic material with infrared laser pulses**. They found that for a fraction of a second, the ceramic becomes superconducting at room temperature. And when we say “a fraction of a second”, we mean a fraction. “It was only a few millionths of a millisecond,” [says Adam Clark Estes at Gizmodo](http://www.gizmodo.in/science/Scientists-Created-a-Room-Temperature-Superconductor-With-Lasers/articleshow/45379649.cms). "That's a very, very brief lifespan for our amazing new room temperature superconductor. However, the successful experiment is proof that such a thing is possible."
>
> The team suspects this is because the pulses from the laser cause individual atoms in the crystal lattice structure of the ceramic to shift momentarily, which increases the superconductivity of the material.

I think we need to get this book or [download pdf version](http://xxx.tau.ac.il/pdf/cond-mat/0606187v1.pdf) though as it was written in 2003 may be outdated by newer research.

Seriously, when I've finally got free time (hopefully by 2nd quarter next year), I'm going to:

-   Get a stenotype and get to 180-200wpm within a year
-   Learn gregg shorthand for the times when I don't have a computer
-   Code up a wiki site for collaborative note-taking (probably just 2-3 weeks of work for me)

Then my notes will be better than word-for-word

### yewwei.tan — 2014-12-12T13:24:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-11-comments-welcome-here.12294/post-149886>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=149800)
>
> So if your into quantum health you know that food becomes electrons. The mitochondrial matrix is loaded with H+. Where do they come from? And H2O has two positives......and one negative charge. So when you charge separate water it can become a small magnetic monopole in the matrix can't it? So how might we control this rogueness?

I thought back to what I wrote about NAD+ and NADH, and my hydrogen entanglement hypothesis -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...omments-welcome-here.12294/page-3#post-149552](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/tensegrity-11-comments-welcome-here.12294/page-3#post-149552) , as well as Jack's facebook post about CD38 (a transmembrane protein) -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/tensegrity-11-comments-welcome-here.12294/#post-149297](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/tensegrity-11-comments-welcome-here.12294/#post-149297).

NAD+ and NADH still feel like good ways to signal using entangled H+ ions to me, so it would be helpful if I could get some confirmation on my theory about NAD+ forming an entangled H+ ion during it's reduction to NADH.

I've been looking at a bunch of papers, and the common theme seems to be that NAD+ allows for mitochondrial outer membrane permeability, while NADH inhibits permeability. Now, I don't know what decreased permeability means for membrane flexibility, and the resulting oscillations that can be produced.

Final random question: Do we need NAD+ for cell signalling during REM sleep? (When only vibrations are being produced)

....

I have a disc bulge (c6/7) (dx'ed by neurosurgeon) and first had weakness, numbness/tingling starting Oct 2012, but it has improved significantly by itself. I saw a neurosurgeon in March 2013 and he said to take it easy, never flex my neck, use cervical traction, etc. i'm doing better, but my arm is still weak (it's about 80% back) and some dullness at tip of index finger...feel like it's getting better, but slowly. He also said I have DDD and a straight neck.

any suggestions on what to do to cure myself of disc bulge/DDD/straight neck and prevent this from happening again? thank you so much!

# Thread: CRP and birth control
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crp-and-birth-control.12308/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dombant — 2014-12-05T14:21:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crp-and-birth-control.12308/post-149300>

So after a year and two blood tests showing very high CRP (11 in the one a few months after my c-section and 7 a few months ago) I read recently that birth control and IUD's can cause elevated CRP (pregnancy does too.) I recently found out that my IUD was protruding (I was expelling it so they had to put a new one in OUCH!!) so now I'm not quite trusting what my inflammation read out was. Just wanted to point this out to any of the ladies here using these methods. It is making me question whether using the copper iud is a good choice, though. I wonder if chronic minor inflammation of the uterine walls is a good idea now (though, weighing that against the cortisol elevation from an unwanted pregnancy, hahahaha.) I was just trying to take a non-hormone route. I take a little extra zinc picolinate and eat plenty of red meat and offal to keep my balance in check. Anyway, as always, context is everything..

[\[url=https://plus.google.com/+JeangreyCofan/posts\]dr jean grey](https://helpouts.google.com/113838601398151265676/ls/8ac079cb1db4077d)\[/URL\]

### Shijin13 — 2014-12-05T15:20:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crp-and-birth-control.12308/post-149304>

There's an entire thread some where here on the forum about charting and using that to prevent pregnancy. I used it, as have many others....

# Thread: Where does COPPER come from?

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8

### lilreddgirl — 2014-12-06T19:59:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-does-copper-come-from.12313/post-149360>

So Im an outdoorsy naked equatorial human.... I see that summer time - Fructose - Cooper goes down. Fall time - Molybdeum up, Copper goes down (does fall Molybdeum in plants go up as much at the equator/warm climate?)

So.... where do I replenish my copper? do spring time plants have copperr?

I know copper is used for regeneration...

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-06T20:38:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-does-copper-come-from.12313/post-149362>

copper materializes from the giant solution in your cells via electrolysis..........when the current varies you get a problem. Your cells are organized to do that house keeping normally below your perception and you only become aware of it when you allow a personal thunderstorm to interrupt that process in you. Becker and I have talked about copper effects of nnEMF forces.......all tied to Ca2+ and electrical changes in cell membranes. Bone allows electrostatic bonding of 2 copper ions......lysyl oxidase does the same for collagen. And In the brain.........copper comes out of solution in the basal ganglia and liver in Wilson's disease...........showing the brain gut connection electrically.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-06T20:39:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-does-copper-come-from.12313/post-149363>

Tensegrity 11 talks about Faraday's 1st law of electrolysis........directly scales to this question.

### lilreddgirl — 2014-12-07T21:11:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-does-copper-come-from.12313/post-149441>

whhhat ? super like!

what about... ZINC?

### Inger — 2014-12-07T21:22:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-does-copper-come-from.12313/post-149443>

i just know that oysters are loaded with zink and copper.... loaded!!!  At days when I have many oysters and I put it into nutrition data I get unbelievable high copper and zink values...

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-08T17:21:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-does-copper-come-from.12313/post-149521>

Zinc and copper dance as opposites.......but the charge is what controls their levels.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-does-copper-come-from.12313/post-149651>

ive never looked into tests for copper and zinc although a lot of local functional docs do it routinely. might start if i am understanding this correctly

Zinc and Copper together create a battery...

# Thread: Hibernation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hibernation.12316/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JMO — 2014-12-07T00:12:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hibernation.12316/post-149375>

Just got home from visiting my sister from the hospital. She is 57 and is probably one of the oldest living with PWS (Prader-Willi Syndrome). We always thgt she had Downs Syndrome but found out later it was PWS. This disease has to be a story abt energy. They seek food 24x7 and literally eat themselves to death. Obseity at a very young age. She prob has the intelligence of a 8 year old but she could never learn time. There are probably a lot of things that could be learned from her syndrome. Lately she has been unable to arouse because her body temp goes down to 94 -hypothermia and she goes into hibernation. She went out for 24 hours and when her body temp eventually goes up she wakes up and says HI like nothing happened. Not sure what sets these episodes up but I think it's dehydration. It's really hard to make her drink water. I checked her BUN/creatinine and it's 26. She has the docs totally stumped. I look at her disease with a new light now and see she is almost out of energy. A sad disease and very interesting.

### sjoshua — 2014-12-08T19:26:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hibernation.12316/post-149531>

Wow, it seems her body is overriding 'conscious' choices in order to achieve the restoration it desires/requires... some internally driven semiconductor chilling.

Curious. does she only 'hibernate' when she's an inpatient or does it also happen when at home?

I'd agree you are quite on point with it being an energy story, though that is true of every disease, I'd say this is an more extreme example.

> [JMO said:](/goto/post?id=149375)
>
> She prob has the intelligence of a 8 year old but she could never learn time.

I can't blame her here, I've been trying my whole life and don't fully understand time...

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-08T19:38:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hibernation.12316/post-149532>

When the body is out of energy is changes to pseudohypoxia and it begins to entangle the 3 gases that allow hiberation to occur. CO, H2S, and NO. this uncouples mitochondria. All these are reactions because of a lack of superoxide pulse at cytochrome 1 due to low NAD+. This is talked about in Sinclair paper from cell in 2013 that I referenced in EE12-13 and has been showing back up in Tensegrity 5-11. My biohack in Tensegrity 5 was all about hypoxia........pseudohypoxia is a condition that develops when you cant pulse superoxide to make new mitochondria. Next step is cancer generation.................Her mitochondria are the cause of the phase redox shift. You are capable of teaching her docs a lot now.......just put it all together. The answers are all there if you know where to look.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-08T19:39:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hibernation.12316/post-149533>

[http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2004/06/0623\_040623\_lemurs.html](http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2004/06/0623_040623_lemurs.html)

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-08T19:45:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hibernation.12316/post-149534>

Read radical theories 6-8 too. [http://jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-8/](http://jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-8/)

### nonchalant — 2014-12-08T19:57:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hibernation.12316/post-149535>

Did she wake up in the morning? It would be nice if she could have access to sunshine.
Does she have access to RO water?

### JMO — 2014-12-08T21:14:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hibernation.12316/post-149538>

> [sjoshua said:](/goto/post?id=149531)
>
> Wow, it seems her body is overriding 'conscious' choices in order to achieve the restoration it desires/requires... some internally driven semiconductor chilling.
>
> Curious. does she only 'hibernate' when she's an inpatient or does it also happen when at home?
>
>
>

>
>
>
> I can't blame her here, I've been trying my whole life and don't fully understand time...

She is at her group home when she just goes unconscious and is impossible to wake. She has done this while eating so know she has no control as food is the most important priority for a person with PWS. The last few times they have called an ambulance and we are noticing when her temp goes down she goes to 'sleep'. This was the longest she's been unconscious for abt 24 hours. They put a heart pacer in last time it happened but didn't help the hypothermia problem although it does seem like it helped her be more energetic. [http://ghr.nlm.nih.gov/condition/prader-willi-syndrome](http://ghr.nlm.nih.gov/condition/prader-willi-syndrome)

Yes...it does seem like its an extreme example of a energy deficient person. They are born floppy and it makes me think they are missing their energy network...so is that why they have a insatiable appetite...blonde hair, fair skinned. There were 3 that lived close in our neighborhood almost the same age-so I always wonder abt environmental issues.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hibernation.12316/post-149540>

Yes, I would wonder about that too ^^.

If I know someone that can sleep for extended periods of time (14-16 hours) then there may some kind of underlying energy issue going on at the mitochondrial level? This person is so hard to wake up. Need to do some more reading...

Couldn't this be said for people in general now, substituting 'energy' for 'food'? Blue light and other nnEMF may be toxic, but it is energy.

### NeilBB — 2014-12-14T19:49:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hibernation.12316/post-150092>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=149532)

> When the body is out of energy is changes to pseudohypoxia and it begins to entangle the 3 gases that allow hiberation to occur. CO, H2S, and NO. this uncouples mitochondria. This is talked about in Sinclair paper from cell in 2013 that I referenced in EE12-13 and has been showing back up in Tensegrity 5-11. My biohack in Tensegrity 5 was all about hypoxia........pseudohypoxia is a condition that develops when you cant pulse superoxide to make new mitochondria. Next step is cancer generation.................Her mitochondria are the cause of the phase redox shift. You are capable of teaching her docs a lot now.......just put it all together. The answers are all there if you know where to look.

I was curbsided this AM about a patient that supposedly has [Shapiro Syndrome](http://rarediseases.info.nih.gov/gard/4815/disease/resources/1). I don't think he actually has the full genetic version which only affects 20-50 people worldwide, making it incredibly rare. But they think that this particular guy developed a posttraumatic version of Shapiro's after head trauma years ago. Apparently he undergoes cyclic episodes of severe hypothermia in which he becomes unresponsive for days. They did EEG's on him during these spells, which were essentially normal awake EEG's, leading some to think that he was "faking a seizure." But he was clearly not faking core temperatures of 94 degrees, which freaked out his doctors. Essentially he goes into hibernation and scares the crap out of everybody, and nobody has a clue of what to do with him when this is going on. But after a few days, this resolves and he returns to normal function and normal core temperature until the next attack. This has apparently been occurring for years, although his family now thinks it is becoming worse. (More frequent, more prolonged episodes, I think.) They believe that all of this was induced by a traumatic brain injury...
[http://www.orpha.net/consor/cgi-bin/OC\_Exp.php?lng=EN&Expert=29822](http://www.orpha.net/consor/cgi-bin/OC_Exp.php?lng=EN&Expert=29822)

She went out for 24 hours and when her body temp eventually goes up she wakes up and says HI like nothing happened. Not sure what sets these episodes up but I think it's dehydration. It's really hard to make her drink water. I checked her BUN/creatinine and it's 26. She has the docs totally stumped. I look at her disease with a new light now and see she is almost out of energy. A sad disease and very interesting.

I also have a couple of seizure patients with Prader-Willi (and also Angelman) Syndrome. But I have not seen the hibernation issue specifically in them.

### JMO — 2016-03-09T03:52:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hibernation.12316/post-190493>

Here's a pic of Shar (Prader-Willi Syndrome). She had cataract surgery this summer and has just completely gone downhill. She's having so many mental issues - waking up at night calling family making up stuff like the staff is in her room or that the police are going to take her away. Bad behavior and throwing fits, biting herself and the 21 meds a day she is on...is not helping...imagine that. She's in a group home and they have taken her to the hospital like 6 times since Jan. Got her some glasses to wear at night but she has her circadian rhythm so messed up. Although it was not the best place, there are a couple crisis centers that specialize with Prader-Willi in other areas of US. The docs here and the group home facility don't understand the syndrome. She has gained back prob 125 lbs in a few years. She is smart enough to know she gets more food at the hospital hence the repeat of bad behavior plus mental issues now. Everything is about food. We had a family meeting over the weekend and I see things so much differently than everyone. She is like the canary in the coal mine.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-03-09T05:49:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hibernation.12316/post-190495>

This is what happens when the mitochondria do not connect to the nucleus and results in heteroplasmy. Any tissues with mitochondria will be most effected......the reason for food is that she needs the electrons to keep her voltage high but if the electrons are not excited by sunlight, and they are not in her case, then she has the drive and craving for more and more electrons only because none are excited to give her the UV she needs............

### Melanie Procter — 2014-12-07T04:21:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dht.12317/post-149395>

Why does Jack recommend a DHT test? I understand there is a connect between hair loss and this bio marker, but I can't figure out what else it is a proxy for.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-07T04:41:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dht.12317/post-149396>

conversion to estrogen........more protons leads to DHT

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-12-10T01:07:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dht.12317/post-149632>

do you mean when one is not converting testosterone to estrogen, DHT is the biomarker? i have a girl with hugely increased testosterone post partum...pg normal at menses (when she took the test, so no indication of ovulation time levels)
first thing has been get her off the carbs etc

### persistence — 2014-12-12T04:16:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dht.12317/post-149867>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=149396)
>
> conversion to estrogen........more protons leads to DHT

I thought testosterone converts to both DHT and estrogens. When you stop conversion to DHT via a drug like Propecia, more of that testosterone may convert to estrogen.

I FINALLY found a way to stop taking Propecia and hope that the mess it creates will slowly unwind. What I discovered was that topical urea (used to treat acne and other skin conditions by forcing more rapid skin turnover) can be used on the scalp. And in my case it seems to directly attack whatever gets stagnated by too much DHT in follicles. The topical urea appears to completely remove the itchiness and hair loss I previously experienced whenever I stopped Propecia. I'm using topical urea 35% in a foam formulation.

# Thread: Are the waves getting too large????
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-the-waves-getting-too-large.12318/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 27

### caroline — 2014-12-07T04:46:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-the-waves-getting-too-large.12318/post-149397>

Dr. K. has said to me that if the waves are getting too large I must ask questions....

I do know that a bunch of us are getting swamped by all the blogs ...and even some threads.

It is amazing to see so many taking all of this on board and processing all the science and asking great questions! I love to see folks digging in ...and all the light bulb moments.

No matter how much I want to ...I will never be at this level......let's be real here!

soooooo - in the spirit of learning - and loving every second of this........and wanting to do so much better -
Do you have questions that would help your understanding ....

Dr. K. has always said there are no stupid questions ......if we want to know - and can't get a handle on it - we need to ask ....or risk being perpetually in the dark. I hate being in the dark!

Dr. kruse is shining his flashlight for all of us ...... so - what do you want to understand better????

We are all here for the same reasons - our journey to our own optimal life .... increase our knowledge, listen to the whisperings of mother nature and dance at the edge of our life.

what simple thing is driving you crazy ...and you want to understand better?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-07T05:08:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-the-waves-getting-too-large.12318/post-149400>

Today in New Orleans I was interviewed for a movie.........none of the questions they asked me moved the dime...........I think their project will help mankind. When they speak to Kate I have a sense my words and her story will resonate on many levels..........but here is the rub:
I'm but an apprentice, in a craft where no one ever becomes a master. The difference between me an others.......is that I realize my limitations.
the question is how all do you view your role?

### caroline — 2014-12-07T05:45:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-the-waves-getting-too-large.12318/post-149402>

What a great question ....... how do we view our role.......

It seems I could talk forever about quantum entanglement and quantum magic and living/enjoying life in the moment and I feel it in every fibre of my being ...and it certainly doesn't go un noticed. I get such amazing and positive. feedback from friends and strangers alike - every single day. I feel extraordinarily lucky for so many reasons.

But - can I talk about QED ... nope - not really. For sure - I do get the overall message and I can be an example of how all of this that you are illuminating for us impacts my life. I keep being told how healthy I look and how happy I am and I can't wipe the smile off my face!

The last three + years I have learned countless life lessons from you and others here...... an incredible win/win for me - inspite of my lack of science understanding and abilities to put up graphs like Yew and Josh!!!

Do I want to understand all this better .... of course - I do love all the brain pain

### Lahelada — 2014-12-07T11:38:31+0200

IThanks for making waves Caroline.

I get fed up,give up then read three blogs in one sitting. Like Caroline I do not get the science,actually better to say I decide not to get it , not to invest sufficient time to get it. I am the one though to say to my friend that her insistence on " diet only fixes all" is like the girl who hides a run in her stockings by moving her skirt up or down but that in the end a run is a run,it will only get bigger and visible I am the girl with the three mjnute message about sunlight and DHA interspersed with enough science to get even recalcitrant docs to look
here ,under protest of course. Or jn other words I scale your message to my audience,your vox populi translator if you will.
Happy Sunday up there in NOLA and everywhere else.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-07T17:46:08+0200

My waves are simple........3 legged stool is where the final common pathway is Caroline. Keep that in mind when I teach you about all these crazy transition states that occur in H+ in the matrix.......Out of the clutter I give you, find simplicity in the 3 legged stool. Bad times have a scientific value for teaching lessons on tough science and ideas and theories.

The thing about the world I am trying to show you is it isn't that these things were harder than you thought they were going to be, it was that they were hard in ways that you never expected. What makes this tough is that I am changing the perspective of how we should look at health...........what sets the program is not what we all thought. Food is a small part of that story and that dogma is going to be tough for most to swallow.

### NeilBB — 2014-12-07T17:57:35+0200

No need to wonder, Damo. You just answered your own question. He's attacking dogmas-he's a heretic. He's dangerous to the establishment.

Furthermore, in my opinion, a novel scientific theory, no matter how brilliant, can never really be fully accepted or applied in a world dominated by faith, dogma, and superstition. Look around at the world and see "how the wind is blowing" today.

The validation and respect for knowledge trumps everything. Without it, we will all be blown away--maybe that's why I harp on it like a damn broken record.

"A plague that resists all science"

### Josh — 2014-12-07T19:07:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-the-waves-getting-too-large.12318/post-149428>

> The Three Prisoners:
> After the death of Rabbi Uri of Istalisk, who was called 'The Seraph', one of the Hassidim came to Rabbi Birnham and wanted to become his disciple. Rabbi Birnham asked, "What was your teacher's way of instructing you to serve?"
> "His way," said the Hassid, "was to plant humanity in our hearts. That was why everyone who came to him, whether he was a nobleman or a scholar, had first to fill two large buckets at the well in the marketplace, or to do some other hard and menial labor in the street."
> Rabbi Birnham said, "I shall tell you a story....
> "Three men, two of them wise and one foolish, were once put in a dungeon black as night, and every day food and eating utensils were lowered down to them. The darkness and the misery of the imprisonment had deprived the fool of his last bit of sense, so that he no longer knew how to use the utensils; he could not see. One of his companions showed him, but the next day he had forgotten again. And so his wise companion had to teach him continually. But the third prisoner sat in silence and did not bother about the fool.
> "Once the second prisoner asked him why he never offered his help. 'Look,' said the other, 'you take infinite trouble and yet you never reach the goal because every day destroys your work. But I, sitting here, am not just sitting. I am trying to bore a hole in the wall so that the light and sun can enter, and all three of us can see everything.' "

Interesting, but I'm skeptical. Of course, the government fiat money of today is a total joke, absolutely. In the past, gold was objective money and could not be easily manipulated by governments. Before Alan Greenspan sold out to politics, he explained why...

[http://www.constitution.org/mon/greenspan\_gold.htm](http://www.constitution.org/mon/greenspan_gold.htm)

But any kind of technological monetarist solution (like Hormann suggests here) is still potentially subject to tampering, especially in times of social upheaval. It's not objective and tangible, like gold. I love Roddenberry's Star Trek and the idea of evolving beyond the economics of today, but I don't know if this really gets it done?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-08T04:32:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-the-waves-getting-too-large.12318/post-149481>

We tend to learn best when we're interested in something and we're interested in topics when they relate to us directly. When you're trying to explain a complicated topic to someone, it's best to play on that egocentric behavior and show what's in it for them.....The idea of connecting ideas to what someone already knows (analogies) has been a common teaching technique since [Socrates](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Socrates), but it works because it's one of the best ways to explain ideas. This why I used the refrigerator to describe entropy and the personal thunderstorm to describe your nnEMF environment. I leave tons of data out in podcasts but I can't in blogs. Skeptics and docs need the complexity of the blog to realize this is not pseudoscience. It is science they just are not aware of, but it is published. When you understand a concept well as I do, it's easy to think of every detail as important, but when you're trying to explain that complicated concept to someone else, you should leave certain details out. I use different forms to do just that. Teaching someone is hard enough but just sitting back while people fumble through it is frustrating. Using bio hacking and quantified self ideas make complex issues easily understandable. If you want folks to actually understand the concepts and learn, you have to [let them do it themselves](http://lifehacker.com/the-science-behind-how-we-learn-new-skills-908488422)....this is why I came up with many of the Rx.
When you learn a new complex subject, do this: Get passionate about it. Don't try to do it quickly......go at your pace but ask questions of others. It will shorten your learning curve.

-   Find the fundamentals straight away. (basics that you must always remember whiel learning)

-   Find the principles straight away. (Rules that do not change regardless of how something contradicts it.)
-   Have confidence that if any other human can learn it, you can too.
-   Have a reason to learn it so that you can use the knowledge AS you learn it.
-   Find the best step by step guide first by asking people or searching and flicking through all guides available to you.

# Thread: OZ Gathering @ The Barossa Vintage Festival
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oz-gathering-the-barossa-vintage-festival.12319/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ssj3 — 2014-12-07T11:58:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oz-gathering-the-barossa-vintage-festival.12319/post-149412>

As it seems like all the gatherings are in a land far far away, I think it would be awesome if us cooler people in the Southern Hemisphere had an opportunity to get gather and cause mayhem.

The week long Barossa Vintage Festival is being held in April 2015 and on Saturday 18th April, the famous Vintage Festival Parade is held.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

The idea is that a bunch of us would stake out a spot along the parade route; and over some interesting conversation share a few dozen oysters and some great Barossa vino.

Send me a PM or reply below if you are interested and we can discuss details further and organise logistics.

Hope many of you can make it!

### ssj3 — 2014-12-10T10:14:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oz-gathering-the-barossa-vintage-festival.12319/post-149676>

Here some extra details for those interested.

The vintage festival runs from 15th-19th April. The main parade is on Saturday 18th April.

There are quite a few options to think about depending on where everyone wants to stay and how long you want to enjoy Adelaide ...
1\. Stay in Adelaide and travel up for day to Barossa on the 18th April.
2\. Stay in Barossa from 16th-19th April or longer.
3\. Stay in Adelaide before and/or after the 16th-19th April and stay in Barossa during the 16th-19th April.

There are a few options in the Barossa that will be able to accommodate a large group of us for a reasonable cost of ~$60pp/night. This is with a 3 night booking with check-in on Thursday 16th and check-out on Sunday 19th April. These a linked below for you to review:

[https://www.airbnb.com.au/rooms/3491789](https://www.airbnb.com.au/rooms/3491789)
[http://www.seppeltsviewcabins.com.au/index.html](http://www.seppeltsviewcabins.com.au/index.html)
[http://www.barossamotorlodge.com.au/](http://www.barossamotorlodge.com.au/)
[http://vineinn.com.au/](http://vineinn.com.au/)

In Adelaide there is accommodation both before, during and after the 16th-19th April. Below are some links to available accommodation.

[http://www.stayz.com.au/accommodation/sa/adelaide/henley-beach/83688](http://www.stayz.com.au/accommodation/sa/adelaide/henley-beach/83688)
[http://www.stayz.com.au/accommodation/sa/adelaide/henley-beach/104704](http://www.stayz.com.au/accommodation/sa/adelaide/henley-beach/104704)
[http://www.stayz.com.au/accommodation/sa/adelaide/henley-beach/132112](http://www.stayz.com.au/accommodation/sa/adelaide/henley-beach/132112)
[http://www.stayz.com.au/accommodation/sa/adelaide/henley-beach/64801](http://www.stayz.com.au/accommodation/sa/adelaide/henley-beach/64801)
[https://www.airbnb.com.au/rooms/247024](https://www.airbnb.com.au/rooms/247024)
[https://www.airbnb.com.au/rooms/267804](https://www.airbnb.com.au/rooms/267804)

# Thread: Alcohol

### Butters — 2014-12-07T13:50:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alcohol.12320/post-149417>

Is Alcohol harmful in Winter and a no go?

Jack talks about red wine often and I wonder if there are some spirits that I should avoid?

### Inger — 2014-12-07T22:13:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alcohol.12320/post-149449>

iirc alcohol is not so great in winter time... Jack has mentioned it somewhere. I try to avoid it then and rather drink wine in summer if I want some. But sure no rules without exceptions
I think most spirits are not good for health but red wine like malbec that is high in resveratrol is good for health when you drink one or two glasses no more - then it turns to poison. Yeah. And no wine after 5 PM as it disturbs your sleep.
If you live in high EMF you should drink no alcohol at all... as it dehydrates you too... iirc

### Haley — 2015-01-10T01:29:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alcohol.12320/post-152271>

I'm wondering the same thing as Jack has mentioned several times of drinking wine lately and considering we are in the middle of winter I am confused. Maybe this is because of where he lives?

### Da-mo — 2015-01-10T02:04:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alcohol.12320/post-152280>

I think its a case - like most things - of how good your redox potential is. Contra-indicated in some instances like when engaging in CT for example.

If I have a wine its usually in the evening and my biggest concern with it is the carbs it contains and hoow that might affect the hormone cascade during sleep.

### Inger — 2015-01-11T09:24:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alcohol.12320/post-152387>

Alcohol in the evening destroys my sleep too... mostly

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-18T07:14:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alcohol.12320/post-168688>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=149449)
>
> iirc alcohol is not so great in winter time... Jack has mentioned it somewhere. I try to avoid it then and rather drink wine in summer if I want some. But sure no rules without exceptions
> I think most spirits are not good for health but red wine like malbec that is high in resveratrol is good for health when you drink one or two glasses no more - then it turns to poison. Yeah. And no wine after 5 PM as it disturbs your sleep.
> If you live in high EMF you should drink no alcohol at all... as it dehydrates you too... iirc

I think that occasionally you can cut loose and drink some alcohol as long as you know what it does to you, like dehydration. But only if you are healthy. If you are sick well that's a different story.
We have to weigh the downsides of alcohol with the benefits of a strong social happy atmosphere and closeness to friends and family. But it should only be occasionally, not every weekend like I used to do.
I probably drank every single weekend for 20 years!

### Danco3636 — 2015-06-19T20:16:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alcohol.12320/post-168963>

I think any alcohol (man made) in any context has consequences.... Other than what you may get from naturally occurring fermented whole foods. I believe it is one of those compromises for quality of life and lifestyle we choose to live........ Kinda like wearing blue-blockers at night because you want the lights, TV or something else on. The best would be no blue light at all so the glasses are a compromise for chosen modern day lifestyle. I find I do much better when not drinking alcohol at all buuuuut it is fun to have a drink now and again. Just extra CT, water and sunshine before and after....

Just my thoughts and experiences....

Still on a learning curve so not certain I can explain the transformation. Can anyone else explain?

### Valerie — 2014-12-08T04:52:20+0200

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-08T14:57:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally-in-ppp.12322/post-149510>

We tend to learn best when we're interested in something and we're interested in topics when they relate to us directly. When you're trying to explain a complicated topic to someone, it's best to play on that egocentric behavior and show what's in it for them.....The idea of connecting ideas to what someone already knows (analogies) has been a common teaching technique since Socrates, but it works because it's one of the best ways to explain ideas. This why I used the refrigerator analogy to describe "entropy" and the "personal thunderstorm" to describe your nnEMF environment. I leave tons of data out in podcasts but I can't in blogs. Skeptics, scientists, doctors need the complexity of the blog to realize this is not pseudoscience. It is science they just are not aware of, but it is all published. I have to weave it into a coherent biologic narrative. When you understand a concept well as I do, it's easy to think of every detail as important, but when you're trying to explain that complicated concept to someone else, you should leave certain details out. I do that in podcasts but not in blogs. I use different forms to do just that. Teaching someone is hard enough but just sitting back while people fumble through it is frustrating. Using bio hacking and quantified self ideas make complex issues easily understandable. If you want folks to actually understand the concepts and learn, you have to let them do it themselves....this is why I came up with many of the Rx's that are science free. They are directions to see the results of quantum actions in biology.
When you learn a new complex subject, do this: Get passionate about it. Don't try to do it quickly......go at your pace but ask questions of others. It will shorten your learning curve.
Find the fundamentals straight away. (basics that you must always remember whiel learning)
Find the principles straight away. (Rules that do not change regardless of how something contradicts it.)
Have confidence that if any other human can learn it, you can too.
Have a reason to learn it so that you can use the knowledge AS you learn it.
Find the best step by step guide first by asking people or searching and flicking through all guides available to you.

### Valerie — 2014-12-08T17:06:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally-in-ppp.12322/post-149518>

Thanks Dr K. All my free time is spent reading your papers, re-learning how the body functions!

### angie-s — 2015-01-09T15:54:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally-in-ppp.12322/post-152215>

Me to, all I do in my free time is read Dr.Ks blogs and Q&As. I have also put together a Bio-hack 101 for me where I write down the fundamentals, basic concepts etc. and read them over and over.

I am still struggling with the EMF and blue light part. I do not understand why the blue light is a problem with older adults if the aging eye blocks it automatically.

I am also following the protocol completely yet cat get into REM sleep. May be some issue from neighbors. What meter do I need to get to measure the EMF.

### Inger — 2015-01-09T16:09:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally-in-ppp.12322/post-152217>

> [angie-s said:](/goto/post?id=152215)
>
> I am still struggling with the EMF and blue light part. I do not understand why the blue light is a problem with older adults if the aging eye blocks it automatically.

I will explain to you how i understand it, Angie.....

It is a really bad thing if your eyes block blue light....  because you need it in the daytime, when sun is out! The NATURAL blue light. It is as important as the darkness, i would say!
We want lots and lots of blue light in our eyes daytime, strong blue light from the sky, it is way way stronger than any lamp, the natural daylight.
In the night we need the darkness, especially, NO blue light.

Fake blue light is never good.  (it still is better IMHO to use fake blue light than no lights at all or red light, in the daytime if your had to be 24 hours somewhere where no natural light can come in. But of course used only when sun is up. Our body needs to be able to tell time and that when it gets blue light in the eyes at day and darkness at night, when it naturally occurs)

If I were you I would be really strict with blueblockers and dimmed lights after dark, and in the daytime i would be outside sans glasses and look up into the sky frequently......... you need it more than anyone because your eyes are not able to absorb it effectively anymore.....

You especially want the morning and evening sun before the sun sets and after it rises... it has lots of infrared in it and it is the really healing kind of light. So if your eyes are damaged you really need to be out at those hours........

### angie-s — 2015-01-09T16:19:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally-in-ppp.12322/post-152219>

Thanks Inger. This helps a lot in understanding.

### Inger — 2015-01-09T16:28:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally-in-ppp.12322/post-152221>

So glad if I can be to any help, Angie

# Thread: Foot structure
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/foot-structure.12323/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2014-12-08T03:08:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/foot-structure.12323/post-149468>

I see some changes in my feet lately. Well, one change has been going on for a while -- the second toe on both feet has moved more toward the big toe, which has also moved away from the smaller toes. So the gap between my big toe and the next has increased, and there is also a gap after that next toe. The big toe movement started with grounding and changes to the next toe started with CT. So it's been going on for a couple years.

The other change I've noticed in the past couple months. When I walk barefoot on a hard floor, it feels like I'm poised on three points. Heel, base of big toe, and base of little toe. This is a completely new feeling for me, and it still feels...surprising. I don't remember it as a kid, though I've always had a high arch between my heel and toes. The base of my little toe was never used much, though I went barefoot plenty until my 30's.

A few years ago I suddenly started developing a callous under the second toe, next to the big one. But I was recovering from plantar faciitis, and going barefoot for the first time in years. But now that callous is almost gone, and now I have new callouses at the base of the little toes. Good for me, because walking on 3 points can get painful. The callouses under the big toes have decreased, though they were always huge since I was quite young.

I don't notice any difference in my footprint, but I don't have anything to compare it to.
I know, I know Dr. K, I shouldn't have callouses at all. I'm still dehydrated.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Cold repelling effect
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-repelling-effect.12327/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2014-12-08T12:09:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-repelling-effect.12327/post-149497>

Hey there.
I'm diving deeper and deeper in this CT thing. For me, I have to understand the mechanism of things to do it better.

So why a magnetic or electric field repels the cold?

Moving currents create a magnetic field in a right angle to the flow of direction. For repelling only the surface charges on the mitochondria membrane are effective I think. Also we are not cooling the core, so it has to happen near the skin.
But how does this repel the cold?
Also wouldn't it be more a heat holding mechanism than a repelling mechanism, because thermodynamically heat diffuses to cold and not the other direction. So the magnetic field must hold back the diffusing mechanism.
So I have to ask: How does a magnetic field is able to break the diffusion? It has to make a neutral zone without charges is my thought, but I would like to know how this can be physically explained.

### HoneyChild — 2014-12-08T18:10:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/documentary-einsteins-nightmare.12332/post-149525>

Documentary on tomorrow night BBC 4 at 9pm.

[http://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/b04tr9x9](http://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/b04tr9x9)

> Professor Jim Al-Khalili traces the story of arguably the most important, accurate and yet perplexing scientific theory ever - quantum physics.
>
> The story starts at the beginning of the 20th century with scientists trying to better understand how light bulbs work. This simple question led them deep into the hidden workings of matter, into the sub-atomic building blocks of the world around us. Here they discovered phenomena unlike any encountered before - a realm where things can be in many places at once, where chance and probability call the shots and where reality appears to only truly exist when we observe it.
>
> Albert Einstein hated the idea that nature, at its most fundamental level, is governed by chance. Jim reveals how, in the 1930s, Einstein thought he'd found a fatal flaw in quantum physics because it implies that sub-atomic particles can communicate faster than light in defiance of the theory of relativity.
>
> For thirty years his ideas were ignored. Then in the 1960s a brilliant scientist from Northern Ireland called John Bell showed there was a way to test if Einstein was right and quantum mechanics was actually mistaken. In a laboratory in Oxford, Jim repeats this critical experiment - does reality really exist or do we conjure it into existence by the act of observation.
>
> The results are shocking!

### HoneyChild — 2014-12-17T17:56:20+0200

I ended up having a dream last night where the police were accusing me of trying to obtain deuterons. I kept telling them I didn't even know what they were or what I was supposed to do with them!

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-17T19:26:11+0200

# Thread: Zero Point -Documentary series
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zero-point-documentary-series.12333/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sjoshua — 2014-12-08T19:02:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zero-point-documentary-series.12333/post-149527>

I really enjoyed this 4-part documentary series by Zero Point, available for free viewing on youtube:

Volume I - Messages from Our Past:

\- Discusses some incredible mathematical alignments between various 'earlier' cultures, as well as related to the design and sizes of the pyramids and similar style monuments found around the world.

Volume II - The Structure of Infinity:

\- Discusses the common fractal patterns that can be found everywhere in nature, even within the constructs of human designs, such as the stock markets. Everything scales by the same basic ideas.

Volume III - The Legacy of the Ark:

\- Discusses the Ark of the Covenant from a scientific analysis - i.e. it was a giant capacitor; also discusses monatomic gold/ORMUS and its funky 'superconductive' abilities/tendencies.

Volume IV - Beyond:

\- Discusses our observations of a global/collective consciousness - i.e. hours before the 9/11/01 attacks at the World Trade Center, the 'randomness' around NYC became a lot less... random, and the same can be said about natural disasters and other worldwide events.

Watching these helped a lot of dots connect around my understands of all things. Enjoy

### sjoshua — 2014-12-08T21:39:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zero-point-documentary-series.12333/post-149539>

> [spinergie said:](/goto/post?id=149537)
>
> Hey sjoshua thank you for suggesting watching these very exciting! Is there a link I am able to use to view the full volume 1 pls, as it's not made available in the UK on utube.
> Volume 2 seems ok so far... I was able to view a short introduction for volume 1 and it looked so good I was sorely disappointed to see it was 2! Minutes long...

Geez, that's a real bummer! Here is a link to their channel, all of the videos are posted by the same company so I'm unsure why the rights would be different: [https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWlK1ZorBxmy8K5M6zDALL0EGiYS51Z1d](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWlK1ZorBxmy8K5M6zDALL0EGiYS51Z1d)

Another option, is trying to go through a site like this: [www.**viewyoutube**.net](http://www.viewyoutube.net/)/ Which is viewing through a proxy server so it doesn't 'think' your IP is from your actual location. Hope these help!

Trying to learn this Quantum Emergency Medicine thing .... Had to start with the basics. I'm afraid I'm nowhere close to finished trying to learn. I must have slept through the Quantum Biology Module in Med School and Residency...

### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-14T22:43:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-152763>

OK, I got back another email from Catherine. I think the community should know that:

-   There are options for **Single**, **Double**, **Triple**, and **Quad** rooms
-   There are 4 room types: Interior, Ocean View, Balcony, Suite

We can organise independently regarding how we want to share rooms.

### cinnamon — 2015-01-14T23:13:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-152764>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=152745)
>
> Ok... I am sure Yew will rather share room with you Patrick because.... yeah.. i am a woman...
>
> Any girls out there who need someone to share room with?  Rerun.... cant you come...........
>
> I am not 100% sure yet i will get the money together fast enough before all rooms are gone... but i do all i can...
>
> Will there be oysters on the cruise? I love that the food is included    (if it is epi paleo for sure...but i would imagine there will be lots of seafood?)

I would like to share! I had pretty much given up on the idea of going when I found that the cost for a single was at least $1,500 US. The cost for 2 people is about the same for both together!

Food is included, but you need to add something for drinks (even if only bottled water) plus gratuities are extra. You need airfare to NOLA. And it is recommended to arrive the day before the cruise (so you don't miss the boat...), so probably at least one night hotel. It would be helpful to share this cost too...

Oh, and Inger, I asked Cathy about the oysters too! LOL
Here is what she said:

**Me:** Oh, and is seafood (especially oysters) included in the cost of the cruise, or would that be an extra? I hope Carnival knows that this group is likely to devour vast quantities! LOL
**Cathy:** I cannot guarantee what will be served on the sailing but you have the main restaurant, a buffet and there are specialty restaurants which do charge an entrance fee. Usually there will be a seafood item on a menu but I do not know if oysters will be offered.​

Remember that [post a few months back](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/consultation-with-jack.11305/#post-134190) (<--- click! there is a link here) that talked about a mutiny on board when all these people with orange glasses have eaten every oyster and there is no ice left! LOL Maybe that was a prophecy?

But seriously, I would love to go! I want to meet everybody!

### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-15T03:56:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-152794>

OK, more info.

Quad rooms are available on request, but payment has to be confirmed by <22-Jan-2015>

For each non-related (not family) person sharing a room, you need to pay a US$250 deposit via Credit Card and complete a form to confirm the room. The forms are available on request from Catherine, who can be reached at [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted])

If you're travelling as a family, then there only needs to be a single form and single US$250 deposit per room.

Getting a double room is the recommended option to make it easier to organise the trip. Find a partner and get going

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-29T15:29:37+0200

I'm just pipe dreaming, I know. But still would like to ask a couple of questions just in case dreams become reality. (And right off the bat, I do realize this is members only.)

\- are there rooms left? is it a matter of "only a few so act now"?
\- regarding the ports that will be visited (I do not have full cruise information) - will one need a passport? (as a US citizen) This is perhaps the most time-consuming chore to cross off one's list if they do not have one and want to attend the cruise.

Really, those are the two biggest questions I have. My 22nd wedding anniversary is in May and we never did go on a honeymoon... a girl can dream anyway...

(Oh let me throw in question number 3 which actually IS important - is there a seasickness biohack?  )

I dont know about room...I hope there is still some. Some big unexpected bills have kept us from pulling the trigger yet....over 7K for this trip....eek!

You are not required to have a passport but the travel agent recommends it. Being a passenger on the ship it is not needed, however if anything ever "went wrong" you are technically out of the country. Its a better safe than sorry thing...

It is my understanding that seasickness is generally not an issue on these large ships....if that is not the case FOR THE LOVE OF GOD SOMEONE LET ME KNOW! I spent last summer fishing the chesapeake with the family....well they fished...I vomited. Dont need a repeat this summer.

### nonchalant — 2015-01-29T17:20:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-154204>

I asked about the passport too. You can even go ashore without one, to all of the ports. You just need a birth certificate (certified copy). When I traveled to Mexico previously I also needed a marriage license, because of the name change.

There are rooms left.

### cinnamon — 2015-02-11T05:08:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-155333>

I had this question too, nonchalant. I found conflicting advice about the tap water onboard cruise ships when others asked about it on various cruise message boards, so I hunted down [this](http://www.cdc.gov/nceh/vsp/operationsmanual/opsmanual2011.pdf). It's the CDC "vessel sanitation" operations manual and it has a whole section on "potable water". See the details in section 5 at the link.

My understanding is that they either get the drinking water from safe on-shore sources (bunkering) or they make potable water from sea water through either a reverse osmosis or distillation process. The water is "halogenated" so that it contains residual halogens of 2.0 ppm. The definition of halogen in the document is: " The group of elements including chlorine, bromine, and iodine used for the DISINFECTION of water." Note that fluorine is not mentioned, but it is a halogen, so in theory could be used. Also, chlorine and bromine both interfere with the body's iodine receptors, so they aren't ideal either.

They limit how much water you can bring on board in [the Liquor and Beverage Policy](http://www.carnival.com/CMS/FAQs/Liquor_and_Beverage_Consumption_Policy.aspx). The limit is 12 non-alcoholic drinks per person. (Oh and one bottle of wine ) Apparently this has to be in your carry on luggage, so the suitcase stockpile idea is a no go.

They sell water through "The Fun Shops". (Check links [here](http://www.carnival.com/FunShops/beverages/water) and [here](http://www.carnival.com/FunShops/beverages/drink-packages)). You pre-order it and pay for it before you cruise. They deliver it to your stateroom so it is waiting for you when you get on board.

You can buy water at the bars and restaurants on board. It's pretty pricey. Here is [a link showing the current prices](http://www.shipcruise.org/carnival-cruise-alcohol-drink-prices/).

So, there's your choices!  I'm leaning toward carrying on a small quantity and pre-ordering a bunch through "The Fun Shops". Summer solstice on the Gulf of Mexico - I imagine I'm gonna need a lot!

### Josh — 2015-02-11T05:35:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-155335>

If one is going to use a lot of water, there is always options such as these...2.75 gal/hr.

[http://www.berkeywaterfilters.com/trbepusygawi.html](http://www.berkeywaterfilters.com/trbepusygawi.html)

I have not ried one. I figure I will end up spending 100 on water.

### TerrierMom — 2015-04-16T18:41:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-161448>

So other than poring through this thread, do we have a list of cruise-goers? Also with respect to wine - you can bring in one bottle per person in your carryon. No hard liquor. They want to control alcohol consumption so people don't get too unruly (and of course also so you have to buy theirs). There is a whole FAQ on alcoholic beverages. On another note - Disney lets you bring in a case of wine if you want (and we have....). Though for this cruise, a flat of water plus a case of Pellegrino will be just peachy for me. Can't wait to see everyone....

### TerrierMom — 2015-04-20T22:06:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-161833>

I don't think there is any way we will not enjoy each other. The 2 people from my area I met already through this forum are great, one couple we have already traveled extensively with. I think when there is some common thread people bond quickly. And one thing we know about people here is that we are open to new ideas and self-experimentation and seeing where the data leads us. And that we all find great value in Jack! Great starting point and we will all have plenty to talk about. This is a great friendly and supportive group of people of all shapes sizes nationalities and health concerns and we have so much to learn about each other, that is why I felt like I absolutely 100% needed to be on this cruise. Now we just need to find an Optimal Retreat where we can all go on another trip to be off grid and eat seafood and CT and heal for a couple weeks at a time.....

### Jack Kruse — 2015-05-03T00:15:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-163114>

Invites to the pre cruise party being crafted today. Here is primer: COME AS YOU WILL BE… IN 2025!
Join us for a celebration that will stretch your imagination and catapult you into your own future.
When:\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
Where: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
Given by:\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
RSVP to: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
Arrive as who you will be 10 years from now. Dress in your very best language or dialect. Speak only in the present tense the entire evening, as if it were already 2025, all your goals have been achieved, and all your dreams have already come true. You might even be photographed or videotaped as you arrive!!!!!! Bring props to show everyone what you have achieved in the years between, such as best-selling books you’ve written, magazine covers you’ve been on, awards you’ve won, and photographs or scrapbooks of your achievements. Throughout the evening, you will have the opportunity to applaud others in their achievements and to receive congratulations.

Be ready for something very different. The 2015 Kruse cruise Bon Voyage will be special.

### Grmoline — 2015-05-10T18:19:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-163778>

I can't believe that I am finally going to be meeting some of you! Jack, I've been following your blogs and webinars for almost 4 years now, and others of you have had impacts on my quality of life and health in ways that you will never know. If this is the only opportunity for this kind of gathering, then I am grateful to be heading for the right place at the right time. I've changed my life so much in the last 4 years that I can barely keep up with that, let alone what I think it will look like 10 years from now. But I can tell you this - 10 years from now looks light years different than 10 years from then did.

As I've [mentioned before in this thread](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/page-5#post-153804), I'll probably be sleeping with my CPAP which produces a faint hiss. ### [email redacted] — 2015-06-02T16:58:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-166266>

I want to 'announce' BFD right?..............that I will be going to the Kruse Cruise. Anyway wanted to let people know and especially our little 'group' Cinnamon, Inger and Yew and Josh I think (sleeping on the floor!) . Thanks for all the encouragement I tend to get 'cold feet' whenever I am doing something 'new' and have a tendency to back out - so it all helped.

Yew I just watched the interview with Stuart Hameroff about consciousness, I really liked it makes an awful lot of sense. I look forward to discussing it more with you. I have one for you and let me be clear I am not 'promoting' this but it is something I have been involved with for many many years. I have mixed feelings about it I believe there is really something true and sound at it's core but also I think the 'implementation' of it has been mis-handled. But it's one of those things that I think has great 'potential' and to make it 'actual' well that's the challenge at least for me.

I hope you can find the time to watch it or of course anyone else too. But I thought it would be nice if you even had a small background in what it is about so then I am not talking out of a void. That way our discussions if we have them would have more context and be easier. I will now watch the lecture Hameroff gave so I am as up to speed as I can be. Here is the Janov interview about 'primal therapy'

### [email redacted] — 2015-06-03T04:57:10+0300

### [email redacted] — 2015-06-03T05:03:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-166370>

Since both Hameroff especially takes about anesthesia but also Dr Kruse at least implicitly I tried to describe below my last expierience of general anesthesia about 5 years ago

_So it was not like sleeping or dreaming or the state between wake and sleep. I was 'aware' but it felt in a very debilitated way like being aware I had lost my mind if that is the right way to put it. But one thing I noticed and this seemed to go on for an awful long time is my brain/consciousness felt like those old fashioned gadgets where someone who show slides of say their vacation. But running fast too fast to see any one. Very disturbing feeling like my mind was a machine spitting out discreet images, no 'flow' to it, just pop pop pop another image. It felt like I was on the one hand trying to slow down the 'show' so I could focus on any one particular slide or maybe speed it up so it would become like 'life' where an actual flow would be happening._

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-03T06:21:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-166376>

Pat.......now think about what people say about the end of your life.......it seems to speed up and you see your life flash........WHy? because ubiquitin rates in MT are speeding at crazy rates........in anesthesia it is reversed. All ubiquitin all the time because of the action of light on the atoms of water in your brain.

### [email redacted] — 2015-06-03T16:59:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-166435>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=166376)
>
> Pat.......now think about what people say about the end of your life.......it seems to speed up and you see your life flash........WHy? because ubiquitin rates in MT are speeding at crazy rates........in anesthesia it is reversed. All ubiquitin all the time because of the action of light on the atoms of water in your brain.

Very interesting. Dr Janov states that anesthesia is not good for the brain and potentially dangerous. Here is his quote...................do you think there is something to this? I can imagine it is not good but would you agree with this? I really don't know I have had 3 fairly major ones (operations requiring anesthesia) but did not 'notice' any long term effects. Hameroff is an anesthetist does it for a living and he never mentioned any 'dangers'. Does not mean there are not any of course. Often the people closest to something can be pretty blind ironically.

Quote:
_"Now, the second aspect of this compounding (in terms of brain problems: my comment) is any long-term anesthesia (over 3 hours) where the brain is shut down for protracted periods. I have seen enough of this to see memory problems lasting months and years. If there is any way to avoid this please do. There is a spinal or other options that avoid cortical brain cell suppression. Perhaps there is no alternative in special cases, but this is a warning sign. We are shutting off the function of a key organ, the brain, and we need to do that with caution."_

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-03T21:12:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-166455>

I dont think anesthesia is optimal.........but it is necessary. This is why elective plastic surgery is crazy talk most of the time

It felt like I was on the one hand trying to slow down the 'show' so I could focus on any one particular slide or maybe speed it up so it would become like 'life' where an actual flow would be happening._

this is what some describe as a glimpse of your true nature, or of true consciousness. a glimpse that what you really are is that which is truly perceiving everything, even the thoughts of the thinking mind that most identify with as "I"
in this case the thoughts and memories were perceived as clunky slides, yet you, as consciousness, remained the constant.
many find it disturbing when they get this glimpse that they are notwhat they thought they were (the thinking mind)

### Grmoline — 2015-06-09T18:12:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-167240>

> [yewwei.tan said:](/goto/post?id=167140)
>
> I need to check if we can sign-up for the excursions on-the-fly. I'd like to stick with like-minded people as far as possible . (I'm at the Cruise mainly for the conversations)
>
> We can probably decide on a couple of excursions to do before hand. We've got 3 ports we're dis-embarking on, and anyone on the cruise can login to their dashboard and view excursions -- [https://secure.carnival.com/shore-excursions](https://secure.carnival.com/shore-excursions)
>
> ....

Yeah, I worried about excursions being filled up early so went ahead and booked, but hadn't expected so many more to be available now than when I signed up! Don't think I can change them at this point, or I'd make some swaps. Being a geologist/geophysicist, I wouldn't mind zipping and tubing through some good cave formations, but I think I'm hooped at this point. I've done a lot of Caribbean cruises in the past. I, too, am on this one for the company and the calibre of conversation.

### Josh — 2015-06-09T20:59:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-167250>

I have not had a week without the phone on in 9 years. I see it as a floating monastery. I will meditate in my cell when not eating or engaged in "debate".....LOL....I like ships anyway,always wanted to captain a fishing boat, not sure why I did not follow that one....last trip like this was transatlantic on the QE2 at age 8 with the same nutcase I am sharing a cabin with this trip....

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-09T21:00:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-167252>

First talk of the cruise: Red light slows time. Cold slows time. Blue light speeds up time and your life is lived like a blue straggler star. Quick and bright. Over supplemented comes from being undereducated about light.
