### Lahelada — 2015-06-11T16:18:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-167610>

On shore excursions could be the natural continuation of conversations. Expeditions need not be a distraction from the subject at hand but a deepening of understanding . Swimming in a cenote, a cavity ....

I am mentioning it as I lived "close " to cenotes but did not know what I know now. Don't laugh at the turn of phrase in the light of a recent webinar but I would like to turn back the clock to when it would have been easy to explore that . Don't miss out on anything just becasue it does not fit into your perception yet. Try something new,something that goes against the grain even. That comfort zone is ever present.
Following seas!

### cinnamon — 2015-06-15T23:50:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-168294>

hmmm...
I just went to go buy water for the cabin and discovered there aren't many options! (No H2O party pack anymore!)

All they have is "Carnival Brand" bottled water and San Pellegrino.

I found this very recent news article as well:
[http://money.cnn.com/2015/06/10/news/companies/carnival-cruises-bottled-water/](http://money.cnn.com/2015/06/10/news/companies/carnival-cruises-bottled-water/)

From [the Carnival website](https://help.carnival.com/app/answers/detail/a_id/2634/session/L2F2LzEvdGltZS8xNDM0Mzk5NDgyL3NpZC9vMkFfUFpvbQ%3D%3D) about the "Carnival Brand" water:
Q. Is the bottled water onboard spring water or purified?
A. The water is purifed and bottled by Ice River Springs.

From [IceRiverSprings.com](http://iceriversprings.com/water-types/):
**Purified / Demineralized**
**We start with either municipal water or spring water, depending on the location. The first step for municipal water is carbon filtration to remove the chlorine. The water is then processed through a reverse osmosis (RO) purification unit, which is a filter unit that is finer than one micron that removes most minerals and other impurities. The final product must contain no more than 10 parts per million of dissolved solids as stipulated by regulatory authorities. Before bottling we add a tiny amount of ozone.**

[The new policy](https://help.carnival.com/app/answers/detail/a_id/2633) goes into effect starting July 9 but it appears they have stopped offering options through The Fun Shops already (hence only 2 kinds of water available). **The price for a 12 pk of 500 ml bottles of the RO Carnival Brand is $2.99 if you buy before you sail. They say it is $4.99 if you wait and buy on board.** The San Pellegrino is considerably more expensive - $3.25 to $4.50 per l, depending on quantity purchased.

As the new policy is not actually in effect yet for our sailing, it appears we should still be allowed to carry on 12 bottles per person as described under [the old rules](https://help.carnival.com/app/answers/detail/a_id/1120/related/1).

So, in summary:

-   You might want to order some cheap Carnival RO water _before_ we sail.

-   And you might want to use your carry on limit to get yourself some Gerolsteiner, Badoit or Perrier!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-18T04:15:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-168659>

You can begin right now to act as if you have already achieved any goal you desire, and that outer experience of acting as if will create the inner experience— the billionaire mind-set, as it were— that will take you to the actual manifestation of that experience. Once you choose what it is you want to be, do, or have, all you have to do is start acting as if you already are being, doing, or having it. How would you act if you already were a straight-A student, top salesperson, highly paid consultant, rich entrepreneur, world-class athlete, best-selling author, internationally acclaimed artist, sought-after speaker, or celebrated actor or musician? How would you think, talk, act, carry yourself, dress, treat other people, handle money, eat, live, travel, and so forth? Once you have a clear picture of that, start being it— at the party! Do not come as you are today..........June 21 you will be who you are in 2025. Read the email invite carefully........I am not kidding about this. If you do not follow the invite just keep walking around the FQ.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-18T04:16:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-168660>

Everyone who got an email invite got the directions. They got the address. There will not be any exceptions.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-18T04:27:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-168661>

Create your ideal vision now 72 hours before it begins. Be very aware there will be reporters present, photographers and video cameras................You must stretch your imaginations even further than you think now, to make our vision even bigger still. When you attend the party, you should act as if it really were 2025 and your vision for you should have already manifest and already come to fruition. You are to dress the part, talk the part, and bring any props that demonstrated that our dream had already come true— books written, awards earned, and large paychecks received. Nothing is out of bounds............even bragging about your accomplishments, celebrating your successes with toasts and relishing the successes of others around you. I want to hear our reporters talking about how happy and fulfilled you were at your accomplishments, and discussing what you have in store for an encore. You are to stay in character the entire party and any spill over. I expect to see some costumes. I want stories from your hearts............I want you to dig deep.

### RobH — 2015-06-29T20:14:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-169934>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=169830)
>
> We know what we give to others but...............but we rarely know what others received from the interaction. Key lesson I learned the last 8 days. Night.

Wow. Magic indeed.

I've been notably and intermittently absent from this forum for a year or so. (well, maybe nobody has noticed but me). My own doing. When I started the Leptin Reset / CT in 2012 I was on fire. It was amazing and then I knew JK was onto something big and I had to pay attention to him. Then I had a medical problem (long story) -- a speed bump that really slowed me down. Lost my coherence. Slid back. Tried a few times unsucessfully to re-capture that magic and without success. Lots of excuses, working hard, busy family, etc. Started laying the ground work to making career and life changes and got behind on the blog and the webinars. The more behind I got the more reluctant I was to dive back in and catch up. Was sort of (now that I think about it) afraid to show up on the forum I think because I didn't want to admit failure -- maybe not to all of you but especially not to myself. We doctors are a stubborn and prideful lot who don't like to show our weaknesses of deficiencies. Now I know better -- and I will do better.

"It is only through failure that the quantum mechanism is revealed." JK

When the Kruse Cruise came along for this summer I wondered -- was this my chance to reconnect, to re-ignite that fire that burned so bright for a time? The embers were still there inside but only smouldering. I knew it was an opportunity for me to reconnect, but it was a risk. I could reconnect or I could fail again. And it seemed an even bigger risk to steer our one big family vacation of the summer to this when we've always sort of shunned cruises (visions of heaping buffet tables, crowds, and the conspicious excesses of modern life have kept that from being our ideal vacation -- used to do a lot of backpacking. We like serenity and wilderness and uncrowded places) My unbelievably supportive wife (have I ever mentioned how wonderful she is) readily agreed to consider the cruise and so after talking about it for a while we booked.

This was by far the most memorable and transformative week of my life. I cannot thank Jack and Sandy enough for putting this together, making it happen, and bringing together an extraordinary group of people from all over the world. I wish I'd gotten to spend more time with each and every one of you, because I have to much to learn. All of our day to day interactions were meaningful to me.

Getting a chance to meet Jeremy and hear and watch little parts of his story was incredible and inspiring. I cannot make excuses anymore. "Nobody I know does more with less than Jeremy" It is true. And yet through all that shines a gentleman, a beautiful soul, and an extraordinary athlete, artist, and human being. There is no self-pity but rather a intuitive determination and resolve to suceed that cannot be ignored.

Sandy, you gave the gift of countless hours of your time to organize this event. My family thanks you.

Jack, you may know what you've given, but let me try in my own poor way, to tell you what I recieved. All of the information you gave was hinted at, posted, or put somewhere else previously. The science you have put out there and sometimes it takes me some work to understand it but there were no surprises there. I marvel at your ability to connect and extrapolate. What was new to me, and what I didn't expect was the tremendous gift of yourself, your time, your passion, your energy, and your wisdom. And you were honest, and sometimes brutally so (the New Yorker in you I suppose). That too is a gift that I am happy to recieve.

There are so many layers to what you teach, from the simple version (it's about "DHA, light and water") to the quantum physics, microbiology, biochem, astronomy, etc. There are many many (but finite I think, although perhaps we'll never see the end of it) layers to this onion and I marvel at how it is all interconnected. I hope in my own poor way to take it apply it to the people around me an the patients I see and the world at large.

What I received from you was a gift of energy -- electrons if you will -- an infusion of your passion, your certainty, your determination. You showed me that I can't compromise if I am to achieve an optimal state. There is a tradeoff in everything we do and I see that now. You shared some of that magnetism that you have created within yourself, your ability to harvest energy, and what I have received from you is a jump-start.

Perhaps the greatest gift I recieved was being able to bring my family to this cruise. Just as Sandy is your battery I know Julie is mine. Having her there to hear you, meet you, and converse with you was priceless. There is no question in my mind that my family will be so much the better for this experience, so much more optimal. Having Max and Quinn and Jack there was priceless. Now that we all know better, we will do better. And we will light others candles along the way.

You re-lit my candle.... with a flame-thrower.

And showed me what I need to do to keep it burning, and growing.

Thank you to Jack, Sandy, and all the Krusers -- what you shared with us was priceless. What I recieved from all of you was energy. I can only hope that I gave as much as I got. I will share more in the future.

RobH

### nonchalant — 2015-06-29T20:15:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-169936>

We're back! We had such a great time! It was so nice to meet everyone.
Thank you so much, Sandy, for organizing this marvelous event in such a magical place with such wonderful people.

### RobH — 2015-06-29T20:17:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-169937>

The place was magical. Both Julie and I commented last night how good we felt there...

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-30T01:12:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-169978>

**My personal top take aways from the cruise without much detail thrown in post surgery today

1\. Jeremy spoke to class in a big way one day: You could hear a pin drop
2\. Shari's response to Jeremy's movie.
3\. The innovator's cocktail party setting the tone for what would come that week. Neil's face getting stuck with the bill at the Italian Barrel. LOL
4\. Max's questions and Julie's facial expressions: I think they got a ton out of this gathering. Rob being re lit.
5\. Me and Sandy doing the crater together in a deep way and seeing the effect on her at our last meeting.
6\. Mamadell's BD dinner........electric meets magnetic.
7\. Yew's performance in pink speedo's showing us what an Asian tiger is built to do.
8\. Joe giving me advice how to begin the meetings.
9\. Joe and Cinammon flying with Jeremy. Cinammon being a calming force in a stormy sea all week..........
10\. Meeting the non members who came with members and seeing how they interacted with us. Magical.
11\. Pat not giving up, but digging deeper.
12\. Meeting Gairy from St. Lucia at the cocktail party and sharing his story with Julie.
13\. touching every person there.........with my fingers without them knowing what I was doing.
14\. Connecting with Danny yet again and helping him out of a tough spot and then entangling him with us.
15\. Yew's 98 Grange and his picture from Danny.
16\. My conversation with Randy and Rob that morining about Jeremy and how I missed some keys from Jeremy
17\. Inger proving Jack wrong.
18\. Entangling Joe, Josh, and Jayme with Doris Metropolitan and the FQ tree by Muses that called Joe and me calling it before he did it!!!!
19\. Spending every dinner with Devin and him showing the old guard how the new guard has the goods.
20\. Meeting Gretchen's kids...........at the cocktail party.
21\. OptimalBounds identity at the innovator's cocktail party and what he gave me that floored me.
22\. My personal favorite talk: Coming back from Belize in a downpour with the boys and discussing a magnetic monopole while ladies listened intently at something they could not fathom.........
23\. Last day in the pool with everyone and the crowd just felt we were different........
24\. Seeing Inger eat seafood live.
25\. Seeing my wife getting entangled with my members live.
26\. Seeing all the kids on the boat stop and ask us about our glasses and no shoes.
27\. Devin's video on EZ water columns during a rainstorm
28\. Sally's potholders and her little speech the last day..........
29\. Seeing Kali's face when she realized people want to hear me talk while she takes what I say for granted. Awakenings.
30\. My discussion on the bus from our mudder day in Belize with Sarah about consciousness.
31\. Teaching CT to people in the crater
32\. Sharing a song with the group close to the end of our journey
33\. Neil stealing my entanglement bracelet and giving me his........he liked mine better. Now mine is too tight and wrong color.**
**34\. Our picture from above of all our bracelets in the aft of the boat.**
**35\. First two nights of dinner and demolishing Carnival's wine closet of any Opus one or good Malbec.**
**36\. My dinner with Michele...........before they crashed it.**
**37\. Seeing Martha's face while she heard new ideas..........**
**38\. Watching Julie take it all in daily.......and seeing her family morph.**
**39\. Patrick in deep need for an orange robe...........all week.**
**40\. Seeing Joe connect in some many non verbal ways...........**
**41\. Talking to Lindsay before the last meeting.........seeing her watery eyes as she spoke with passion.**
**42\. Hearing Jayme tell me that Sandy told her how we got engaged..........without me knowing it.**
**43\. See John at the innovator's cocktail party.**
**44\. Diego telling me many times how this week was life altering and then seeing it in his eyes............**
**45\. The emails I received about the party.........**
**46\. All the help we received from Cathy Parnell............**
**47\. Speaking privately with many about who should have been on this boat............**
**48\. All my electron stealing all week during my massages and my touching.............**
**49\. The wait staff on 12 faces and reactions wondering why all these people kept crashing my table all week...........The manager finally asking me the last night............**
**50\. The conversations that members had with my wife trying to convince her to do this again................Sandy was touched and I was surprised.**

### Chrysanthemama — 2015-06-30T01:26:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-169979>

Sounds fantastic and magical and entangling. Love it. If there is a next time, I will not let other distractions and voices get in the way, but will follow my intuitive heart, since she is always right.
Beautiful!

### caroline — 2015-06-30T01:28:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-169980>

All lot of us just couldn't make it this time ...... but I think we did understand that we would be missing something huge.

I really understand what everyone is feeling ...... When I went to Orlando - it was as if powers out of my control took over and I needed to go there.

I was lucky enough to entangle ....

**39\. Patrick in deep need for an orange robe...........all week.**

That's a pretty epic tour de force/description..............not to make it all about me but to make it all about me (the behavior of an orange robe wearer) I really think that is pretty clever. Throws me for a bit of a loop. Here I am 'studying' and 'deconstructing' cults or trying to.............the joke's on me who really has a concern about 'cults' and cultist behavior...........the guy 'studying' it ha ha ha. Still maybe that could be applied to all 'scientists' why are they studying something............because they don't get it or can't feel it or whatever.

What's a little discouraging too is Jack says "all week" like I did not change or go with the flow or whatever..............and though I did or tried to it's true too I did not or wish I could or did more. Anyway just trying to be honest here above all with myself. It was also interesting to look at all the pictures it's like mostly I do not LIKE the way I look. I look fat and heavy which I am....................but it's all good good to see myself from the outside a bit and I am home now and KNOW I have work to do................a lot of work to do. Hope I strike out in mostly the right directions.

About the Cruise I feel I only started to get the hang of it around Friday..............better late than never I suppose but it would be nice to be on time not always trailing behind. My GPS is running slow...........

### Inger — 2015-07-02T15:28:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-170352>

OMG I love all the pics, guys!

I always forget to take pics..... so glad some of you did!

I am still waiting for jetlag to kick in.... but nothing... I do not get this?!
I am absolutely zero jetlagged.... even if I went wild  and had a glass of wine and dinner at the longest 11 hour flight... I just had to pee twice even if I had lots of water... and the other flight that was 4 hour I had to pee once and the other 1 hour flight I did not pee... had lots and lots of water... mysterious!
I am feeling just awesome.... sleep was great last night and I feel completely tuned into my environment... I did as soon as I was here.....
must be all the love
I did tan whole bod 20 minute the mornings before work - maybe it did the trick?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-04T19:23:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-170768>

The fourth of July has become a farce. We need to celebrate people we care about it. Here is some perspective we all need today: The paradox of our time in history is that we have taller buildings but shorter tempers, wider Freeways, but narrower viewpoints. We spend more, but have less, we buy more, but enjoy less. We have bigger houses and smaller families, more conveniences, but less time. We have more degrees but less sense, more knowledge, but less judgment, more experts, yet more problems, more medicine, but less wellness.

We drink too much, smoke too much, spend too recklessly, laugh too little, drive too fast, get too angry, stay up too late, get up too tired, read too little, watch TV too much, and pray too seldom.

We have multiplied our possessions, but reduced our values. We talk too much, love too seldom, and hate too often.

We've learned how to make a living, but not a life. We've added years to life not life to years. We've been all the way to the moon and back, but have trouble crossing the street to meet a new neighbor. We conquered outer space but not inner space. We've done larger things, but not better things.

We've cleaned up the air, but polluted the soul. We've conquered the atom, but not our prejudice. We write more, but learn less. We plan more, but accomplish less. We've learned to rush, but not to wait. We build more computers to hold more information, to produce more copies than ever, but we communicate less and less.

These are the times of fast foods and slow digestion, big men and small character, steep profits and shallow relationships. These are the days of two incomes but more divorce, fancier houses, but broken homes. These are days of quick trips, disposable diapers, throwaway morality, one night stands, overweight bodies, and pills that do everything from cheer, to quiet, to kill. It is a time when there is much in the showroom window and nothing in the stockroom. A time when technology can bring this letter to you, and a time when you can choose either to share this insight, or to just hit delete.

Remember to spend some time with your loved ones, because they are not going to be around forever.

Remember, say a kind word to someone who looks up to you in awe, because that little person soon will grow up and leave your side.

Remember, to give a warm hug to the one next to you, because that is the only treasure you can give with your heart and it doesn't cost a cent.

Remember, to say, 'I love you' to your partner and your loved ones, but most of all mean it. A kiss and an embrace will mend hurt when it comes from deep inside of you.

Remember to hold hands and cherish the moment for someday that person will not be there again.

Give time to love, give time to speak! And give time to share the precious thoughts in your mind.

And always remember, life is not measured by the number of breaths we take, but by those moments that take our breath away.

George Carlin

### RobH — 2015-07-04T20:33:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-170803>

We text too much and talk too little.

Thanks for sharing. I share little over social media, but will share this. I'm a bit new to putting much of anything on social media. Generally eschew it as I spend too much time on computer / phone / text as it is. I see the value of it in building a community though. Given our current climate I've always worried a bit about building a footprint / trail. But some things are worth sharing.

Independence and freedom are worth celebrating -- as are connections that we willfully seek out and make.

RobH

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-05T23:28:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-170974>

So Patrick asked me on the cruise several times if I was going to burnout because of all the questions, wine, late night dinner's, etc...........and I smiled at him.

I told him Irishman to Irishman I had a plan mapped out.................and my eye twinkled a bit when I said it to him.

So what did I know he didn't?

A teaching lesson about numbers: Numbers explain physics better via colors but few people see the world in numbers. Most everyone can see colors to understand a picture. As I posted in the Ubi series, colors is all about frequency. Colors for me, and hopefully for our community here, is not about just what we see, it is about what color fundamentally are in physics..............they are quantized information and energy packets of vibrational energy. Consider red light: has a frequency of 436,803,079,680,000 oscillations per second. That equates to the same energy in a high C note in music. Paul Teipe might like that comparison. That note has an oscillation frequency of 4,096 oscillations per second. Xrays, microwaves, cosmic, and gamma rays all have these numbers and colors tied to them.........but when you read them they make no sense to you........but color has a _qualia_ you do get. Your grandma's book.........the pic has meaning.........to you. You know what it means to me? Magnetism has the HIGHEST oscillatory rate we know of in nature. Few people know this. It is 18,446,744,,073,709,551,616 oscillations per second.........IT is hard for the mind to grasp that many things vibrating that many times in a second........But guess what.......last week on the cruise.....everyone else did there own thing in Playa Del Carmen on the cruise........and What did I make sure to do? I went into tthe crater where those Oscillations have existed for 65 million yrs.........Patrick asked me how I expended all the energy I did every day and night on the cruise.......and not get tired. I reminded him I flew to Austin the day before the cruise and spoke to a large group of humans at Voice & Exit and then got back on a plane and flew home to the innovator's cocktail party and blew some more of my redox away too! He just chuckled. I did not. I knew exactly what I was doing. I remained ketotic and at once and day and drank a ton of water........why? Because that water assimilated all those oscillations from the crater to renew me.........I knew I could give a lot for 9 days.......because I was going to get a lot more in my very short soujourn in the crater. For those of you bad at math: That number above is 18 quintillion, 446 quadrillion, 744 trillion, 73 billion, 709 million, 551 thousand, 6 hundred, and 16 oscillations per second of vibrational energy. That my dear people is physics 101. Get some. I invited everyone to the crater but only my family went................too bad.

Dr K. I can't be the only one who wonders about that................how do you avoid 'burnout'? To continue the Irish theme my feeling is be careful to avoid giving too much of yourself. People tend to just take what is given and not give a thought to what it costs you. I know a bit of what I speak here.............I did that too until I had little or nothing left and IMO it's worth keeping in mind most of the world is not "Irish" (that can stand for any indigenous people) and in a world of 'every man for himself' people just tend to take and take.................until there is nothing left.

### prAna303 — 2015-07-07T10:18:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-171237>

> Burnout

Something i felt and looking back on my life as a painting i always found clues why i sometimes in history felt something one might call that...

I think it is one of the best signs that i do not belong. Something that pointed some markers inside of me at the wrong direction, often when zoomed out i find one great mismatch, lost natural energy. But it is not just about gaining it, it is more about sharing it... Always when i used to do work i come to a point where i almost explode, systems are to dumb and dull, the people around me is not in sync anymore, one could say that entropy has reached a state where it is almost impossible to withstand it.

The word burnout is quite good because it is more how good the flow is. Force flow cost to much, force gain and wear and tear comes and nock on your door.

One great factor of this error today is the non complex world of computers. It is not information overload, it is the lost of it. Often today we make systems that should be easy and friendly to use but that makes us feel stressed, why? Because it is such a stupid tool that do not keep up with the tempo of a living thing.

Okey back to the thing called burnout. For about ten years now i never felt this, again why?

Turning the technology side of me away has given me so much greater chance to not get drained. Simply by switching the time from being at "work" for eight hours to be out in nature that time is like... Outstanding.

Being in nature and take those ideas, thoughts i have and put them in the right light they often get crystal clear. Problems get solved in seconds and they are no more, problems.

Greatest thing for me has been looking. To take away my eyes from dead things and pointing them to living has started a tsunami inside of me... This is something that many today are missing. Being inside a house, sitting in front of a computer, walking with a smartphone, looking thru a lense the whole time opens up wormholes you do not want in living life. This has also been a clue to why i can stand modern environment better BUT only when i share it with other living things AND we look at each other... Being in a place where you can look at eyes, bodies, movement and other things gives me a better hand at the poker table. I give and suck light! Also, i would give the advice to shut up. Just look and talk with your eyes and body. Again words are so low in bandwith compared to what we as living things are.

Is this also why some of us love being on a stage? When standing there and having many eyes locked on you can give you such a high that time and space expand, it is a state where we are connected, just like we once were BEFORE we got to smart. Most get stressed to be in front of a group of humans, can it be the tons of glimses that starts waves of energy passing inside of you and you feel for the first time in a long period, parts of you that gets energy. If you are out of sync with the brain and body you feel bad, in sync you get lifted BUT then again, there should not be any difference between the two of them because they are not isolated by nature.

### JMO — 2015-07-07T16:23:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-171261>

I was home for a day after the cruise and left for Golden, CO. When I returned from the cruise, I lost 3 pounds. My weight is so incredibly stable it hardly ever moves so 3 lbs is a lot for me. I was in CO for 6 days...when I returned stepped on the scale and I had gained the 3 lbs I lost plus 3 more. Today I'm down 2. Magnetism is big. Alot of the parents we were there with complained every morning about not being able to sleep...hypoxia. I slept well...but I used to go to Denver for work and I always remember not sleeping so my redox must be better. Whew....a busy couple weeks for me. Back to CT and oysters!

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-07-08T01:16:04+0300

>
> Dr K. I can't be the only one who wonders about that................how do you avoid 'burnout'? To continue the Irish theme my feeling is be careful to avoid giving too much of yourself. People tend to just take what is given and not give a thought to what it costs you. I know a bit of what I speak here.............I did that too until I had little or nothing left and IMO it's worth keeping in mind most of the world is not "Irish" (that can stand for any indigenous people) and in a world of 'every man for himself' people just tend to take and take.................until there is nothing left.

my biggest pow moment from the entire cruise was how many peoples health was affected by personal "burnout" issues such as work, and relationships
interpersonal communication defects are seldom spoken about in health circles but the cruise uncovered that it was the main thunderstorm that affected most people's health. i made the call to jack that one persons description of their life story to me was like a description of a pinata that had been hit too many times.
when there have been too many insults to the system crawling back to health (holding energy - or candy in the broken pinata story) seems a daunting task.

pat i found your story very touching too.

### Grmoline — 2015-07-08T13:10:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-171362>

> [JoeBranca said:](/goto/post?id=171299)
>
> yes, an expanded definition of "toxic environment" was a big takeaway for me as well. lots of meaningful interactions, though looking back i wish i personally made time for more opportunities to real-talk with others during that week. Jack did a tremendous job paying attention to what might be up with individual thunderstorms, and enzyme-like getting the right people talking to each other and listening to each other.

When life is viewed through the lens of frequencies and energies, then the expanded definition of toxic environment makes a lot of sense. In my experience, there are frequencies that resonate and those that don't, whether it's art, foods, music, textures, or other people's energies. All could be explained by frequency, and it's rarely a case of neutral. I know what it feels like when deep resonance happens, whether it's a phrase of music that takes my breath away or a connection with another human being that goes beyond words. We even talk about it as "being on the same wavelength". In resonance, 2 + 2 is greater than 4 because the frequencies join and enhance each other. Conversely, in non-resonance (or dissonance) they can damp each other out. Now think about what happens in your relationships or your work environment - does it enhance your energy or damp it out?

In doing transformational training, which I do now, it initially involves understanding the other person's context and meeting them where they are. When you drop into intuition the second part of that, meeting others where they are, becomes much easier. I think it's reading and matching energy, getting resonance, and then walking a path together.

I wish I'd had more connections on the cruise, to allow more opportunities for those 'enzyme moments'. I let shyness get the better of me....
Yet, the ones I had were transformational for me.

### prAna303 — 2015-07-08T20:04:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-171402>

Why we are important.

There is a good reason why a small group of humans start to look at things in a different light will create something way bigger. Some might think this is to much for us humans to bare, i think of many who left this forum...

In nature we see this small factor play a huge role, we have been shown it thru some posts but do most of us understand who big this is? What is it? The role of few and small.

Over and over again we see that when something goes to big, a small thing will change that in just a second. It is like watching a crowd filled with people dresses in black and just one in red, we spot it in a blink of a second. We see it in economy and in companies, the creative minds that stand out and always do change in business. We see it in music where some are real stars, way beyond the others, changing things over and over again. Why try to change the big group, the big society when it is way more natural to find these small particles that goes against every common sense...

That small change is not someone with a special gift, just a talent to see and change and that my friends are us.

### TerrierMom — 2015-07-09T06:49:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-171448>

I don't think I have posted since the cruise, I am still processing. I have a notebook full of notes and a heart full of feelings. I got what I wanted out of the cruise - to meet some amazing people I have only "known" online for a while and CONNECT with them. I wanted to be pulled back into something I had once completely immersed myself in. Just committing to the cruise did that as I scurried to "catch up" on the webinars (not that I managed to!) so that I could at least have as much knowledge as possible. Every single person enriched my experience and had something to teach me. I learned so much from the Q&A of others. I was not at all surprised that people from all lands, races, and walks of life and backgrounds could connect like that, because we all are striving to find optimal and when you are doing that, you know you cannot do it alone and it takes other people. People on the cruise were some of those people!! Like Gerry I wish I had been a little less shy in talking to people more, and to be fair I did get myself a good ways out of my comfort zone so I can't beat myself up too much. I drew strength, inspiration, and courage from all of your journeys and hope there will be more opportunities to meet like this. I was thrilled that my sister went and learned too - it is better for her to hear it from someone other than me, but that is just icing on the cake because it is ME that I need to work on (I hear you Jack!). And I am refreshed, renewed, and recommitted to moving the needle towards optimal.

Thank you to Jack for sharing yourself with us all these years and on the cruise, thank you to each and every person who was a fellow Kruse Cruiser, and double triple quadruple thanks to Sandy for making it happen and for sharing Jack with us. Now I just need to upload a freaking photo for my profile!

### Lahelada — 2015-07-11T15:35:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-cruise-thread.12344/post-171680>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=170974)
>
> So Patrick asked me on the cruise several times if I was going to burnout because of all the questions, wine, late night dinner's, etc...........and I smiled at him.
>
> A teaching lesson about numbers: Numbers explain physics better via colors but few people see the world in numbers. Most everyone can see colors to understand a picture. As I posted in the Ubi series, colors is all about frequency. Colors for me, and hopefully for our community here, is not about just what we see, it is about what color fundamentally are in physics..............they are quantized information and energy packets of vibrational energy. Consider red light: has a frequency of 436,803,079,680,000 oscillations per second. That equates to the same energy in a high C note in music. Paul Teipe might like that comparison. That note has an oscillation frequency of 4,096 oscillations per second. Xrays, microwaves, cosmic, and gamma rays all have these numbers and colors tied to them.........but when you read them they make no sense to you........but color has a _qualia_ you do get. Your grandma's book.........the pic has meaning.........to you. You know what it means to me? Magnetism has the HIGHEST oscillatory rate we know of in nature. Few people know this. It is 18,446,744,,073,709,551,616 oscillations per second.........IT is hard for the mind to grasp that many things vibrating that many times in a second........But guess what.......last week on the cruise.....everyone else did there own thing in Playa Del Carmen on the cruise........and What did I make sure to do? I went into tthe crater where those Oscillations have existed for 65 million yrs.........Patrick asked me how I expended all the energy I did every day and night on the cruise.......and not get tired. I reminded him I flew to Austin the day before the cruise and spoke to a large group of humans at Voice & Exit and then got back on a plane and flew home to the innovator's cocktail party and blew some more of my redox away too! He just chuckled. I did not. I knew exactly what I was doing. I remained ketotic and at once and day and drank a ton of water........why? Because that water assimilated all those oscillations from the crater to renew me.........I knew I could give a lot for 9 days.......because I was going to get a lot more in my very short soujourn in the crater. For those of you bad at math: That number above is 18 quintillion, 446 quadrillion, 744 trillion, 73 billion, 709 million, 551 thousand, 6 hundred, and 16 oscillations per second of vibrational energy. That my dear people is physics 101. Get some. I invited everyone to the crater but only my family went................too bad.

Crater vacations ? self.gutenberg.org/articles/List\_of\_impact\_craters\_on\_Earth

# Thread: Cocoa butter?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cocoa-butter.12345/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lilreddgirl — 2014-12-10T06:25:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cocoa-butter.12345/post-149659>

It's December and in the Northern Hemisphere San Francisco Northern California area...

The cocoa nibs/ 100 % Chocolates have 14 grams carbs listed per oz. I've been eating about 1 oz per meal. I get the feeling my body mostly 'likes' it - energy wise. Should I still eat them as the weather gets colder and I start practicing CT - beginner here ? I'm eating no other carbs usually... and I like how I'm feeling no carb. I notice I did not 'like' my carb 'binge' of eating 2 oz + of Cocoa nibs the other day. Is Cocoa encouraged even in cold?

On the other hand **COCOA BUTTER** has same Magnesium content as the nibs, and lots of Saturated fat listed... but 0 carbs. I've melted some in my cooking. Do I still get the Cocoa SIRT1 stem cell protectant (right?) benefits from the butter? Is Cocoa Butter ok'd for including in cooking, in cold?

### shah78 — 2014-12-11T02:11:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cocoa-butter.12345/post-149775>

ninety percent of the carbs are fiber. should be good to go.

### lilreddgirl — 2014-12-12T19:37:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cocoa-butter.12345/post-149923>

Thank you shah78. That handles the Cocoa nibs...

what about the Cocoa Butter? has lots of saturated fat looks creamy off white... same benefits as Jack says for cocoa/ 100 % chocolate ?

# Thread: Vitamin K2 where in food?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-k2-where-in-food.12346/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lilreddgirl — 2014-12-10T06:28:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-k2-where-in-food.12346/post-149661>

How can I mega load up on Vit K2 from foods rather than supplement? I think I have some gallbladder problems - inflammation - I read Jack has mentioned K2 deficiency as possibility for gallbladder issues.. Went kinda anorexic last year... How can I mega load up? What food should I eat for K2 recovery and how much a day.

Lots of thanks.

### nonchalant — 2014-12-10T06:30:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-k2-where-in-food.12346/post-149662>

It's made in your colon, if you have the correct flora.

### lilreddgirl — 2014-12-10T06:32:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-k2-where-in-food.12346/post-149664>

Thank you Nonchalant. What if I want to rapidly go up from a deficiency? Can I intake extra from diet and from what foods?

Also, can I check if my colon has correct flora? Intuition says nost of my flora is in good shape... not sure about surrounding Gallbladder Liver area... working on healing that with Coconut Oil and L-Glutamine... it's working.

### nonchalant — 2014-12-10T16:26:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-k2-where-in-food.12346/post-149694>

Raw oysters, especially if they are swallowed whole, can nourish the correct flora. If swallowed whole, more of the good stuff makes it further down the tract. I forget where Dr. K discussed this.

### shah78 — 2014-12-10T16:49:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-k2-where-in-food.12346/post-149699>

Green Pastures Butter Oil. I consider it actual, real food. The web site exudes hockes-pockus, but the product seems to work. IMHO.

### lilreddgirl — 2014-12-12T19:48:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-k2-where-in-food.12346/post-149925>

Thank you both. I am also going to be ordering the supplement K2 in Mk4 form. Just sharing i found it very inexpensively at Vitaspace for 80$ for 5 grams plus 6$ shipping in US. The dosage is usually in milligrams or micrograms.

### lilreddgirl — 2014-12-12T19:52:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-k2-where-in-food.12346/post-149926>

Nonchalant do you know an inexpensive way to get raw Oysters? I can get Crown Prince boiled oysters in a jar for $3.85, about 10 in there they are big and most oyster/price I've found. I want raw but I want to find some way to sustain my continued consumption financially.

Shah78 - thats one - Green Pastures Butter Oil /Cod Liver capsules - I've been putting off due to it's somewhat pricy. How many capsules do you consume/recommend as food per meal ?

### shah78 — 2014-12-12T19:58:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-k2-where-in-food.12346/post-149927>

Actually I stay clear of the cod liver oil part. You can order straight Butter OIl.

### lilreddgirl — 2014-12-12T20:02:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-k2-where-in-food.12346/post-149930>

Shah78 - why do you stay clear of the cod liver oil?

### shah78 — 2014-12-12T20:14:27+0200

# Thread: Pentacyclic triterpenes

**Posts:** 1

### Dombant — 2014-12-10T13:40:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pentacyclic-triterpenes.12353/post-149684>

I was searching for the effects of Pentacyclic Triterpenes on brown fat generation and found that ursolic acid, oleanolic acid, and betulinic acid have wide ranging health effects. Ursolic acid can increase brown fat, at least in mice. UA seems to convert between Oleanolic acid and UA in the body. Betulinic acid, UA, and OA are being studied for wide ranging health effects including obesity reduction and immunity. It is interesting that birch bark and chaga mushroom which grows on the birch bark in the far cold north have relatively high amounts of 1 or more of these PT's. Chaga is considered a medicinal or adaptogenic mushroom. Rosemary and apple peels are high in PT's, too.

Do PT's have a place in the overall Rx?

[\[url=https://plus.google.com/+JeangreyCofan/posts\]dr jean grey](https://helpouts.google.com/113838601398151265676/ls/8ac079cb1db4077d)\[/URL\]

# Thread: Hydrogen proton's........rogueness.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hydrogen-protons-rogueness.12358/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-11T03:25:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hydrogen-protons-rogueness.12358/post-149785>

Hydrogen has the largest energy density over any other fuel in the world. When we burn hydrogen in air, the reaction should be:

2H2(g)+O2(g) → 2H2(l) (ΔH=-286kJ/mol)
Which means that 1mol of hydrogen can produce 286kJ energy through reaction with oxygen. It is different when proton spin is altered. Obviously, the energy density of hydrogen is E = 286 kJ/mol × 0.5 mol/g = 143kJ/g, which is much higher than that of many other fuels such as methane. Because of the high energy density, in order to reduce the weight of space shuttle, people choose to use liquid hydrogen as the fuel.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-11T03:33:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hydrogen-protons-rogueness.12358/post-149786>

What are the two products of mitochondrial action? CO2 and water................

Another important application of "hydrogen action" as an energy carrier is in the ultimate biologic fuel cell (think monopole). Hydrogen (H+) acts as the anode (like the planets) and splits into protons and electrons under the assistance of catalyst (sunlight or hydrophilic proteins). Protons move from anode towards cathode through a conductive electrolyte 9cell water or an ionic plasma), and electrons go through external circuit (ECT) and meet with protons and oxygen in cathode (cytochromes). Then the product, water, is produced, as well as electric power (BOOM). The voltage of this cell can be calculated by the Gibbs free energy, ΔG = -237.1 kJ/mol, which gives the voltage applying the Nernst equation E = -ΔG/nF = 237.1kJ/mol2 × 96485C/mol = 1.229 V. The fuel cells (sum or mitochondria) contain a large amount of energy, and the product is water, which is totally friendly to the environment. Did you know new stars spew out water when they are formed? The investigations into the fuel cells are going to make an avenue to the applications of hydrogen as an energy carrier, such as the hydrogen cars and hydrogen power station.

[http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2011/06/110613-space-science-star-water-bullets-kristensen/](http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2011/06/110613-space-science-star-water-bullets-kristensen/)
[http://www.popsci.com/technology/ar...ng-star-spewing-water-jets-interstellar-space](http://www.popsci.com/technology/article/2011-06/herschel-spots-young-star-spewing-water-jets-interstellar-space)

The sun and mitochondria have a lot in common...............I am just piecing it together for you as you're in disbelief.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-11T03:35:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hydrogen-protons-rogueness.12358/post-149787>

Nowadays there are two ways for us to produce hydrogen. In industry, hydrogen is produced by steam reforming of natural gas (CH4), which is shown as follows:

CH4(g) + H2O(g) → CO(g) + 3H2(g)
Most of the hydrogen we use today comes out from this reaction in factories.

we dont have methane and we are not a factory. So where does H+ come from in our biologic star: a mitochondria?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-11T03:37:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hydrogen-protons-rogueness.12358/post-149788>

In humans, who have more mitochondria in their brain, what surrounds the neocortex? CSF. 99.9% of CSF is what? Water.

Want to guess where mitochondria H+ comes from?

Most want to guess food.................

Y'all are too smart for that.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-11T03:45:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hydrogen-protons-rogueness.12358/post-149790>

However, it is familiar to most people on my site that we can also split _water_ to produce **hydrogen**. As I mentioned above in a biologic fuel cell, the output voltage is 1.23 V. This voltage is reversible (key point of the tensegrity system), which means that 1.23 V is the _lowest limit voltage_ we need to split water into hydrogen and oxygen. However, the voltage is calculated thermodynamically (statistically), and kinetically (sounds like the matrix no?) , higher voltage is needed depending on the electrode we use (inner mito membrane has 30,000,000 volts DHA and thinness). This 'idea' relates to the catalyst for hydrogen's evolution into life, and in order to explain it clearly, I introduce the Tafel behavior in g electrochemistry briefly. What is that?

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tafel\_equation](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tafel_equation)

For Hydrogen to evolve or emerge with acid electrolyte, hydrogen evolves from the reaction 2H + aq + 2e → H2(g). The rate of this reaction is tested by the current through the circuit. The relationship between the current and the voltage we apply is called current-overpotential equation: (another name for the Tafel equation)

i = i0 \[C0(0,t)/C0\* e-αnfη - CR(0,t)/CR\*e (1-α)nfη\]
in which C0 and CR represent the concentrations of the participants, i0 is the exchange current, α stands for the asymmetry coefficient, f=F/RT, and η represents the overpotential. When the overpotential goes to large negative, the left term in the bracket is much larger than the right one, and the equation can be rewritten as i=i0 e-αnfη. The current increases exponentially with overpotential, which is named as Tafel behavior. From this behavior, we can extract two important parameters, exchange current i0 and Tafel slope 1/αnf by fitting the linear relationship between log(i) and η. These two parameters are the criterions for the catalytic activity of catalysts. In order to be the best catalyst for hydrogen to evolve/emerge, it should achieve the largest exchange current and the smallest Tafel slope. And until now, as we now realize, platinum is the unbeatable catalyst for hydrogen evolution which has the exchange current density of 4.5 × 10-4 A/cm2, and the Tafel slope as small as 30 mV/decade. This works in your car..........but not so much in us. We use something else.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-11T03:46:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hydrogen-protons-rogueness.12358/post-149791>

We dont use platinum for two reasons........atomic mass too big.......and it is rare on earth like Helium.

So what do we use in our cells sun?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-11T03:48:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hydrogen-protons-rogueness.12358/post-149792>

Molybdenum and sulfur or phosphorus The free energy calculations for MoS2 is very close to platinium.

Ironic, no? MoS2

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-11T03:51:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hydrogen-protons-rogueness.12358/post-149793>

Do we have MoS2?

Nope.

What do we have? Moly and Fe-S clusters. and electrons and protons in the cytochrome channels. Fe is ferromagnetic and S dimagnetic.

Moly is special too in a magnetic field.........and electron sink

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-11T04:01:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hydrogen-protons-rogueness.12358/post-149795>

The whole process of Hydrogen evolution/emergence in a system can be divided into two steps: absorption and desorption. First, the protons from the solution (ionic plasma) are attached to the catalytic sites of the electrode (matrix close to Molybdenum). Then the electrons from the electrode combine with the protons to form hydrogen atoms. Two hydrogen atoms combine together to become a hydrogen molecule. Desorption then takes place and molecular hydrogen leaves from the site of the electrode. One of the two steps may be the rate determine step. Platinum is the rate determining step in a car's exhaust and is the _desorption step_ because the Gibbs free energy is uphill. For MoS2, the rate determining step changes to the _absorption step_. Why is this big? Because it becomes a zero entropy step because Gibbs free energy of the reaction is ZERO!!! This is why biology like Moly. Any time the Gibbs free energy approaches to zero, the _catalyst becomes very active (kinetic ipsotope effect)_ because the rate determining step is also very fast (faster than the speed of light).

Now remember that Mo and S are close to one another with iron in between them on the inner mitochondrial membrane. Coincidence? However, bulk chemical MoS2, it is not active for Hydrogen evolution at all because of its atomic structure.

Here you see QED enter the picture with size and shape and quantum abilities. MoS2 has a hexagonal structure consisting of three covalently bonded atomic sheets, which are S-Mo-S in sequence. The planar, two-dimensional layers are linked by van der Waals interaction. Mitochondria break up this effect by putting Fe next to the S and further away from Moly.

Why? The reason why bulk chemical MoS2 is not an active catalyst for hydrogen in biology is because not enough edge sites are exposed. So what did lady evolution do?

She decided to use a transition metal with lots of D shell electrons that was ferromagnetic and could electrically or magnetically effect this atomic relationship.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-11T04:03:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hydrogen-protons-rogueness.12358/post-149796>

Cites for my ramblings:

\[1\] A. J. Bard and L. R. Faulkner, _Electrochemical Methods_ (Wiley, 2000).

\[2\] B. E. Conway and B. V. Tilak, "Interfacial Processes Involving Electrocatalytic Evolution and Oxidation of H2 and the Role of Chemisorbed H," Electrochim. Acta **47**, 3571 (2002).

\[3\] B. Hinnemann _et al._, "Biomimetic Hydrogen Evolution: MoS2 Nanoparticles as Catalyst for Hydrogen Evolution," J. Am. Chem. Soc. **127**, 5308 (2005).

\[4\] C. Kisielowski _et al._, "Imaging MoS2 Nanocatalysts With Single-Atom Sensitivity," Angew. Chem. Int. Ed. **49**, 2708 (2010).

\[5\] T. F. Jaramillo _et al._, "Identification of Active Edge Sites for Electrochemical H2 Evolution From MoS2 Nanocatalysts," Science **317**, 100 (2007).

\[6\] Y. Li _et al._, "MoS2 Nanoparticles Grown on Graphene: An Advanced Catalyst for the Hydrogen Evolution Reaction," J. Am. Chem. Soc. **133**, 7296 (2011).

\[7\] Z. Chen _et al._, "Core-Shell MoO3 Nanowires for Hydrogen Evolution: A Functional Design for Electrocatalytic Materials," Nano Lett. **11**, 4168 (2011).

\[8\] J. D. Benck _et al._, "Amorphous Molybdenum Sulfide Catalysts for Electrochemical Hydrogen Production: Insights Into the Origin of Their Catalytic Activity," ACS Catal. **2**, 1916 (2012).

> Most want to guess food.................
>
> Y'all are too smart for that.

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=149790)
>
> However, it is familiar to most people on my site that we can also split _water_ to produce **hydrogen**. As I mentioned above in a biologic fuel cell, the output voltage is 1.23 V. This voltage is reversible (key point of the tensegrity system), which means that 1.23 V is the _lowest limit voltage_ we need to split water into hydrogen and oxygen. However, the voltage is calculated thermodynamically (statistically), and kinetically (sounds like the matrix no?) , higher voltage is needed depending on the electrode we use (inner mito membrane has 30,000,000 volts DHA and thinness). This 'idea' relates to the catalyst for hydrogen's evolution into life, and in order to explain it clearly, I introduce the Tafel behavior in g electrochemistry briefly. What is that?
>
> [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tafel\_equation](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tafel_equation)
>
> For Hydrogen to evolve or emerge with acid electrolyte, hydrogen evolves from the reaction 2H + aq + 2e → H2(g). The rate of this reaction is tested by the current through the circuit. The relationship between the current and the voltage we apply is called current-overpotential equation: (another name for the Tafel equation)
>
> i = i0 \[C0(0,t)/C0\* e-αnfη - CR(0,t)/CR\*e (1-α)nfη\]
> in which C0 and CR represent the concentrations of the participants, i0 is the exchange current, α stands for the asymmetry coefficient, f=F/RT, and η represents the overpotential. When the overpotential goes to large negative, the left term in the bracket is much larger than the right one, and the equation can be rewritten as i=i0 e-αnfη. The current increases exponentially with overpotential, which is named as Tafel behavior. From this behavior, we can extract two important parameters, exchange current i0 and Tafel slope 1/αnf by fitting the linear relationship between log(i) and η. These two parameters are the criterions for the catalytic activity of catalysts. In order to be the best catalyst for hydrogen to evolve/emerge, it should achieve the largest exchange current and the smallest Tafel slope. And until now, as we now realize, platinum is the unbeatable catalyst for hydrogen evolution which has the exchange current density of 4.5 × 10-4 A/cm2, and the Tafel slope as small as 30 mV/decade. This works in your car..........but not so much in us. We use something else.

BOOM Jack. This morning during my CT I was thinking about why HCG works, and it comes down to the increased Hydrogen bonds and the associated energies Hydrogen creates when HCG increases condensed water at the mitochondria! damn.... you're lighting my brain up today....

There are all kinds of questions about what are the right levels of individual fatty acids, and of course the big question everyone tries to answer is what is the right ratio of Omega-6 to Omega-3. While those are very interesting questions, those are not the questions I was asking.

Jack makes a big deal in some of his podcasts about the unique role of DHA in the cell membrane. It's ability to support ION channels and electron transport gives it a unique and critical role. And because Jack is all about the electron and molecular biochemistry, this is a core issue. What I want to find is a lab that will directly \*measure\* the \*amount of DHA in the cell membrane\*. What most labs do is give you values in your plasma. That probably approximates the membrane value, but if you have a metabolic condition that affects the cell membrane, maybe not. Since DHA in the membrane is what gives you the benefit, DHA in the membrane is what I want to measure.

Apparently I am not the only crazy who has asked this question. This researcher realized the same issue and did a custom measurement of DHA in the cell membrane:
[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2785093/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2785093/)

So, attempting to get this thread back to where it started: does anyone know of a commercial lab that will give a fatty acid profile of your cell membranes, rather than relying on plasma.

[http://www.omegaquant.com/omega-3-index/](http://www.omegaquant.com/omega-3-index/)
**gives:**
**The Omega-3 Index is a measure of omega-3 fatty acids, EPA+DHA, in red blood cells,**

That is at extreme one can have DHA=0(zero) and EPA 4x over the limit
**and the test will tell that there is excess of EPA+DHA.**

Okay, just had an interesting discussion with OmegaQuant. If your intent is to understand how DHA affects electron transport chain, then you need to measure fatty acid composition of the mitochondria, not the outer cell membrane. This then raises the questions:

1) How the heck are you going to get a tissue sample that contains mitochondria, and which tissue do you sample? Short of doing an autopsy, you would need to probably get a biopsy, maybe a muscle biopsy.

2) Then you need to worry about how do you isolate mitochondria in your tissue sample. For sure no commercial lab is doing that. You are in the realm of research project. Would need to look if any researcher has done this.

3) Assuming you solve these problems, how would you interpret the result from a mitochondrial measurement. You would need a baseline value, presumably extracted from research papers. But has any researcher even profiled fatty acid composition of mitochondrial membranes on humans?

The "good news" is OmegaQuant told me "If you can get us an isolated sample of your mitochondrial membranes, we'll measure it for you."

### persistence — 2014-12-17T04:23:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-way-to-measure-dha-in-cell-membranes.12364/post-150380>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=150376)
>
> In the link you posted I see individual fatty acids. GOOD
> but I do not see there recommended ranges for each individual fatty acid. BAD

I think this recommendation is beyond the scope of the report. This is in fact one of the most contentious issues in science. Biochemists will line up and tell you that your omega 6 to omega 3 should be as low as you can get it because of the inflammation and pro cancer effects of omega 6. Clinicians will line up and point to all of the studies that show omega 6 lowering heart disease and ask you to load up on it.

Why would I trust any recommendation that OmegaQuant gives me? It's a point of study and you have to make a decision about what is the right goal. Personally I want Omega-6 to Omega-3 not above 4:1, and you will see recommendations from 9:1 to 2:1.

> On the same page, on left I see:
>
> The Omega-3 Index is a measure of omega-3 fatty acids, EPA+DHA

This is a non issue because they give you every one of the separate 24 fatty acids and you can quickly do your own calculations using any combination of those that fit to the science you want to use.

Out of curiosity, what combination of Omega-3 do you want to use to make an Omega-3 Index?

Keep in mind that research studies simply compare Omega-3 to Omega-6, so if you choose a metric that no one has published research for, what good does it do you?

> On same page there is:
>
> We do not list “normal” values for any fatty acid or ratio except for the Omega-3 Index on the report. This is not an oversight. We are acutely aware that, although there are “average” values for blood fatty acids in America, these are clearly not “optimal” levels. Average American levels of palmitic, stearic, oleic, trans oleic, linoleic, arachidonic, etc. are not necessarily “healthy” levels. At OmegaQuant, we don’t want our clients to aim for a target that is off-target.

There is some confusion here. "Normal" means "normalized". And what they are trying to say is that they use a dried blood drop on a piece of spotter paper to do their standard test. This ends up measuring 50% plasma and 50% RBC membranes, and they are reporting those non-normalized values. For the one line that says "Omega-3 Index" they are doing a calculation to "normalize" the value to what you would get if you had done an RBC membrane test. Notice in their sample how EPA + DHA does NOT mathematically add up to the number in the Omega-3 Index line? That's because they normalized the value.

What I would do when getting this test is send them an EDTA tube, which would then let them spin down and get RBC membranes. I would get a test result where every individual value on the report needs no adjustment to normalize.

> Out of curiosity, what combination of Omega-3 do you want to use to make an Omega-3 Index?

> Keep in mind that research studies simply compare Omega-3 to Omega-6, so if you choose a metric that no one has published research for, what good does it do you?
>
>
>
> There is some confusion here. "Normal" means "normalized". And what they are trying to say is that they use a dried blood drop on a piece of spotter paper to do their standard test. This ends up measuring 50% plasma and 50% RBC membranes, and they are reporting those non-normalized values. For the one line that says "Omega-3 Index" they are doing a calculation to "normalize" the value to what you would get if you had done an RBC membrane test. Notice in their sample how EPA + DHA does NOT mathematically add up to the number in the Omega-3 Index line? That's because they normalized the value.
>
> What I would do when getting this test is send them an EDTA tube, which would then let them spin down and get RBC membranes. I would get a test result where every individual value on the report needs no adjustment to normalize.

I am not interested in their Omega-3 Index. Their index is based on (EPA +DHA).
I will use same argument that you did defending them for not publishing ranges of recommended values or average population values for each fatty acid.

Why would I trust any recommendation that OmegaQuant gives me?
But I also do not like Omega-3 Index from other laboratories.
That include my favorite Laboratory, Spectracell.

\-----------
Like I said before, I prefer to follow dr Patricia Kane.
1-- I like her way.
2-- dr Kruse recomended her to me

..............

.

### persistence — 2014-12-17T06:45:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-way-to-measure-dha-in-cell-membranes.12364/post-150404>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=150384)
>
> I am not interested in their Omega-3 Index. Their index is based on (EPA +DHA).
> I will use same argument that you did defending them for not publishing ranges of recommended values or average population values for each fatty acid.

I want high quality data, taken from the right part of the cell. I'm also not interested in their Omega-3 Index, but just remove that from your thoughts and what is left is pretty interesting because of where they are measuring the values (from the cell membrane and not from plasma).

> Like I said before, I prefer to follow dr Patricia Kane.
> 1-- I like her way.
> 2-- dr Kruse recomended her to me

I did some quick scan of her online, and she looks very interesting. I'm going to start on phospholipids after reading some of their work.

Is this the test you have been using:

[http://www.bodybio-wellness.com/report-fatty-acid.html](http://www.bodybio-wellness.com/report-fatty-acid.html)

Note Dr Kane also has her critics:

[http://articles.chicagotribune.com/...m-autism-recovery-movement-autism-treatment/3](http://articles.chicagotribune.com/2009-11-23/health/chi-autism-science-nov23_1_defeat-autism-autism-recovery-movement-autism-treatment/3)

Her credentials are from a nonaccredited academic institution, and the common criticism I read was that she uses the IV infusions as a way to charge patients huge amounts of money. But I think the idea of supporting phospholipids by oral supplementation is pretty low risk and reasonable cost.

### JanSz — 2014-12-18T05:18:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-way-to-measure-dha-in-cell-membranes.12364/post-150485>

Code:

```
Is this the test you have been using:

http://www.bodybio-wellness.com/report-fatty-acid.html
```

I would like to use this test, but have a problem.
I as private person cannot order this test.
This test have to be ordered by someone who have account with bodybio.
I called bodybio, they have given me few addresses in my area, I called them.
I was told that I can come (and pay for visit), but I will get the test ordered only if professional will make investigation, and as result of that investigation he/she will decide that I need such a test.

It looks to me like a scheme designed for profit.
I am not desperate enough to go that route.

I am able to get script from my doc for fatty acids from Mayo Clinic, that have to do.
\====================================

Code:

```
cell membrane and not from plasma
```

OmegaQuant is using dry blood

Mayo Clinic is using serum that is spinned within 45 minutes of draw.
I think (not sure) that they keep red blood cells and discard plasma, so they test fatty acids in plasma.

At this time I am not able to agree or disagree which one is better, but definitely the results will not tell me anything because OmegaQuant is not providing any values/ranges to compare.

Mayo Clinic does provide ranges for each individual fatty acid that they test.

### Shijin13 — 2014-12-12T21:59:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-and-space-in-our-brain.12367/post-149943>

> [av8r said:](/goto/post?id=149942)
>
> Makes me ponder that maybe verbal language isn’t primordial and only came about as we became more disconnected….

disconnected from what?

I ask this b/c in one of my philosophy classes with a great professor, in fact @[QuantumRadical](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/165/) had the same instructor I did several years earlier, who asked the question regarding the biblical story of the tower of babble - why would god make different languages to disrupt communications between his creations? and If God created us all in his image would his message differ if it were in different languages? Thought provoking discussion followed.

But when I think about disconnection - I don't just think its from the earth, but from the universe at large. the ability to perceive what's going on beneath the current of perceived reality.... the connection with who you are now, and who you were in the past and who you are in the future. no end, no beginning. and constant stream of consciousness regardless of what form you take in matter. consciousness is the energy of the universe.

As Havoc told me one day in the car: "Mommy you're God." To which I replied: "um dude, no I'm not." To which he replied: " Yes, you are. God made you, therefore you are God." my reply back to him: "you know dude, you're pretty smart." His response: "I know"

Energy = God = Us = Energy = Universe = Energy

disconnection is from our true selves, the untapped energy within each and everyone of us.

I would say our idea of a god compared to the ancient civilizations of the world is dramatically different. People think we moved forward as a whole in every area besides health on this website. Ha, omg , ha.

I think we haven't even learned 1% of the knowledge we use to have...... There is evidence of laser semiconductors being used 14,000 years ago. Yea, we are so advanced with our phones and cancer! The great flood took everyone out (different day).

Call me stupid but here it goes. People make huge assumptions with the pyramids. They couldn't be bigger mistakes (different day). We still have many different theories to how they were built. How could we know something's true purpose if we can barely even fathom how they constructed it. Where did they get their gold? How did they have the fractal structure of the whole universe under the paw of many sphinx around the globe. Spend a day plotting the pyramids around the world on the globe you will literally shit yourself. We aren't being told the great ARCANUM because of the worlds powers. There you have it. What's in the Vatican that they couldn't show 7 billon non believers ? Maybe we need to play catch up instead of thinking we are moving forward. Our de evolution has created de coherence to the point that we can't understand the basic fractal laws of the universe. It gets way more fundamental. You can get the speed of light in the Giza pyramids and pi and the golden ratio just by dividing the dimensions. They knew the speed of light down to the Fenta second 10,000 years ago before we did. They matched magnetic north very very accurately. Pyramids around the world line up with Orion's Belt, why? Our idea of being disconnected and the idea of having to come before a god is radically different than our pasts. I don't even believe e=mc^2 is right or a full equation. It's a very brief description of a great observers perspective on reality. I think I know why entangling happens faster than the speed of light and why light must travel through what you call space time but my idea is RADICALLY different. Just because we can google something doesn't make it right.

### NeilBB — 2014-12-14T15:15:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-and-space-in-our-brain.12367/post-150068>

Language is not primordial to life as such. But I would argue that language is fundamental to humanity. I consider it the distinctive feature of normal human cognition. Not only for communication, but for conceptual _cognition_ as well. Language is merely conceptual abstraction, which is the tool ma nature innovated in us, so that we could transcend the immediate perceptual moment in a number of respects.

Sure, it is still possible to be a human without much or even any language ability. From strokes, tumors, trauma, or developmental anomalies, etc. I see these people all the time in my work. Non-verbal functioning is all that is possible to them. But I think if you talk to their families, you will find that this is a massive impairment for almost all of them, not any kind of liberation. (There may be some rare "savant" type exceptions to some extent. True.)

But quite frankly, I think the new age idea of desire to "transcend language" is mostly an excuse for wanting to be freed from the responsibility of understanding epistemology and the deeper meanings of concepts and principles. A significant portion of the expanded human neocortex accommodates symbolic language function and it works pretty damn well. Could we evolve beyond language one day? Sure. But why not try to better appreciate the unique conceptual tool we already have? Very few do...

I totally agree that language is fundamentally to humanity, it’s essential; by primordial I meant could there have been a more efficient means of communication before verbal language came on the scene? I only ask this because of what I observe in nature, schools of fish and flocks of birds moving in total coherence without verbal cues … not that we would move in flocks or schools of coherence  but maybe our brains could be resonate coupled giving an efficient means of instant communication…exactly how that communication would emerge… I am not sure. BTW this is just a thought experiment of mine.

Intra and extra cellular communication in an optimum organism is instantaneous via longitudinal waves, as long as there is resonance coupling through a common medium, I could be wrong, but that’s my understanding. Fractal up to an optimum human coupled to the earth, I then wonder if two or more brains could share instant information if resonant coupled through the same medium.

> Oh yeah, totally agree. I guess it depends on what we mean by more efficient though. I think these instantaneous "quantum" forms of communication are lightening fast, but probably also quite limited in scope, unlike language, so perhaps "efficient," but not "robust" maybe?

Interesting to ponder indeed. I'd posit though, in the same way we can learn better verbal communication (i.e. an infant learning their first words vs. an 'average' adult who is likely well versed in their common language), or considering other facets of our bodies like that we can train our muscles to run further, faster, harder etc, if our form of communication was though a different medium I don't see why we also wouldn't get better at that as well. We are quite elastic, especially the more exposure there is to things at younger ages.

...What if this is really how infants are trying to communicate to us, but due to our disconnection they must resort to crying, gesturing, etc until they fumble their way into our verbal construct?

> [NeilBB said:](/goto/post?id=150080)
>
> Interesting line of thought. I agree that ancient cultures likely had a lot of knowledge that has been lost and that there is always a bias that current humans "are now smarter than those dumb people in the past" that is mostly unjustified. However, to my knowledge, there is not a lot of evidence from the past of these specific kinds of advanced technologies that we have today. Perhaps there were superior but totally different technologies that have been lost. It seems feasible. What evidence of laser semiconductors from 14,000 years ago? Please continue to enlighten us...

I'd agree that a lot of this past knowledge is not easily supported today, and if it is, there is a lot of conjecture... but what if we take this thought experiment one step further, as a hypothetical consideration: Per se you were a society that was communicating instantly/spatially/whatever through non-verbal means of some higher-energy form of communication... would there ever have been a desire or need to document (in writing or otherwise) the information that is instantly and easily accessible to all?

# Thread: FRET/FLEEM?
