I guess the other issue involves the flow of atoms and particles through the body, correct? Atoms and molecules will be constantly turning over (although probably at varying rates in different tissues/circumstances). So some entangled particles will be retained for a while and some lost more quickly? "Motor memory" could utilize this mechanism?

### [email redacted] — 2014-12-19T17:33:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cosmology-evolution-and-time.12402/post-150610>

Neil - I have always been a bit unsure about the "Uncertainty Principle" in one way it can be seen as profound, but in another maybe trivial. Like if we have an inability to do something (like measure location and spin or something of the sort) do we then impute that quality to 'reality'. And that is something I am wary about on some of this 'quantum magic' business. Like something can be 2 places at once or things keep a 'connection' though trillions of light years apart. Really?? Isn't it as much likely to be some 'artifact of our thinking' some limitation of our way of looking at it or something we 'impute' to reality.

On the other hand I do believe QED has discovered a lot of valuable things and that video Jack put on the blog recently of the English physicist showing how quantum actually does apply to biology made things a lot clearer for me. I now can see more why Jack is stressing this (first I thought maybe he was just trying to stress us out lol!!), since life IS that delicate and operates on the smallest and subtlest of scales, it seems to make massive use of these tiny forces that I has assumed only were of interest to physicists then OF COURSE nnEMF is likely to be a huge problem.

The effect of the magnetic field of the earth is very small on an individual charged particle but very very important so OF COURSE fields from electric lines and all the rest of the stuff we are exposed to are hugely to be reckoned with. There is method to his madness I should not have doubted it. At the same time I think there is a danger (I am a good example of this myself) of getting 'over-intellectual' about some of this. It can literally become a 'head trip' and we all seem to disappear in frequencies and waves to the point there is no 'individual' left. That is why for myself at least I keep hold of the 'feeling' aspects of life if I can and I like Janov's notions about pain and memories even if his 'application' seems to lack something

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-20T00:02:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cosmology-evolution-and-time.12402/post-150635>

The magnetic field limits the noise on the surface of this planet.......the sun increases that noise and most life needs that noise to awaken..........but life is structured around darkness and sleep because it requires the stillness of oscillations to touch the quantum world.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-20T00:10:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cosmology-evolution-and-time.12402/post-150637>

> [NeilBB said:](/goto/post?id=150608)
>
> I guess the other issue involves the flow of atoms and particles through the body, correct? Atoms and molecules will be constantly turning over (although probably at varying rates in different tissues/circumstances). So some entangled particles will be retained for a while and some lost more quickly? "Motor memory" could utilize this mechanism?

Atoms flow from the Earth and into all life forms by Onsanger's reciprocity. This means that the flow of life is tied to energy state of the system. If you go back to the EE6 blog.......what did I say about life? It is a collective phenomena. The more energy it sustains, the more its architecture can retain the spark of life. That spark of life is the ability "to drink" from the quantum process pool in the darkness of night recurrently, with a circadian regularity. This is why the Circadian Cycles are massively important to get right before you do anything else. Aging is a slow steady erosion of energy by the addition of neutrons to our proton networks in mitochondria. When we age, we gain atomic mass while simultaneously losing energy.........the more atomic mass we have in our cells, the harder it is to stay on the quantum ledge in sleep to drink from the fountain of youth to recycle our protons to lose their neutron baggage. As atomic mass increase enzymatic fluxes slow. It is really not that hard to understand once you get the basics down.

# Thread: DMT (Dimethyltryptamine), Melatonin & Sleep/Snoring
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dmt-dimethyltryptamine-melatonin-sleep-snoring.12405/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 53
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JC1980 — 2014-12-17T01:05:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dmt-dimethyltryptamine-melatonin-sleep-snoring.12405/post-150341>

Hello All,

I don't know if this is the correct forum or not to post this in. This isn't a drug related discussion persay but just a question I had for all you biohackers out there.

**DMT has the following molecular structure:
**[C](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Carbon)12[H](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hydrogen)16[N](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nitrogen)2****

**Melatonin has the following molecular structure:
[C](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Carbon)13[H](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hydrogen)16[N](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nitrogen)2[O](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oxygen)2**

It appears as though the only difference between DMT (the strongest hallucinogenic in the world) and Melatonin is one Carbon atom and two Oxygen atoms or better known as Carbon Dioxide.

It is generally well known that Melatonin is produced in the pineal gland. It is less well known and theorized that DMT is produced in the pineal gland. There is no blood brain barrier separating the pineal gland from the blood stream and is stated to have profuse blood flow only second to the kidney.

It is well known that your breathing pattern dictates the levels of Carbon Dioxide in the blood. For example, during hyperventilation, your levels of Carbon Dioxide in the blood can fall to extremely low levels known as Hypocapnia. During hypoventilation, your levels of Carbon Dioxide in the blood rise to extremely high levels known as Hypercapnia.

My question is when we sleep, especially in the midst of our peak sleep patterns it would appear to me that we tend to take extremely deep breaths that induces snoring in some people. If we were to lay down while we are fully awake, we wouldn't be inducing this type of deep breathing pattern as it would be deemed unnecessary to us. However, it seems as though it is almost reflexive when we are in deep sleep. Is there a possibility that we are inducing severely low levels of Carbon Dioxide in our blood while we are in deep sleep due to our deep inhale/snoring or a subconscious version of hyperventilation? If so, could that possibly equate to DMT being circulated in our blood stream as well as our brains during this time due to the temporary lack of CO2 in the bloodstream while the pineal gland is producing ****[C](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Carbon)12[H](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hydrogen)16[N](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nitrogen)2**?**

If this is the case, in theory it would be possible for someone to induce DMT secretions without CO2 attachment when fully awake if one where to utilize the same exact methods to induce Melatonin secretion from the pineal gland which is complete, sustained darkness. Couple this with deep breathing/hyperventilation techniques in order to create extremely low levels of CO2 in the blood and instead of Melatonin being produced, it would be DMT instead.

This would mean that your pineal gland produces pure DMT but based on your blood composition has compounds that attach to it's original structure which alter it's affects/capabilities.

It's also interesting that many of the historical and modern mystics claim that the breathe is the gateway to other realms and DMT is known as the "Spirit Molecule".

Just some food for thought... please let me know what you guys think! [http://q4lt.com/dmt-melatonin-miracles](http://q4lt.com/dmt-melatonin-miracles)

We were there in late 1993, in north India for 6 weeks. At that time there were many people in Lucknow who had been with Rajneesh before he died. You must have encountered some yourself. A very expanded group of folks there at the time. After Lucknow we spent 6 weeks in southern India, part of which was in Kerala at Ammachi's ashram, and we ended up in a sleeping cubicle above her Kali temple with energy so powerful that we had to leave after 2 weeks. This whole trip was incredibly transformative, although we ended up back in the states and fell back into the corporate matrix for more than a decade. We've been healing from that contraction for the last 6 years. Discovering Jack's fountain of knowledge and awareness has definitely been a breath of fresh air in this healing process.

I know the whole cast of characters intimately, many are here in Sedona now. The play is greater than I can see and it goes on....one must crystallize and ground along the way....I have found it important to contrast the lives of mystics and sages with their public teachings and to also look for how they worked in "private" with those close to them. If one reads through Swami Rama's works and the accounts of hius life he dictated to his disciples, one will notice that he and Ramana are in the same "tradition" yet the outward manifestation of their "work" was quite different. If one looks at Ramana and Swami Rama's master who was his contemporary, their origins are as different as could be. Yet, they are in the same "tradition".

### [email redacted] — 2014-12-17T17:45:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-and-the-bio-hacking-conference.12406/post-150431>

I remember some time last Winter Dr Kruse did a podcast with Dave Asprey and I even mentioned it at the time how Jack was literally streets ahead of this guy and also I commented on how lost I would feel 'following' someone like Mr Asprey. Reliance on 'supplements' and all kinds of crazy devices to cheat Mother Nature. In that way of course the 'alternative health' scene is really not much better and in the short run at least much less effective that standard medical practice. I suppose it shows the difficulty of being a true innovator one is not on the same wavelength as conventional wisdom but not necessarily in agreement much with the 'alternatives' either. It seems a lot of these 'alternative' guys don't actually have much training in Medicine and if they do they tend then to concentrate on one 'pathway' that they happen to know something about then it becomes all about 'gluthiathone' 'CoQ10' 'clorella' on and on take your pick. It seems to me and maybe seems odd me saying it Dr Kruse sometimes sells himself short by even dealing with these kinds of people. But I understand we all need a 'eco-system' we all need friends and neighbors I suppose is another way of looking at it.

Also I think it can be hard to spot an 'innovator' for example I just saw the bio-pic about Stephen Hawking "The Theory of Everything" and he struck me as strangely 'conventional' and he never changed his way of thinking it was 'black holes all the way down' you might say. It was interesting to me also he never seemed to delve into anything much about the real CAUSES of his disease which was Motor Neuron Disease or Lou Gehrig's disease as it is sometimes called. I thought wouldn't it have been fascinating if he had turned his attention to that but if he did very likely would have get caught up in more conventional thinking about 'genetics' and so forth.

### Josh — 2014-12-17T19:47:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-and-the-bio-hacking-conference.12406/post-150437>

> When Bodhidharma reached China, Emperor Wu had come to the border to receive him
> because his name and his fragrance had reached far ahead of him. Bodhidharma, in the
> tradition of Gautam Buddha, is one of the rarest flowers... even Gautam Buddha may
> sometimes feel jealous of him, although Bodhidharma is his disciple.
> Bodhidharma came with one shoe on his foot and another shoe on his head. Emperor Wu
> could not trust his eyes, and could not trust... he has heard so much about the man, and he
> seems to be absolutely mad. But he was a man of very great culture, etiquette; he was an
> emperor. He tried to avoid seeing that shoe on the head. It was not right to enquire about
> it, but the temptation was becoming greater and greater... what is the matter? He talked
> about God, and he talked about truth, but all the time inside he was thinking about the
> shoe.
> Finally Bodhidharma said, "Don't ask unnecessary questions; ask the necessary question.
> I can see a shoe in your heart. You cannot hide from me. My eyes are almost capable of
> penetrating into the thickest skull: \`Why are you keeping that shoe on your head'?"
> The emperor was amazed: This is too much. This man seems to be either drunk or mad,
> but certainly he has a method in his madness. He is carrying the shoe on his head.... But
> he is not wrong -- I am suppressing the shoe in my heart, in my mind; my whole being
> wants to ask only one question: "Why are you carrying this shoe?" And when he is
> insisting, "Ask the real question," it is better to ask it; otherwise, I will not be able to
> sleep.
> He said, "Forgive me, it is very embarrassing."
> Bodhidharma said, "Nothing is embarrassing. You simply ask."
> The emperor said, "I am trying to avoid that question, and just to avoid it I'm asking all
> other questions -- I don't mean anything. But you seem to be a strange fellow; you have
> caught me red-handed. I have not asked, but you have heard the question: "Why are you
> carrying the shoe on your head?"
> Bodhidharma said, "Now we can talk. Now you are being simple, innocent -- now you
> are not repressing. I am carrying this shoe on my head so that you can ask a real question.
> Now there is no need...." He removed the shoe, put it on his foot, and he said, "Now you
> have to understand: start from the very beginning; don't start asking questions about God.
> You seem to be a born shoemaker."
> The emperor was very angry and shocked. Obviously this man was making fun of him,
> but he still tried to hide his embarrassment. His whole court was present, and they were
> all trying to hide their laughter. He asked, "Who are you?" -- because that is one of the
> most significant and most ancient, spiritual questions.
> Bodhidharma said, "As far as I am concerned, I don't know. Do you know who you are?"
> The emperor said, "This is something I have come to enquire from you because I don't
> know."
> Bodhidharma said, "You are ignorant; I also don't know, but I am innocent. Just now, go
> back. Sleep will not be possible because you will have to figure out why you are ignorant
> and I am innocent -- and for the same reason."
> The emperor returned home very puzzled. The man had a charisma: he could not forget
> his eyes, he could not forget his presence. He could not sleep the whole night; he could
> not make the distinction how one person saying, "I don't know" is ignorant and another
> person saying, "I don't know" is innocent.
> He came back again. Bodhidharma said, "You are knowledgeable; you have never
> recognized the fact that you don't know yourself -- you have always believed that you
> know. At least, you have pretended to the world that you know. It is out of ignorance.
> "I have searched deeply into myself. I don't know because there is nobody to be known. I
> have found the house empty -- a pure nothingness, a sky without clouds. There is no
> knower, there is nothing to be known.
> "You are saying it out of ignorance, because you still think you are, but you don't know
> who you are. I am saying it out of innocence, because I know there is no one to be
> known, to be a knower. It is just pure silence."

Osho, "The Golden Future", Chapter 27

**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### persistence — 2014-12-17T07:14:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-soy-source-for-phosphatidylcholine.12408/post-150406>

I have a problem digesting soy. Is there a non-soy (sunflower?) source for phospholipids such as phosphatidylcholine?

### Kristi Lambert — 2014-12-17T14:30:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-soy-source-for-phosphatidylcholine.12408/post-150418>

I used to use this in my raw food days: [https://www.mysunflowerlecithin.com/product/100-organic-liquid-sunflower-lecithin/](https://www.mysunflowerlecithin.com/product/100-organic-liquid-sunflower-lecithin/)

### persistence — 2014-12-17T14:56:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-soy-source-for-phosphatidylcholine.12408/post-150421>

> [Kristi Lambert said:](/goto/post?id=150418)
>
> I used to use this in my raw food days: [https://www.mysunflowerlecithin.com/product/100-organic-liquid-sunflower-lecithin/](https://www.mysunflowerlecithin.com/product/100-organic-liquid-sunflower-lecithin/)

Nonono!!! Placing a phospholipid into a fat base DISABLES ITS BIOAVAILABILITY!!!! This is one of the biggest scams in nutrition. The large industrial seed companies all convinced the regulators to allow them to take their engineered lecithin formulas and put 30% phospholipids into those and then call the resulting goo a "phosphatidylcholine". But it isn't bioavailable! It's just lecithin. Yes, it contains phospholipids, but your body cannot incorporate them into cell structure because of how they were packaged. They conveniently forget to tell you that on the product label. To my thinking, it is an obscene violation of consumer trust.

Here is a great video, and I am pointing you into the part of the video that explains how this scam works:

If you want bioavailable phospholipids, you need to buy a product where those have been extracted out of the lecithin, and packaged alone, not in a fat base. BodyBio makes a great one, but theirs is from soy. I found this one on Amazon that looks good from sunflower lecithin:

[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00DILLV0G](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00DILLV0G)

### persistence — 2014-12-17T23:43:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-soy-source-for-phosphatidylcholine.12408/post-150458>

> [SeaHorse said:](/goto/post?id=150423)
>
> Pure Encapsulations has a phosphatidylserine that is sunflower derived, but as for a choline supplement….why not eat eggs and sardines?

To be clear, we don't want the choline. We want phosphatidylcholine, which is directly used to build cell membranes.

If you are fighting a metabolic disease that attacks cell membrane structure, there aren't enough hours in a day to eat all of the whole foods needed to supply enough bioavailable phospholipids.

I have noticed that most all encompassing phospholipids include just three out of the four on the label and leave out phosphatidylserine. Then you see other supplements that offer just phosphatidylserine. Any idea why no one puts it all together in one supplement?

### Kristi Lambert — 2014-12-18T02:34:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-soy-source-for-phosphatidylcholine.12408/post-150467>

These guys say they remove the soy components: [http://www.thepowermall.com/thecenterforhealth/healthy\_bodies/phosphatidyl\_choline.htm](http://www.thepowermall.com/thecenterforhealth/healthy_bodies/phosphatidyl_choline.htm)

### persistence — 2014-12-18T02:59:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-soy-source-for-phosphatidylcholine.12408/post-150474>

> [Kristi Lambert said:](/goto/post?id=150467)
>
> These guys say they remove the soy components: [http://www.thepowermall.com/thecenterforhealth/healthy\_bodies/phosphatidyl\_choline.htm](http://www.thepowermall.com/thecenterforhealth/healthy_bodies/phosphatidyl_choline.htm)

This is soy lecithin, not isolated phospholipids. It's all there in the ingredients list:

Each 1 teaspoon (3.3 grams) serving contains:
• Soy Lecithin 3,333 mg
• Phosphatidyl Choline 1,333 mg

The phosphatidylcholine in this product will not be bioavailable to reconstruct cell membranes because of this packaging.

# Thread: Clever Product For Determining Mineral Needs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clever-product-for-determining-mineral-needs.12409/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### persistence — 2014-12-17T12:44:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clever-product-for-determining-mineral-needs.12409/post-150416>

This has got to be one of the cleverest products I have ever seen:

[http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/BodyBioBulletin-LiquidMinerals.pdf](http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/BodyBioBulletin-LiquidMinerals.pdf)

They provide eight minerals in homeopathic doses and in liquid form, and they are relying on the fact that if your body needs a mineral it will taste sweet, and if your body has too much the taste will turn bitter. The same mineral in the same person will taste sweet or bitter based on how much your body needs and has absorbed.

The beauty of this testing is that you can back off on minerals that you are taking too much of, and you can up your doses on minerals your body is not absorbing. Backing off of overdoses is at least as important as increasing intake of minerals you don't absorb. Minerals often compete against each other and affect critical enzyme systems, so getting a healthy balance is important.

I'm totally sold on the idea for this product. Test once a week, and keep adjusting dosage until you satisfy the body's need and get a neutral flavor during the test.

Now if only we had reliable home-based testing for other nutrients. Half of the stuff we are taking is probably killing us and we don't know it because we don't know what to measure.

### fitness@home — 2014-12-17T14:54:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clever-product-for-determining-mineral-needs.12409/post-150420>

Interesting. Thanks for posting

### Atif — 2015-02-23T12:05:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-link-to-the-discussions-for-each-blog-post-would-be-helpful-for-me.12411/post-156364>

According to an ancient legend, there was a time when ordinary people had access to all the knowledge of the gods. Yet time and again, they ignored this wisdom. One day, the gods grew tired of so freely giving a gift the people didn’t use, so they decided to hide this precious wisdom where only the most committed of seekers would discover it. They believed that if people had to work to find this wisdom, they would use it more carefully. One of the gods suggested that they bury it deep in the earth. No, the others said— too many people could easily dig down and find it. “Let’s put it in the deepest ocean,” suggested one of the gods, but that idea was also rejected. They knew that people would one day learn to dive and thus would find it too easily. One of the gods suggested hiding it on the highest mountaintop, but it was quickly agreed that people could climb mountains. Finally, one of the wisest gods suggested, “Let’s hide it deep inside the people themselves. They’ll never think to look in there.” And so it came to be— and so it continues today.

# Thread: How to Turn Off Email Notifications as DEFAULT Behavior?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-turn-off-email-notifications-as-default-behavior.12412/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### persistence — 2014-12-18T03:19:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-turn-off-email-notifications-as-default-behavior.12412/post-150477>

How do I turn off email notifications of new messages in Watched Threads?

I have seen this software used elsewhere, and how it is normally done is that you select "Watched Threads" and then "Show All Watched Threads" and then there is a pull down menu where you disable email notifications globally.

Someone here changed that default behavior. In this forum there is a pull down at the bottom of the all threads list
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?watched/threads/all](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?watched/threads/all)
that says:
"With selected
...
Disable email notifications"

The implication appears to be that only the specific threads you select will stop sending email notifications.

Is there a way to turn this off globally, for future posts as well as current ones? It's way too much hassle to keep resetting each thread manually.

### Lahelada — 2014-12-18T11:20:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-turn-off-email-notifications-as-default-behavior.12412/post-150500>

Click on your name in the top right hand corner or thereabouts. A menu comes up . Select "alert settings" and voilá.

### persistence — 2014-12-18T15:38:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-turn-off-email-notifications-as-default-behavior.12412/post-150520>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=150500)
>
> Click on your name in the top right hand corner or thereabouts. A menu comes up . Select "alert settings" and voilá.

Did you mean "Alert Preferences"? That controls what notifications you receive when you are online, and I DO want to receive most of those when I am online.

Nothing on that page selectively controls receipt of notifications to email.

### angie-s — 2014-12-18T04:57:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-serum-leptin-going-up-while-on-leptin-reset-protocol.12413/post-150484>

I have been diligently following the Leptin Reset Protocol for 3 months. I have lost 15 lbs, reduced HS-CRP to 0.9 from 1.4 reduced hgA1c to 5.8 from 6.9 but my Leptin has increased more than 4 times **( 206 now compared to 48 before the protocol)**. This is going in the wrong direction. WHAT IS WRONG? Adiponectin has also gone down to 6 from 14. That is also going in wrong direction. NEED HELP/ADVICE.

All other items are good, Omega 6/Omega 3 ratio is 2.5 (was 5 before), HS-Omega 3 index is 8.4 (up from 4.6). Vit D is 72 (up from 30 before). TSH is 1.84 (from 3.89). Rev T3 is 12.8, IL-6 is 2.2. sdLDL is down to 18 from 33.

I am a T2D. I have reduced the insulin injections by 60% on the protocol while keeping fasting glucose below 90. That is good also. What do I do about Leptin??

Thanks in advance for your help.

### Shijin13 — 2015-01-12T19:59:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-serum-leptin-going-up-while-on-leptin-reset-protocol.12413/post-152567>

@[angie-s](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/12029/) Just getting back on line, after a major push at work. I'm not as active as I used to be on the forum due to demands of life, my current biohacks, and work which seems to get int the way of doing what I want!

It can take btwn 18-24months to get into the PPP. some of us who have PCOS, or T2D (DM) have a harder time getting into it b/c we're not able to leverage NAD+ and Cytochrome 2.

You really need to focus on Seafood based keto diet. You also NEED TO FIRE YOUR DOC. He/She doesn't know jack about hormones. If you're in Houston - leverage Dawn's (fitness@home) BHRT Doc. If you're not - consider DzLogic with Dr. Serge Dzugan or Centigenics if you can afford it.
You also need to get on a daily Cold Thermogenesis (CT) routine. start with Face dunks, then move too full body soaks. it will help you immensely.

Reversing PCOS or T2D or any other disease doesn't happen over night it takes YEARS! I speak from experience. I've been at biohacking since 2000, and I'm slowly refining my efforts.

Need you to answer some environmental questions - that will help me and others here on the forum help you.

What is your work environment like?
What is your home environment like?

Wifi networks, cell phone towers, powerlines, types of trees, how much outdoor time do you get, what is your sleep like, are you groggy in the morning, when do you go to bed, do you use artificial light at night, do you have fluoresent lighting at home/work, what does your meal timing look like, what is your stress like, how do you deal with stress, what are your relationships like, how's your sex life?

these questions will help us help you.

There are no electronics in my bedroom except the alarm clock. We turn the router and printer off at night (which is in the study not in the B/R). No smart meters by the bed room. No cell towers or power lines close by. I go to bed typically at 10:30 p.m and wake up at 5:30 a.m to go to work.
>

>

I could go out in very cold weather with just a t-shirt and feel good but now I have started feeling more cold and cant seem to take the cold weather as well. My husband think it is the fat I have lost.
>
> I am supplementing with Vit D3, Vitamin K2, B12, Vit C, Calcium, prescription grade fish oil, Co-Q10, PQQ, curcumin and biotin. I was also using 7-keto for but it seems to be causing my hair to fall so stopped last week.
>
> My mother is 83, has aboslutely no ailments (other than an occasional cold). Can eat anything without any stomach issues. Does not go to the doctors or take any medication. Still walks around quite well and does not even wear glasses (other than reading). watches TV 24/7, yet sleeps like a log. I was born when she was 17 so hopefully inherited a good mitochondria from her. My grandmonther also lived till 84 and had no major ailments. There is no T2D in the family.
>
> Sorry for the long rhetoric. I am hoping people here can guide me towards the right track. I am determined to reverse my diabetes. I will make appointment with Dawn's BHRT doc and will redo the labs in 90 days.

### angie-s — 2015-01-12T20:53:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-serum-leptin-going-up-while-on-leptin-reset-protocol.12413/post-152575>

Shijin - Thanks for taking the time to respond. I truly appreciate it.

I cam across Dr. K's blog and leptin rest protocol in September 2014. Since mid sept I have been following the protocol diligently. I eat sea food at all 3 meals. Do CT 3 times a week on upper body, use blue blocker glasses in the evening and have f.lux on my computer. Did the labs before starting and then in mid Dec (posted results in my Journal). Although directionally I have improved, cut down significantly on insulin (from 175 units daily to 40-50 units max), and lost around 15 lbs, I feel I have stalled for last 3 weeks and seem to be getting no improvement now.

Nothing has really changed in my environment. At work I am hard wired to the network but I am sure there is EMF around. I spend a fair amount of time in front of the computer. At home I minimize EMF but do read the optimal health blog and do my google research on health in the evening. I am not much of an i-phone user, don't watch TV or use ipad. There are no electronics in my bedroom except the alarm clock. We turn the router and printer off at night (which is in the study not in the B/R). No smart meters by the bed room. No cell towers or power lines close by. I go to bed typically at 10:30 p.m and wake up at 5:30 a.m to go to work.

I don't get to spend a lot of time outdoors due to the rainy weather these days but try my best to get enough sun and love gardening outside. I do not have issue with morning grogginess and/or lethargy. I typically have a lot of energy and am always on the go.

I have always been a very heavy water drinker. The water is either RO or spring water (bottled). In the past I drank a lot of water but rarely peed. Since the leptin rest it seems like I am not as heavy on water but still drink about 8 bottles a day- perhaps don't get as thirsty.

Another thing I noticed, before reset I was fairly cold adapted. Since I use to get skin rashes for no apparent reason so I use to always wrap myself in ice for long periods because it helped. I could go out in very cold weather with just a t-shirt and feel good but now I have started feeling more cold and cant seem to take the cold weather as well. My husband think it is the fat I have lost.

I am supplementing with Vit D3, Vitamin K2, B12, Vit C, Calcium, prescription grade fish oil, Co-Q10, PQQ, curcumin and biotin. I was also using 7-keto for but it seems to be causing my hair to fall so stopped last week.

My mother is 83, has aboslutely no ailments (other than an occasional cold). Can eat anything without any stomach issues. Does not go to the doctors or take any medication. Still walks around quite well and does not even wear glasses (other than reading). watches TV 24/7, yet sleeps like a log. I was born when she was 17 so hopefully inherited a good mitochondria from her. My grandmonther also lived till 84 and had no major ailments. There is no T2D in the family.

Sorry for the long rhetoric. I am hoping people here can guide me towards the right track. I am determined to reverse my diabetes. I will make appointment with Dawn's BHRT doc and will redo the labs in 90 days.

### Shijin13 — 2015-01-12T22:58:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-serum-leptin-going-up-while-on-leptin-reset-protocol.12413/post-152591>

(1)**

At home I minimize EMF but do read the optimal health blog and do my google research on health in the evening. I am not much of an i-phone user, don't watch TV or use ipad. There are no electronics in my bedroom except the alarm clock. We turn the router and printer off at night (which is in the study not in the B/R). No smart meters by the bed room. No cell towers or power lines close by. I go to bed typically at 10:30 p.m and wake up at 5:30 a.m to go to work.
>
> **(3)** I don't get to spend a lot of time outdoors due to the rainy weather these days but try my best to get enough sun and love gardening outside. I do not have issue with morning grogginess and/or lethargy. I typically have a lot of energy and am always on the go.
>
> **(4)** I have always been a very heavy water drinker. The water is either RO or spring water (bottled). In the past I drank a lot of water but rarely peed. Since the leptin rest it seems like I am not as heavy on water but still drink about 8 bottles a day- perhaps don't get as thirsty.
>
> **(5)** Another thing I noticed, before reset I was fairly cold adapted. Since I use to get skin rashes for no apparent reason so I use to always wrap myself in ice for long periods because it helped. I could go out in very cold weather with just a t-shirt and feel good but now I have started feeling more cold and cant seem to take the cold weather as well. My husband think it is the fat I have lost.
>
> I am supplementing with Vit D3, Vitamin K2, B12, Vit C, Calcium, **(5) prescription grade fish oil,** Co-Q10, PQQ, curcumin and biotin. I was also using 7-keto for but it seems to be causing my hair to fall so stopped last week.
>
> My mother is 83, has aboslutely no ailments (other than an occasional cold). Can eat anything without any stomach issues. Does not go to the doctors or take any medication. Still walks around quite well and does not even wear glasses (other than reading). watches TV 24/7, yet sleeps like a log. I was born when she was 17 so hopefully inherited a good mitochondria from her. My grandmonther also lived till 84 and had no major ailments. There is no T2D in the family.
>
> Sorry for the long rhetoric. I am hoping people here can guide me towards the right track. I am determined to reverse my diabetes. I will make appointment with Dawn's BHRT doc and will redo the labs in 90 days.

**(1)** Progress in improving your health is never a straight line. You'll see forward progress, then "stalls", and backwards progress. This is how the body works. Your body has lost weigh, and you've reduced insulin supplementation quickly in 3 months time. Your body will take time to adjust to this rapid change. It is also in the process of changing how your body repairs itself. Give it time. Before I started on the post Leptin Reset - I followed the Leptin Reset protocol for almost 2 years. This isn't a sprint, or a marathon. This is the ultimate endurance race, the end result is your health. It won't happen over night, nor will it happen the way you think it should or want it too.

**(2)** Something in your environment has changed. You started this in September. the Light cycle is different now than it was in Sept, Oct & Nov. You should be activating the signals that leverage Cold, not light. Using the computer at night, even with blue blocking glasses and i.flux on will trip the incorrect seasonal signals. Are you watching the sunrise in the morning and sunset in the evening? If not. you need to do that ASAP.

Have you tested your house with an Alpha UHS meter to test the ELE and VLE EMF? I was in denial that my house was the culprit, and blamed everything on my work environment (it contributed a lot of negative environmental influences). My house sucked. it took us almost 2 years to position ourselves for the move we made last summer. You'd be surprised what changes when the "powers that be" put up an extra cell tower or antenna in your area.

**(3)** This is the suck and beauty about winter. Sometimes you just gotta go out and walk barefoot in the rain. or sit out on the grass/cement porch and watch the sun rise while having coffee or tea.

**(4)** Keep drinking the water. as you get into a seafood based ketosis and you CT more regularly you'll find you need more water... You're not supposed to get thirst. If you get thirsty it's your body's last signal that your dehydrated....

**(4)** This is hormonal, linked to leptin and your Progesterone levels. You're Estrogen dominate, which makes you cold adverse. to beat E, you've got to get the CT in every single day. Trust me. As much as the cold sucks you've got to do it daily to bring down E and bring up Pg. You're also going to need to get your Progesterone levels fixed. You've got a serious challenge given your hysterectomy, but it can be done.

**(5)** Have you tested your O6:O3 levels with a HDL Lab, a Berkley or another O6:O3 test like the ones Genova have? If not you should. @@Jack Kruse doesn't recommend fish oil b/c it doesn't leverage the Sn2 position. You need seafood to do that. If you're eating seafood at all 3 meals, there's a good chance your spending a Shit Ton of $$$$$$$ on Lenova or other Rxgrade fish oil, that'sot doing anything. This is way testing your O6:O3 levels is important.

This is a journey. It takes a while to reverse disease and your body goes about it in its own way, and fixes the things that will be most beneficial for the entire system first, not necessarily the things we want it to fix first.

### angie-s — 2015-01-13T01:44:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-serum-leptin-going-up-while-on-leptin-reset-protocol.12413/post-152602>

Shijin - Thanks for the guidance, I will follow up on everything you have recommended.

My omega 6/Omega 3 ratio is **2.62** (down from 5.2)
HS-Omega 3 Index (RBC EPA+DHA) is **8.4** (up from 4.6)
HS-CRP is **0.9** (down from 1.4)

# Thread: Distance to Wifi Router
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/distance-to-wifi-router.12416/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### persistence — 2014-12-18T08:58:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/distance-to-wifi-router.12416/post-150493>

If you are forced to live with a Wifi router in your home, turned on 24 hours a day, is there a recommendation about the minimum distance that this device should be from where you sit and use the computer?

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-12-21T01:41:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/distance-to-wifi-router.12416/post-150707>

as far as humanly possible imo

### persistence — 2014-12-21T02:30:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/distance-to-wifi-router.12416/post-150709>

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=150707)
>
> as far as humanly possible imo

That's really escaping the question. The core issue is what is the distance under which you would receive some acute dose of EMF, enough to cause even 5% dysregulation of your normal.

Remember even if I turn my wifi off, I am still being inundated by wifi from all of my neighbors. At some point you have to commit to a specific recommendation or the idea just becomes some amorphous and not actionable concept.

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-12-21T03:41:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/distance-to-wifi-router.12416/post-150716>

i cant give you the answer you want.

### Da-mo — 2014-12-21T04:17:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/distance-to-wifi-router.12416/post-150717>

Here's an answer, but you need to understand the question and the only way to know for sure is to test.

> **B14. What levels are considered safe?**
>
> RF/microwaves in the home vary greatly, usually depending on the use of cell phones, cordless phones, wireless and Wi-Fi. Average levels vary from 0.01 to 0.5 microwatts per centimeter squared (μW/cm²). **We generally use a precautionary level of 0.1 μW/cm² as suggested by the Bioinitiative Report, and 0.001 or less for sensitive individuals**. [http://www.emfcenter.com/emffaqs.htm#B10](http://www.emfcenter.com/emffaqs.htm#B10).

### persistence — 2014-12-22T03:27:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/distance-to-wifi-router.12416/post-150767>

> [Da-mo said:](/goto/post?id=150717)
>
> Here's an answer, but you need to understand the question and the only way to know for sure is to test.

Thanks for that. I found a great article through Google:

[http://www.emfwise.com/distance.php](http://www.emfwise.com/distance.php)

Based on this, for a wifi router you would need 48 meters (!) to get to the Salzburg 2002 limit. But - more pragmatically - if you can put just 6 meters (about 20 feet) between you and the router you are getting rid of most of the EMF.

Reading this article I referenced above is like an advertisement for Jack. It's terrifying the amount of EMF we are being exposed to. Look how far you need to be from a cell phone tower to meet the Salzburg 2002 limit. No one living in a US suburb is more than 3 km from more than one cell phone tower.

### persistence — 2014-12-22T03:30:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/distance-to-wifi-router.12416/post-150768>

> [Da-mo said:](/goto/post?id=150717)
>
> Here's an answer, but you need to understand the question and the only way to know for sure is to test.

Which EMF meters would you recommend for a home user? Do you think it is important to have it be digital?

### Da-mo — 2014-12-22T04:45:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/distance-to-wifi-router.12416/post-150772>

### Jude — 2014-12-22T05:55:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/distance-to-wifi-router.12416/post-150776>

> [persistence said:](/goto/post?id=150493)
>
> If you are forced to live with a Wifi router in your home, turned on 24 hours a day, is there a recommendation about the minimum distance that this device should be from where you sit and use the computer?

Kinda like our problem...only way we can get internet...so have the modem+cradle 8meters away in outside garage with trees in between it and house, and a long ethernet cable to the house. Fortunately no neighbours and we turn ours off when not in use. Cheap way to protect your sleep could be to paper your bedroom walls with aluminium foil?

# Thread: Seems like modern science is catching my drift: Spintronics
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seems-like-modern-science-is-catching-my-drift-spintronics.12418/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-18T15:29:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seems-like-modern-science-is-catching-my-drift-spintronics.12418/post-150515>

Multiferroics are materials in which unique combinations of electric and magnetic properties can simultaneously coexist. Researchers have shown that an electric field can reverse magnetization direction in a spintronic device at room temperature.

[http://newscenter.lbl.gov/2014/12/17/switching-to-spintronics/](http://newscenter.lbl.gov/2014/12/17/switching-to-spintronics/)

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-18T15:29:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seems-like-modern-science-is-catching-my-drift-spintronics.12418/post-150516>

# Thread: Sudden appearance of spider veins? CT-related?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sudden-appearance-of-spider-veins-ct-related.12419/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hansen Kenimer — 2014-12-18T18:21:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sudden-appearance-of-spider-veins-ct-related.12419/post-150531>

They are suddenly appearing en mass on my butt! And one on the outside of my right calf. The only thing I've changed recently is more CT (from a half hour once a week to thrice-weekly hour-long sessions with ice added the past 2 sessions to my torso) and the substitution of DHEA for my wild yam. I am tolerating my CT increasingly well, never shivering. No more issues with the weird crampy/pumped feeling had had that one time. I'm feeling great, sleeping great (9pm to 4am) never waking, REM is good, eating keto epi-paleo. I cannot imagine why in the world these are popping up now unless they are somehow related to the CT!

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-18T19:20:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sudden-appearance-of-spider-veins-ct-related.12419/post-150536>

increase cortisol and loss of tensegrity.

### Hansen Kenimer — 2014-12-18T19:32:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sudden-appearance-of-spider-veins-ct-related.12419/post-150537>

Why would this be happening so suddenly? Nothing has really changed.

### Hansen Kenimer — 2014-12-18T22:27:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sudden-appearance-of-spider-veins-ct-related.12419/post-150554>

It occurred to me that maybe these are older and I'm just noticed them. Maybe having been in the cold water for an hour makes them more noticeable? They were VERY blue. I have a lot more on my right glute than my left, and I had a very bad fall off a horse in January of 2011 in which I fell on that hip/glute. Oh, and I had a horse kick me full-force on that side too in 2008. Maybe it's just from old traumatic injuries? All I know is cold water is supposed to be great for spider veins, so they can only benefit.

### Da-mo — 2014-12-18T22:52:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sudden-appearance-of-spider-veins-ct-related.12419/post-150559>

Are they just showing up as a result of reduced subcutaneous fat?

My veins show more whenever I get my body fat levels low enough.

### Hansen Kenimer — 2014-12-19T17:28:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sudden-appearance-of-spider-veins-ct-related.12419/post-150609>

> [Da-mo said:](/goto/post?id=150559)
>
> Are they just showing up as a result of reduced subcutaneous fat?
>
> My veins show more whenever I get my body fat levels low enough.

I think you might be right. But I do think they were preexisting. I'm just losing some subcutaneous fat and now they are showing up more, especially after CT due to the color of my skin. I could see my ab veins today, too. Very blue, once again. However, I didn't see quite as many spider veins on my right hip/glute, so I think the CT is having a pretty quick effect!

I was watching [a documentary about the weather](http://www.bbc.co.uk/iplayer/episode/b04vr2p4/wild-weather-with-richard-hammond-3-temperature-the-driving-force) a few nights ago and they were talking about how sand from the Sahara can travel as far as the UK. They gave a demonstration of a ping pong ball and tennis ball being dropped at the same time and the kinetic energy from the tennis ball was transferred to the ping pong ball making it bounce way higher than it would normally. The more balls you use the bigger the effect. It made me wonder if this could happen on a sub-atomic level with kinetic energy being passed between protons, neutrons and electrons.

Here's an example of what I'm talking about...

# Thread: Q'Rad Randy's Journal--Journey for a 1 year bio-hack!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/qrad-randys-journal-journey-for-a-1-year-bio-hack.12438/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### QuantumRadical — 2014-12-22T05:18:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/qrad-randys-journal-journey-for-a-1-year-bio-hack.12438/post-150774>

Hi, gang! Well, it is about time I did a little bio-hacking myself. I have lived the SAD life over the last couple of months to intentionally get near to a point in health I was a few years ago--before I discovered Jack's Blogs. What do I want to get out of this? With a disciplined but fun approach to health and wellness, and following Dr. Kruse's protocols to the best of my ability, what types of changes and how much change can I, in my own N=1 bio-hack, experience over the space of a year. I am not trying to isolate any single parameter--analogously living the life of the monk without the Ferrari, but I will still drive my A-6!

I will be taking a robust/comprehensive blood analysis on December 26th this year (2014) and then again at 6 month intervals. If Nature wills, I will have nearly 6 months of results on Jack's Kruse \[sic\] . I will post my initial conditions here as soon as I get the results back. If Jack let's me have 15 minutes of fame on the cruise, I can do a summary debriefing on my first 6 months of the bio-hack. I will be journaling everything I can think of without making that activity too stressful--maybe just weekly entries here. I will also take photos of myself before, during, and after at regular intervals to see any visible physique/body composition changes (not necessarily for publication here but,... I might think about it). I will post some initial conditions on the day after they take my blood (26th), but for starters, I am 61.5 years old.

I think I will also go get a telomere test sometime in January, with a final measure next December. Not sure if I should get results midway. I don't know if the tests are accurate enough to make a difference after 6 months--each result may be inside the error bar of the other. I could use some advice here.

I do have 23andMe results from three years ago which I may post here along the way. I also have previous blood results which I may also post just for information and possible comparison to this bio-hack.

Some highlights of what I will track are:
CT events
Alcohol consumption
Coffee consumption
Bowel movements
OMs (or oxy moments, but no details! ), maybe!
Exercise
Food--seafood, oysters, junk, etc. Type and quality of food.
nnEMF exposure, or not.
Exposure to the sun
Sleep quality/hours per day (start time, finish time)
BAB or not
Water consumption
Weight/weight loss
Use of blue-light blockers
Use of IR nasal treatment. I have one of those VieLight 810s and will keep track of usage.

I am sure I will have a few more items. Just thought of one: focusing on posture--no slouching. If you can think of additional parameters to track or think about, please let me know.

Anyway, I am looking forward to journaling my journey--maybe even write a book about it. I expect this group to keep my spirits up as I go hardcore. Just kidding! I know this will make me feel better and healthier with blood testing results to prove it. Just thinking about doing this already lifts my spirits, and I know you hardcore Krusers will keep me in the game!

I welcome any and all constructive comments and suggestions. Let the games begin!!

Merry Christmas and a healthy and happy New Year.

Q'Rad Randy

> I will also go get a telomere test sometime in January, with a final measure next December. Not sure if I should get results midway. I don't know if the tests are accurate enough to make a difference after 6 months--each result may be inside the error bar of the other. I could use some advice here.

There is a company based in Spain that offers a newer technology Telomere testing. My doctor told me about it last week, when I emailed them about getting a test done (have always been curious). Supposed to better than Spectra cell's test which is just the average telomere length.

I have the info at work on availability in US and will post it later, in case you are interested.

Edited to post this:

[http://www.lifelength.com/index-eng2.html](http://www.lifelength.com/index-eng2.html)

Hello Ms. Clark,

Thank you for your interest in Life Length’s Telomere Analysis Technology. Your email inquiry was forwarded to me as I cover Texas.

I would like to answer your questions as best I can.

1.  The physician cost of a TAT test is typically $589. This does not include ancillary fees such as blood draw and your doctor’s time.

2.  If your doctor’s office draws blood, they can certainly draw a TAT test kit. If they do not, perhaps see if they can recommend an outside draw station.

If your doctor is not already in our database, I can assist with opening an account so a kit can be sent to you. Please let me know how I can be of assistance.

In health,
Micah

**Micah Weathers**
Western Regional Sales Manager
[[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted])

_**Quite pricey compared to the Spectracell test I can get at my doctor's for $200, but it is a more detailed test and report.**_

### QuantumRadical — 2014-12-27T21:25:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/qrad-randys-journal-journey-for-a-1-year-bio-hack.12438/post-151068>

> [Valerie said:](/goto/post?id=151065)
>
> What is your experience with the vialight 810 to date?

I have used it a few times. Nothing negative. I am sure it works over the long term, but things change at biological rates--weeks to months, so I have no expectation of noticing anything immediate. In fact, it is very likely that any changes will be subtle (in the big mix of things) and unnoticeable, except in the bigger picture of lab results over time.

The via light is easy to use. I am trying to make it a routine at night. Read a little before going to sleep with the thing stuck in my nose.  My family thinks I am crazy, but I intend to prove it via the bio-hack and real lab measurements. Vie light is just one of the many things that I will be using to get more toward optimal.

Valerie, does this help? I am buying into the electrons, light, magnetism, and water stuff that Jack talks about. Certainly doesn't hurt my head--actually makes sense if you have any understanding of quantum physics. The vie light at the correct light frequency makes sense. We shall see.

Happy New Year!

### QuantumRadical — 2014-12-28T19:34:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/qrad-randys-journal-journey-for-a-1-year-bio-hack.12438/post-151094>

I have been thinking more and more about this bio-hack. "Full court press" is the way I want to describe it. Heavy focus on CT, seafood, and good sleep. Get some sun when I can. No artificial foods or sweeteners. Minimal to no alcohol or coffee. Plenty of good water. Use blue-light-blockers as much as I can. I will be talking to my optometrist about getting blu-light blocker glasses for anytime and anywhere. My eyesight has actually improved over the last couple of years so it may be an interesting discussion with her. I think I will also go ahead with the telomere testing but I won't get to that until late January at the earliest. I should probably also get a general physical from my doctor to set a baseline. Again, that won't happen until the last part of January--working on a big proposal for the next two or three weeks in Chicago with plenty of exposure to nnEMF and plenty of opportunities for stress.  Shijin13 would understand.

# Thread: Looking for Ms Optimal....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-ms-optimal.12443/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 93
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### rgp888 — 2014-12-22T15:44:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-ms-optimal.12443/post-150795>

Hello

I have just found Jack Kruse and his blog and this forum. The best writing and thinking I have found to date about the whys and wherefore's... - very excited that I may just have found the answers I have been looking for.

A bit of history - I was a very sick child, not expected to live (born with whooping cough or cystic fibrosis or both - "cured" by a natural hygienist), began competitive swimming at the age of 5, moved into the elite ranks as a teenager through to late 20's. Then changed sports and went on to two Olympic Games and top 4 in the world (retired at 42 years old!).

Such a long period of time in elite sport (20+ years training fairly hard plus 10 years of ultra high intensity elite level aerobic endurance with a good splash of strength, speed and power) leaves it's mark. (Not difficult for the clever people on this forum to simply guess what is going on with me now and I bet they would be right on the money!) But, its better than being a fat (my mother never weighed less than 250lb and her mother topped the scales at over 450lb), and sick kid, but still some interesting effects (like pretty major adrenal fatigue once I retired).

Eight years ago I had to have my right ovary out (it was huge) - there was no mention of it but I suspect I may have had PCOS and just never noticed being so fit (explains a few very bad backaches that used to appear and disappear), then I had to have a partial hyterectomy due to all sorts of nasty things going on with my uterus.

So now I am 56, overweight (not a lot - but I don't accept I should have to have a "middle aged spread"), constantly tired and frustrated by having such difficulty losing weight. Of course, even though I was a "fat" athlete (15% body fat weighed underwater), I was never really overweight. But now it seems the only way I can lose weight is to go on less than 800 calories a day and train for more than two hours. Can't sustain that, so bounce back up again.

Also, every time I get close to my ideal weight, I get sick. Last time I had success I tried a really great IF program with a decent weights program done in the morning while fasted. Totally comfortable not eating and training - spent most of my life doing up to three hours hard training before breakfast!!! I lost 8 kg's in 5 weeks and felt pretty good, but then got shingles.

Since then I feel like I have never recovered. Feel more tired than ever, run down, not happy with things in general....

Cortisol levels, as an elite athlete, were always an issue. Constantly pushing though glycogen depletion while heavy training in the morning for three hours at a time fasted! Thought I might have left that phase behind!

Reading here, everything starts to make sense! Cortisol, leptin, melatonin (I don't sleep well), while I'm traveling through menopause pretty well I know I can do better.

Embarking on the Leptin Reset. Exciting! Went to doc to get blood done but won't hear back til the new year I suspect. I live in a sub tropical area, the sun comes up at 4:30am, I have been known to go to bed at 3am!!!! Often. Circadian will be an interesting journey! And, oh how I hate the cold!

Ms Optimal is in there I want her to come out and play!

### shah78 — 2014-12-22T16:05:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-ms-optimal.12443/post-150796>

You have come to the right place. While certainly not Olympic quality, I once was Olympic quantity.  Ironman triathlete. I'm healthier now (age 59) in so many ways now than (age 26). Same waist (30 " with about 35lbs more muscle. I've reduced my strenuous physical activity by over (95%). That was no typo, (95%). Sure I can't run a marathon, or even a 10k, but everything else is better. If you apply your Olympic enthusiasm to this program, you are a sure thing.

### nicld — 2014-12-22T22:18:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-ms-optimal.12443/post-150813>

Welcome Ms Optimal,

For years I drank the juice of eat less, exercise more and all it got me was sick and fat. You have come to the right place to get yourself right. Just a note, for some people, it will take alot of time to get better and you may feel worse for a bit.

Oh and as you get going you may start to crave the cold (I do) and get yourself a pair of blueblocker glasses [http://www.amazon.com/Uvex-S1933X-E...19279459&sr=8-2&keywords=blue+blocker+glasses](http://www.amazon.com/Uvex-S1933X-Eyewear-SCT-Orange-Anti-Fog/dp/B000USRG90/ref=sr_1_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1419279459&sr=8-2&keywords=blue+blocker+glasses). I think this is the one thing that has helped me the most in fixing my sleep.

Take care

Nicole

### rgp888 — 2014-12-30T03:03:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-ms-optimal.12443/post-151160>

So here we are, in between Christmas and New Year.
First report:
\- I have taken some "before" pictures - only with the use of a mirror so not very good but at least recorded
\- Food wise, its not much of a change for me - mostly timing and eating breakfast! I don't usually eat very often (you could describe my usual eating habits as "paleo IF'er" with a few treats and cheats) but now have been having three meals a day - 7am, 1pm and 6pm, or fairly close to. Plugged my breakfasts into a nutrition program and I discovered its actually quite a lot of food to get the required amount of protein - when I do that it makes a BIG difference in how I feel all day. Considering I haven't eaten breakfast for many years, and close to 30 years of doing between 1 hour and 3 hours of highly intensive training before breakfast when I did eat breakfast! But one of my main goals is to reduce cortisol, so sacrifices to tradition must go! My appetite is changing - already waking up looking forward to food - haven't done that for - well, forever! Because of my "treats" I was always looking for them, but those desires have completely gone away - in just a few days! If I grab a coffee while I'm out the thought of a treat to go with it is almost revolting! I have my coffee as an espresso - at home I put a teaspoon of organic grass fed double cream, and out as a piccolo (its an espresso with a touch of milk served in a short glass) - no more than two coffees a day and only ever in the morning. I also drink herb tea - licorice, gotu kola, and rosehip are my favourites. Oh, I have taken my water intake up to 3-4 Litres a day - cold - (from next to nothing - room temp). The other thing I have changed is eating seafood. When I was young I worked on a prawn (shrimp) trawler in the Gulf of Carpentaria - seafood was really fresh (;-)) since then I have not desired it. I have replaced 95% of my chicken consumption with shellfish, prawns, Atlantic salmon and canned fish. It's quite satisfying! I have just bought some lamb's fry but haven't ventured to eat it yet!
\- Sleep and circadian rhythm - sunrise here Gold Coast Australia summertime (tropical - 95% humidity and 30 - 35 degress C) 4:30am, sunset 6:30 pm. Three nights a week I turn the lights out at 7:30pm and four nights a week I teach martial arts until 9:30pm, at which time I turn the lights out. Been going to bed about 10pm, but still not going to sleep til closer to midnight. I am waking up earlier naturally, but have not achieved my goal of waking naturally at 5am and heading to the beach (5 minutes away) for a quick dip (don't worry not workout intended!) in the early morning sun (when its not raining). I am waking up a lot more refreshed and with more energy and looking forward to breakfast, so I guess that's a good start.
\- Cold Thermagenics - I don't have a bathtub - so I have confiscated a wheely bin we weren't using (its brand new)!!!! I can fit inside quite easily and can get in and out with a step. Our tap water is quite warm at present. The first test I put in about 6 kg of ice and the water was pleasant but not that cold! The second one, last night, I used 12 kg of ice - it was cold but not enough to make my skin numb - stayed in for 15 minutes - shivering a bit at the end. Did a face dip after - that was cold enough for an ice cream headache and to make my skin numb - strange how good these things feel!!! Been trying to find a thermometer to measure the water temp but no luck so far.
\- Exercise - what exercise? I have decided to do no exercise (except a dip in the ocean) for at least two weeks, and then introduce 20 minutes of light walking before dinner. My martial arts are a gentle "no power, no strength, no speed" form and as the teacher I don't need to do a lot - so that doesn't count. I spend some time on the roller and stretching in between getting up, showering (in the cold water) and breakfast.

I haven't got my blood tests back yet. Haven't got my blue blocker glasses delivered yet. My goals are to regain my energy (tired all the time), lose my middle aged spread (I have about 10kg to lose), control my menopausal hormones, and create a very vibrant life going into the second half of my life (I believe I will live at least until 100 - and I want to do it well). I am usually the fittest and healthiest person I know, until I ran myself into the ground earlier this year and ended up with shingles, which I feel I haven't fully recovered from. My blood pressure is 110/70, I take no medication whatsoever and never have, and since my childhood illnesses, never got sicker than a cold until I had to have an ovary removed, then a hysterectomy (my mother and mother's mother had the same thing). I have trained the house down all my life, but no-one ever believes my age - I still look 45 (according to others - not me - I think I look 145!). So, this is not about recovering from obesity, or illness, or anything chronic for me, but my belief that a person should live very well until the time they die, and that should be in their late 90's to early 100's (or even more???)

Ok - here is my before shot and my wheely bin ice bath!

(have to figure out how to share an image - the link to dropbox won't work, and sure as hell don't have these pics anywhere online!!!!!)

### rgp888 — 2015-01-01T09:36:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-ms-optimal.12443/post-151298>

My goodness - I must be weird!

Currently reading [http://jackkruse.com/energy-and-epigenetics-4-light-water-magnetism/](http://jackkruse.com/energy-and-epigenetics-4-light-water-magnetism/) - there's a whole section on the doom of Australian's - oh well, at least I have moved from the "driest capital of the driest state of the driest continent in the world" (seriously, 10% humidity, and 45C in summer, and only a few days with rain in winter - grew up on a farm with less than 1" of rain a year - king of arid farming techniques!) to the sub-tropics - its currently summer - but here it rains all day and night and humidity rarely gets below 90%, still hot - about 33C most of the time.

Then this part of the post - "Exercise in the heat (40° C) increases muscle glycogen oxidation and reduces whole-body fat oxidation (Febbraio et al. 1994), in comparison to the same exercise intensity performed at 20 C. When temperature rises, serum lactate levels rise" - (it says also that cravings for carbs increases, and perceived exertion increases) ..... I am weird.......

Back in the days when I was one of the weirdest athletes on the planet (massive VO2 Max and an odd ability to maintain close to max heart rate for an extended period of time) I THRIVED in Adelaide's intense dry heat. I remember doing 4 time trials at the end of a 45C day, 6pm, still 42C. The length of the "race" was around 1 minute 50 seconds, with about 15 minutes between time trials. I did consecutive best times for the 1st, 2nd and 3rd time trials, the fourth one being in the middle. Lactate tests at the end of every time trial showed lower than my normal lactate for "races" of the same distance. And the heat always made me feel lighter and the effort easier....go figure!

At rest my lactate levels were very low - o.5 - it took a lot of work to get them to come up to 2.0 (which we took to mean "aerobic exercise") - this was always my least favourite level - I'd rather be in the zone of near max HR where for me time and space stood still! The (more advanced) exercise physiologists used to say that I "ate my lactate" - that I used it for fuel - very effectively, and spared glycogen, also very effectively.

Now in those days my resting heart rate was about 45 normally and my under tongue temp in the low 35C range (on the cold side). Also I was never hungry after training, especially when it was hot, and when I did eat I craved steak and greens and had to force the expected rice in (the sports so-called nutritionists who controlled us insisted we eat "sufficient" carbs but I never enjoyed them - never heard of "paleo" or anything like that back then).

Conversely I hated having to race or train in the cold. I always joked I must be cold blooded (since I was colder than average body temp) and I liked to move in the heat and hibernate in the cold. Not that I was ever cold - 0C was as cold as it got!

I once did a metabolic test, kind of a reverse VO2max test, where I was walking at a fairly brisk pace before my metabolic rate came up to the average person's resting rate. With my low resting HR, low body temp and this measure they said I had a low metabolic rate but it wasn't a "normal" low metabolic rate because of my capacity for extremely high exercise output.

How does all of this impact things now? After my first week Leptin rx I have gained 3kg, but I feel great, just loving the cold thermogenisis, sleeping much better and feeling more in tune with things, certainly brighter mentally.

Today is New Year's Day - no resolutions per se, however, this week, I am going to focus even more on going to bed early, turning out the lights as early as I can, and getting up at sunrise (still haven't got there yet). I have been forcing myself to eat three meals a day (because the guidelines said to) but I think I can safely go just breakfast and dinner - it's a question mark for me because I have a history of not eating much, which I think tugs into the cortisol issue, however, I have never tried to eat regularly every 12 hours before. I also want to spend a bit more time in the sun (I work all day on the computer - web developer) - when its not raining.

Hopefully I will get my blood test back next week and can have a look at my hormone levels....
