**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sore-stomach-from-raw-oysters.12461/post-150952>

What are some possible reasons why my stomach may feel sore after eating raw oysters. I have been eating 2 per day. My stomach doesn't get sore straight away but normally by the second or third day of eating eating them. The pain is not strong.... just a fairly mild discomfort. Would it help if I cooked them....at least until my gut is able to handle them?

### Lahelada — 2014-12-25T11:37:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sore-stomach-from-raw-oysters.12461/post-150970>

### Ben1983 — 2014-12-26T08:11:39+0200

### nicld — 2014-12-26T17:13:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sore-stomach-from-raw-oysters.12461/post-151016>

### Christina Gagnon — 2018-07-21T18:34:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sore-stomach-from-raw-oysters.12461/post-246354>

I LOVE raw oysres and clams - when I was 6-years-old I ate them by the gallon happily but now in adulthood I have hemochromatosis (iron overload) so can't eat ANY raw shellfish because the bacterium called Vibrio Vulnificus thrives on iron and could kill me. You have no idea how envious I am reading your wonderful comments about guzzling down a couple of dozen but fortunately I like them cooked also (just not as much).

I would like to know what Jack thinks too but I'm guessing there is really nothing I can do about it. I have phlebotomies still but not as many as I had when I was first diagnosed. Believe or not I was giving up three pints of blood a week with no problem because I had so much iron in my blood.

### Jan Christer — 2018-09-19T21:16:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sore-stomach-from-raw-oysters.12461/post-251359>

Just watch out for potential zinc overdosing when eating oysters. Since oysters are very high in zinc, the daily upper tolerance of 40 mg per day can easily be exceeded unless one monitors the intake.
Intake also depends on variety of oysters, e.g. eastern wild oysters contain 74-182 mg zinc per 100 g serving, while Pacific oysters only contain 17-33 mg zinc per 100 g serving.

More info:
[https://nutritiondata.self.com/foods-015124000000000000000-w.html](https://nutritiondata.self.com/foods-015124000000000000000-w.html)

### Jack Kruse — 2018-09-19T21:24:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sore-stomach-from-raw-oysters.12461/post-251360>

> [Ben1983 said:](/goto/post?id=150952)
>
> What are some possible reasons why my stomach may feel sore after eating raw oysters. I have been eating 2 per day. My stomach doesn't get sore straight away but normally by the second or third day of eating them. The pain is not strong.... just a fairly mild discomfort. Would it help if I cooked them....at least until my gut is able to handle them?

I never argue with the ignorant, because people watching on social media may not be able to tell the difference due to their own dopamine state.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

This is the picture of what is left of a friends house after we had tornado's in the Gulf South. If you can help out please do so. We are taking donations at [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted]) via paypal. If you want to send the family a gift card at amazon they are coming to our house at ten AM to shop on my laptop. Clothing and shoes would also be helpful. Aaliyah Howard, Alexis Howard, Cornelius Howard. youngest has size 8 pants 13 shoes 13 yr old petite teen size pants size 8 shoe, 10.5 female shirt oldest XL shirt 9.5-10 shoes. They need clothing and shoes most now. Cash is also helpful. For those who have already reached out to them thank you from the bottom of my heart. They also could use male 2XL shirts 40/32 pants; size 18 ladies pants.

### nonchalant — 2014-12-25T15:18:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-can-help-please-consider-it.12462/post-150975>

Thanks for the second choice. Amazon is easier for me than dealing with Paypal.

### fitness@home — 2014-12-25T15:27:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-can-help-please-consider-it.12462/post-150976>

Their home is destroyed. Truly hope that they are all okay (physically) and that any pets made it through too.

Grew up in midwest and witnessed firsthand the power of tornadoes. In 1973 a tornado picked up my aunt and uncles home and dumped it in the middle of the road. They were asleep and never knew it was coming. Aunt Alma spent the rest of her life in a wheelchair.

Glad the Howard's have friends like your family to help, especially today on Christmas.

### caroline — 2014-12-27T03:03:02+0200

# Thread: Zipcodes and Lab Rats
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zipcodes-and-lab-rats.12470/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TimB — 2014-12-26T18:56:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zipcodes-and-lab-rats.12470/post-151019>

I ran across this posting today on the Geoengineering Watch website.

[http://www.geoengineeringwatch.org/...fare-training-would-harm-humans-and-wildlife/](http://www.geoengineeringwatch.org/documents-show-navys-electromagnetic-warfare-training-would-harm-humans-and-wildlife/)

The Navy is planning on doing electronic warfare training over the wilderness area of the Olympic Peninsula in Washington state, with the usual obfuscated pronouncements about the safety of wildlife and humans. The article does a pretty good job of reviewing some of the research on the harmful effects of non-native EMF, although Geoengineering Watch undoubtedly has a pretty tiny readership due to even greater widespread ignorance about geoengineering than about EMF.

Fortunately there is some resistance to the war games by some of the potential local lab rats.

### Josh — 2014-12-26T19:23:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zipcodes-and-lab-rats.12470/post-151020>

### Konish — 2014-12-26T22:03:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/functional-diagnostic-nutrition-practitioner.12471/post-151022>

Has anyone here does this practitioner course? [http://functionaldiagnosticnutrition.com/learn-more/](http://functionaldiagnosticnutrition.com/learn-more/)

It seems to cover drawing diagnostic lab tests. The specific tests learnt about can be found here: [http://www.windycitycrossfit.com/windycitycrossfit/functional-diagnostic-nutrition.html](http://www.windycitycrossfit.com/windycitycrossfit/functional-diagnostic-nutrition.html)

In my mind was thinking it may be a good educational opportunity-especially when ready to teach others. The closest course i could find to the lab testing aspect of this website, Optimized Life. To learn how to read labs when 'helping' yourself and other people.

Well aware the QAB would be left out. One would need to combine the holistic quantum aspect ourselves

Its about $4k

I'm starting to see others could use help and some form of qualification, especially in reading labs, would be useful as a credential. You cant really learn this stuff 'officially' as the world as yet to catch up, but this is the closest ive found. Free feel to suggest any other courses.

I have read almost all blogs, Q&As even on MDA, listened to podcast, gotten the lab results, take the recommended supplements and follow Dr.K's advice but seem to have stalled on the leptin rest. Any help on how to move forward will be greatly appreciated.

### caroline — 2014-12-29T01:28:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-leptin-reset-increase-bun.12472/post-151109>

Hi again! As I said above ..... Dr. K. needs context to steer you in the right direction. He can't practice medicine on the net and in fact has to be very, very careful ..... so that is the reason for giving him a health history.

It is also a great idea to follow other's journals - we all help each other when we talk about stuff and our concerns ...and how to get to our own n=1.

Dr. K. always tells us that there are no stupid questions ...so ask away and we will try to help - but we need to know more of your history.

Where do you live?

What are you eating?

water?

non native EMF ..... home? work?

C/T?

A journal is great for detailing what you are doing and keeping track .... and it is fun to look back and see the progress!

to start a journal ...... click on "forum" above top bar - and then scroll down until you see the optimal journal section. Start a new thread.... ......Give your journal a simple name ... like "angie's journal to wellness"
and let us have all the good, the bad and the ugly! or whatever you would like to tell us ...lol

Please ask if you are having any issues - or pm me if you want.....

welcome to this amazing journey!

I will be back ..... going to the beach!

The important thing is the three legged stool ......

# Thread: new beginnings, everyday blessings
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 84
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### rlee314 — 2014-12-27T21:36:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-151069>

aloha all,
time to start a journal.
i've been reading dr kruse's blogs for a few years, checking in on the forum from time to time too. it wasn't until the optimal reset at the beginning of 2014 that i really got into and inspired to commit to epi-paleo and all the rest of it.
bit of intro--i'm 35, mother to a jovial 4 year old boy. we live on maui and enjoy 'island life'.
as a child i was sick often. vaginal birth, 2nd born, breastfed for 1 year. tonsils out at age 5, chronic pneumonia from years 6-8. (started having symptoms of gluten intolerance at age 11 though didn't make the connection of why i hated pizza til 16 years later). born and raised in utah, moved to idaho at 15-18, moved to oregon at 18-31, moved to maui 5 months pregnant at 31. wisdom teeth out at 29, kidney stones a year later. on BCP for 10 years. after going off BCP hormones fluctuated a bit, eczema appeared on face--that's when i decided to go 'low gluten', skin improved, pregnant a few months later. excellent pregnancy and birth-home birth, breastfed baby for 3.5 years--around the time my boy weaned is when i started having some health issues...noticed pain/tenderness in right breast and lump, determined it was plugged milk ducts from weaning...around the same time i decreased dosage of lugols iodine--i had previously been supplementing 50mg for 6 months. breast pain, tenderness and lump appeared to be cyclical--assumed was related to decreased iodine and possibly hormones. traveled to utah to visit family--was careless w/my diet, returned home and became ill w/a stomach virus---recovered from stomach virus but gut felt 'off'. post stomach virus symptoms of sinus infection started. dealt w/breast pain/tenderness, lump/cyst and sinus infection symptoms from july-october. beginning of october i felt getting some labs would be a good idea--i posted the results in the 'optimal labs forum'. not great results. TPO ab, not great thyroid, elevated TSH, clear indication of chronic bacterial infection etc. at this point i became VERY serious about epi-paleo and avoiding nnEMF, CT and grounding every day. I ate seafoods 2-3x/day for several weeks--feeling awesome. labs improved-though TSH increased, i did increase lugols dose in addition to eating seafood every day, also increased selenium dose, infection cleared-mostly....
so...after getting my labs back and realizing i was dealing w/a chronic infection-i really should have done something about it before i had the labs done-and realizing that i may be developing an AI-dis-ease/hashis i was pretty depressed and discouraged. i also assumed w/my labs and elevated TSH that i was infertile--i had been thinking of considering another child for several months. well...much to my surprise-shock really-i got a + pregnancy test on november 9th, just one month after getting the horrible lab results! obviously not the 'optimal' time to get pregnant. (this is certainly not the optimal pregnancy i was hoping for/planning for) up until the week of thanksgiving i was continuing w/my seafood diet 2-3x/day, avocado, raw onion and raw garlic + salad greens...then the feelings of nausea and not much appetite started to set in and the thought and smell of seafood was repulsive. Other than not being able to continue w/the daily seafoods intake i've felt pretty good. this past week i've been able to start eating some seafoods again-sardines wrapped in seaweed, some salmon and shrimp too.
i had some labs done again about a month ago--vast improvement in most areas from the previous labs done in october. vitamin d level is 70, metabolic panel looked good except for alkaline phosphatase is low, mcv is still a bit elevated, hemoglobin and hemotocrit are low/normal at 12.5 and 37.3. LDH is also low at 121 and TSH is elevated at 4.7
reading Kovita's optimal journal and the lovely words expressed to her when she announced her pregnancy have been very inspiring for me. my ND said it's quite a miracle i conceived w/my previous labs, infection and TSH and likely thyroid issues-though i am inclined to think the thyroid may be linked to the infection-as i don't have symptoms of hypothyroid-and reading about iodine, perhaps the increased TSH is due to supplementing iodine?
anyhow...hoping from this point forward i will be able to have a healthy and enjoyable pregnancy. i contine to CT-in the ocean several times/week. ground daily. do daily epsom salts baths. trying to focus on the blessings that a baby brings and not the unfortunate state of my health...though, i'm not going to be ignorant about that either.

thanks for reading....

### rlee314 — 2015-01-31T00:58:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-154298>

thanks all!!

so, i have a positive update-i think-on some of my labs.
got TSH retested a week ago and it went from 4.7 in november to 2.5 i'm not exactly sure what pregnancy does to the thyroid or TSH? but, i'm interpreting this as a good thing?

also, my irons increased from november as well. had labs done on 1/7
hemoglobin 11/25 was 12.5 now, 1/7, 13.4
hemotocrit 11/25 was 37.3 now, 1/7, 39.5
i'm wondering if epsom salts baths and lots of sun could have improved irons? i thought i read something from dr seneff -and dr kruse- that iron is connected to sulfate?

anyhow. feeling pretty great overall. i'm able to eat seafood daily-ideally multiple times/day-again now. i wasn't able to stomach my beloved salmon and shrimp for a few weeks. and i was told at a restaurant they would NOT serve me oysters since i was pregnant--my fault for opening my mouth bc at the time i didn't look pregnant!

### rlee314 — 2015-01-31T01:08:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-154301>

what's not been so great is sleep. sigh. wondering about adenosine. safe for pregnancy? also curious about DHEA. safe to supplement? how can i increase? it was low on labs from october-before conception.
part of the issue may be that i still co-sleep w/my 4 year old. i'm trying to transition him to his own bed--slowly.
i don't eat carbs past 6. i wear blue blockers at night. and candle light after sunset. i was doing good w/not having to get up in the night to urinate--i normally never do, but, now being 14 weeks pregnant i am waking about 3am to urinate. should a pregnant woman be able to go all night w/out needing to urinate? so, sleep from 3-6am is not great.

thoughts or suggestions on how to improve sleep?

also, i feel like my glutathione levels have improved...but, recently reading how important glutathione/GSH is during pregnancy. would a transdermal or liposomal form be ok to do? i know sulfur is also connected to glutathione--maybe the sulfur-epsom salts baths and garlic and onions-really is doing me good? looking up old threads for the sulfur/iron connection.

### rlee314 — 2015-01-31T07:11:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-154334>

just reread the hasi/melasma blog. holy fucking hell!! this is/was me. i'm going to go w/was b/c i feel soooo much better. and my labs seem to have improved--thanks to tons of grounding. epsom salts, iodine, selenium, dha, epi-paleo, positive thoughts, blue blockers, electrons from the sun and ocean.

quote from the blog. ""The amino acid tyrosine is a precursor for thyroid hormones, DHEA, and dopamine synthesis. An enzyme activated during chronic bacterial infection (think altered quorum sensing in the gut due to synthetic estrogens) can deplete tyrosine levels, which is why there is an association between low thyroid function, DHEA, and chronic sinus infections. It also lowers iodine and lithium levels. This is why Hashimoto’s can be a gateway disease to many other diseases like PCOS, Multiple Sclerosis, Devic’s syndrome, or anxiety/depression too." question for dr jack or anyone else who knows. what is the enzymre that is activated during chronic bacterial infection?

### rlee314 — 2015-05-26T21:03:50+0300

so...reading UBI 10 again...and UBI 12...bc i am thinking/feeling that my baby-almost 33 weeks-is getting hiccups sometimes. at night mostly it seems. and, i've also been experiencing what i assume is heartburn? reflux maybe? i honestly don't know bc i have only ever experienced heartburn/reflux during the last few weeks of my previous pregnancy-and that seemed to be triggered by foods-specifically tomatoes...so, i'm wondering if both of these symptoms are 'red flags' that something is not quite right w/me and baby...or, if both are perhaps a 'normal' part of pregnancy caused by organs being shifted-reflux-and baby learning to breathe-hiccups.

### rlee314 — 2015-05-29T01:42:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-165726>

thanks hope.

the reflux has subsided thankfully. i spent a few days at the beach, did some epsom salts baths and have been drinking more water.
i was about ready to biohack my diet--which i feel like is pretty good. though, idk. i eat fish 2-3x/day and have pretty much the entire pregnancy. i live in hawai'i and while i \*could\* be eating a variety of fruits i don't much. i don't really crave fruits. i don't really have any pregnancy cravings--which friends feel is odd. but, i didn't have pregnancy cravings my last pregnancy either. so..idk? i've been doing a mostly leptin type method of eating most of the pregnancy. i just wake up hungry-bc i don't eat past 6pm-and have just always felt better in general if i eat a BAB. though the past few weeks it takes me a while-like an hour or more-to eat the BAB. i usually eat it in 2 installments. since the reflux-which was only awful and noticable for a day or 2-i've not been eating so much at breakie...just breaking it up a bit. so, i feel like i'm eating regularly from about wake-up, 30 min of waking til about 9 or 10 am, so that's like 3-4 hours of eating kindof.

(he really wants one bc ALL of his friends have one or more) and--he is an epi-paleo kid every day. we eat fish at least 1x if not 2x/day, every. single. day. his friends--ALL have some sort of screen device. and, their diet is different, completely.

sigh.
38 weeks and feeling a bit discouraged.
at last weeks apt my hemoglobin was tested via finger prick-came back under 9. i know that iron lowers due to blood volume expansion during pregnancy. but, unless the device used to measure this was way off-which is possible i guess-then my hg has gone down significantly in the last month--it was a little over 10 when checked about a month ago.

rereading the redox rx i came across this.
"12. Your **hemoglobin and hematocrit, called an H/H level,** can tell you if you have a lowered redox potential (you do if you are anemic)."

i'm curious how this applies to pregnancy? @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) my midwives didn't seem terribly alarmed but noted they wanted to see the level come up and would be cautiously watching post delivery for signs of too much bleeding and consider a possible pitocin shot. i'd really like to avoid this. but, of course also desire a safe birth.

really hoping my redox is not low.

39+ weeks now...feeling ready. i had my son at 39 weeks so hoping this baby makes it's debut soon.

### rlee314 — 2015-08-07T01:23:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-174145>

happy to introduce my aloha baby girl!!!!! (i was a bit surprised she's a girl...the entire pregnancy and before i actually thought she'd be a girl but most everyone else would look at me and say boy. my little boy was also pretty set on having a brother...but he's pretty pleased w/his little sister.

she arrived on 7/15 at dawn, 6:02am. 7'7 18.5 inches
i called the midwives at 3:30. they arrived at 4am. she was born 2 hours later.
she came very aware. she cried for about 1 min and then stopped and just looked around.

she is very strong. holds her head up well wants to stand. can roll from side ti side well.
nursing is going great on the left breast, however the right breast has been a struggle w/mastitis and plugged milk ducts.

she is very content. her circadian rhythm at night iss almost too good. for the first several days she would sleep the entire night w/out waking. not so great for breastfeeding, mastitis, engorgement etc.

she is my LIGHT baby.

born at dawn on a beautiful sunny day.
her name is Ilee Solana (eye-lee)
Ailey is irish meaning light. i'm spelling it differently--my middle name is lee. i also think the irish pronunciation might be different.
Solana is spanish meaning sunshine.
Sol=sun
Lana is hawai'ian meaning of calm waters.

i spent a lot of time in the water during my pregnancy with her. i was in the ocean the day before giving birth. i had a water labor but stood up to birth her so she was born above the water. she's very calm and alert.
she naturally wakes at her time of birth every day and stays awake and alert for a few hours. the morning hours are her most awake, alert parts of the day so far-3 weeks in no. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### kovita — 2015-08-07T14:37:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-174177>

The hemoglobin levels, I had some decline during the winter too, but it went to 11,5 at the delivery. I did some plant based isron supps, very mild to see such a good levels again, but it was probably necessary since I ate liver but it did not go up and the iron metabolism showed iron reserve depletion. I continue with this herbal approach now and my baby does not look anemic, so I assume it works. We spend a lot of time naked in the morning and late afternoon sun. The summer is very hot here, unusually hot. My baby started to reach for the things before age of two months and is now able to catch them. He continues sleeping so well in the night (1 max 2 feedings) that it is a real pleasure to share a bed with him. I feel blessed while listening to a lot of mothers who cannot breastfeed and have babies who wake up every hour and are much older than Loren. Of course they ask what I do and they think. i am crazy, when I say what I do and in what. i believe ;-) Light is too ordinary to be considered the best medicine.

### rlee314 — 2015-08-30T08:30:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-176054>

thanks all!!

so...my breastfeeding struggles turned into abscesses that needed surgery!! ugh! sigh. so....it's been many years since i've taken antibiotics...i was on IV abx for 5 days and then oral abx for 10 days. how do i repair the damage to my gut?
i'm currently breastfeeding my sweet girl on one breast and having to give her donor milk---so grateful for the mommas who have shared their milk so that i can keep my girl off formula! i do hope and plan to nurse on the other side once completely healed.
i'm loading up-even more so-on dha, sunlight and drinking tons of turmeric tea and water.

anyhow, aside from that....my little girl is amazing! very alert and SO CALM and CONTENT. <---this makes her very predictable. her fussing has a reason. no guessing. and fussing is rare and short. fussing is...i am hungry, i need to burp, i am tired. she sleeps a lot. i forgot how much newborns sleep. her circadian rhythm continues to be pretty great--and she is night nursing more which i am pretty happy about actually. co-sleeping makes 'breast sleeping/night nursing' easy. and my circadian rhythm doesn't get messed up. she wakes with the roosters-just like my little boy.

so, again friends, id love advice/suggestions on how to repair my gut and body after the onslaught of antibiotics--of which i am grateful for--the abscesses in my breast were cultured as strep.

# Thread: Vision effects
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-effects.12478/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2014-12-28T02:39:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-effects.12478/post-151078>

Early this morning, soon after awakening, I noticed a vision sensation. I was still in bed, with my eyes closed, and I noticed a ring of light around my peripheral vision that got smaller and moved toward the center of my vision and vanished. Then it was replaced by another circle, and so forth. Like the reverse of most of the standing wave videos I've seen. The ring was white to light grey light on a dark grey background. It looked like a smoke ring, because its shape changed slightly as it moved toward the center. Sometimes the light was fairly bright. Each ring lasted about 5 seconds. This continued for about 5 minutes, I suppose. It was fascinating.

But for the last minute or two, the rings gradually lost their coherence. Instead of decreasing steadily in size until it vanished, some parts would lag, distorting the circle, but it would all come together at the last instant. Finally, though, I just got blobs of light with some movement, but no organization. The light was still bright, but wacky. More like a lava lamp than a standing wave. Then the show was over.

Thinking back, I realized that a little before this neat stuff, I had opened my eyes briefly to see if the sun was up or not. I guessed that sunrise was 30-45 minutes away, so I closed my eyes again. That's it. The whole show resulted from a couple seconds of low light.

I have seen this before, but I didn't recognize the potential so I disregarded it, or opened my eyes and spoiled the effect.

This sensation could be similar to the fluttering I saw before, just in slow motion.
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/kruses-theorum.11382/page-3](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/kruses-theorum.11382/page-3)
The fluttering came from longer exposure to brighter light.

So were these waves from the retina headed towards the optic nerve? Visual Purple and all that?

### Martin — 2014-12-28T15:51:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-effects.12478/post-151084>

Fascinating. I've gotten "ceiling fans" spinning in my peripheral vision in the morning. Rotating and squished up into a corner of my vision. Color was smokey. Lasted a few minutes, if that.

NSH

### nonchalant — 2014-12-28T16:51:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-effects.12478/post-151085>

> [Martin said:](/goto/post?id=151084)
>
> Fascinating. I've gotten "ceiling fans" spinning in my peripheral vision in the morning. Rotating and squished up into a corner of my vision. Color was smokey. Lasted a few minutes, if that.
>
> NSH

Interesting, Martin. Sounds like a vortex. Actually it sounds like the fluttering I see occasionally. But I've had my eyes open, so I wouldn't have seen the smoke, just whatever is in front of me, with a visual effect to it.
I think the most important variable, for me, is that it is morning. Everything is closer together then. But wait, that's when cortisol is running rampant... Maybe my vision is freshly repaired then?

One thing I forgot to mention, I had my closed eyes pointed towards the dim light yesterday morning. That may have helped me see it.

I was still in bed, with my eyes closed, and I noticed a ring of light around my peripheral vision that got smaller and moved toward the center of my vision and vanished. Then it was replaced by another circle, and so forth. Like the reverse of most of the standing wave videos I've seen. The ring was white to light grey light on a dark grey background. It looked like a smoke ring, because its shape changed slightly as it moved toward the center. Sometimes the light was fairly bright. Each ring lasted about 5 seconds. This continued for about 5 minutes, I suppose. It was fascinating.
>
> But for the last minute or two, the rings gradually lost their coherence. Instead of decreasing steadily in size until it vanished, some parts would lag, distorting the circle, but it would all come together at the last instant. Finally, though, I just got blobs of light with some movement, but no organization. The light was still bright, but wacky. More like a lava lamp than a standing wave. Then the show was over.
>
> Thinking back, I realized that a little before this neat stuff, I had opened my eyes briefly to see if the sun was up or not. I guessed that sunrise was 30-45 minutes away, so I closed my eyes again. That's it. The whole show resulted from a couple seconds of low light.
>
> I have seen this before, but I didn't recognize the potential so I disregarded it, or opened my eyes and spoiled the effect.
>
> This sensation could be similar to the fluttering I saw before, just in slow motion.

### CTforlife — 2014-12-28T20:52:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-effects.12478/post-151098>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=151078)
>
> Early this morning, soon after awakening, I noticed a vision sensation. I was still in bed, with my eyes closed, and I noticed a ring of light around my peripheral vision that got smaller and moved toward the center of my vision and vanished. Then it was replaced by another circle, and so forth. Like the reverse of most of the standing wave videos I've seen. The ring was white to light grey light on a dark grey background. It looked like a smoke ring, because its shape changed slightly as it moved toward the center. Sometimes the light was fairly bright. Each ring lasted about 5 seconds. This continued for about 5 minutes, I suppose. It was fascinating.
>
> But for the last minute or two, the rings gradually lost their coherence. Instead of decreasing steadily in size until it vanished, some parts would lag, distorting the circle, but it would all come together at the last instant. Finally, though, I just got blobs of light with some movement, but no organization. The light was still bright, but wacky. More like a lava lamp than a standing wave. Then the show was over.
>
> Thinking back, I realized that a little before this neat stuff, I had opened my eyes briefly to see if the sun was up or not. I guessed that sunrise was 30-45 minutes away, so I closed my eyes again. That's it. The whole show resulted from a couple seconds of low light.
>
> I have seen this before, but I didn't recognize the potential so I disregarded it, or opened my eyes and spoiled the effect.
>
> This sensation could be similar to the fluttering I saw before, just in slow motion.

### Jude — 2014-12-29T00:15:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-effects.12478/post-151106>

Have a similar story...this time last yr developed a detached vitreous humor in left eye with significent bleeding ..left with large dark floaters occluding vision.
11mths later my dr can't believe the difference in my floaters ..have reduced in size and colour..vision has improved so much thank goodness....he even commented that my long standing corneal scar has almost disappeared ..really had to go look for it.....
He said he supposed I was still following JK!
Daily tinned sardines and salmon does the trick as well!

### cinnamon — 2014-12-29T04:10:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-effects.12478/post-151113>

This past summer I noticed some interesting vision effects with reflective materials.

The first time it happened, I was walking toward a stop sign. I don't think there was anything special about this stop sign - I've walked past it many times before and since. But that afternoon it looked huge and glowing. I could see the pattern of reflective coating that made up the red part of the sign. I had never really thought about why you can see stop signs at night with your headlights until that moment! LOL I walked right up to it and stared. It was afternoon and the sun was behind me, shining toward the sign.

After that, I noticed many times when I was out walking around that the white paint used for the pedestrian crossing lanes at intersections is reflective. I had never noticed before, but now I often found it glowing brightly.

### Sue-UK — 2014-12-29T10:54:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-effects.12478/post-151124>

I've been playing card games with the family over Christmas and DD downloaded an app for me to learn the rules of a new game. On Saturday evening as an experiment I played using the app on the ipad late into the evening, switching off just before I went to bed. Got into bed, room very dark and closed my eyes..... For several minutes I could see circles of playing cards going round and round .....  Really showed me the effect of technology on vision  Last night was computer technology off as soon as it got dark and blue blockers on. Remembered my dreams this morning

It did look like ceiling fan flutter, but in all "corners" of my vision. I closed my eyes and watched (they lasted only a minute or so) but I didn't detect any spin. \[If I turned toward the light with my eyes closed, they disappeared, only to reappear when I turned back towards the dark room.\] No rotation on their own axis, or about the center of my vision. ### nonchalant — 2015-01-15T00:25:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-effects.12478/post-152773>

A couple mornings ago I got another light show behind my eyelids. But this time the peripheral light ring got 'stuck' at the 10 o'clock position. The rest of the light band would contract towards the center of vision, but not that part. Then I noticed a clockwise sweep of light, like from a weather radar screen, that seemed to solve the problem. Over and over it happened, the same way each time. I was happy to see the rotation, which was a first for me.

But since I had read Tensegrity 13, I just had to try something. I quickly moved my closed eyes back and forth once. Then I watched for more smoke rings. But they would not reappear.  The movement had killed the show. I didn't even see any lava-lamp type effects.

That leads to another question. Does one have a 'dominant eye' (or dominant hand) if they lack energy to coordinate the vision from both eyes? Do we rely mostly on one eye to reduce chaos? Should ambidexterity be the norm for humans?

# Thread: Angies journey of Leptin Reset
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/angies-journey-of-leptin-reset.12481/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### angie-s — 2014-12-29T04:00:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/angies-journey-of-leptin-reset.12481/post-151111>

Hi ,

I am 65 years old, female living in Houston Texas for last 40 years. In general, I consider myself in good health. Can't remember when I last (or ever) had a flu or headache. I do have T2D and high blood pressure. I was diagnosed with T2D after my total hysterectomy in 1998. Not sure if that caused it. I had to have blood before and during surgery because my Hgb was around 4 due to excessive bleeding during my periods caused by fibroids on the uterus

I also have allergies and have been having skin rashes for last 30 yrs. Usually I take anti-histamine (prescription grade) and use ice packs to get over them. No doctor seems to ever figure out why I have skin problems. I have been tested for every thing during these years and I am always told that everything is fine. Last year around this time the rashes became so bad and unbearable that I could not function or sleep. This went on for 6 months. I tried everything , went to dermatologists, allergists and many other doctors to find out what was wrong. No one could figure it out. They gave me steroids which did not work. They said they cant give higher doses of steroids because of diabetes. In the end I was given some anti-biotics which helped some.

This is when I decided to stop all my diabetes medications thinking that one of them may be causing the allergic reaction. To my amazement, I found out that many of the stomach issues I was having all went away when I stopped the medicines and the rash also got a little better. I was started on insulin for sugar control. I have always been very skeptic of doctors, so I started doing my own research to cure myself. During this research I came across Dr. K's site. Since I am a Chemical Engineer by education, his blogs were very interesting to me due to the organic and physical chemistry as well as physics discussed in detail by him. I was always very interested on how the brain functions so that was an added plus. I think he is great and finally I have found a doctor I can trust.

I have read most of his blogs, listened to pod casts. Reviewed all Q&A for the blogs I read and reviewed the monster thread at MDA. I try to keep notes of item that I think apply to me.

Based on these, I started the Leptin reset protocol in September 2014. I did my labs before I started and redid after 3 months. The results are below. I keep a spreadsheet of all lab results and also daily sugar readings. I am also taking the supplements recommended.

Although, I have lost about 15-17 lbs (now 155 lbs at 5'4") and see better labs (lower HS-CRP etc.), it seems that I have stalled now for past 2-3 weeks and see/feel no further improvements. I am also a little concerned that some of the things went in the wrong direction on the Leptin reset, such as leptin, adiponectin, BUN etc. and am wondering what I am doing wrong. Also I do not know if my Rev T3 , free T3 and Cortisol are OK. I had to fight the doctors to get the tests done and they tell me there is nothing wrong but I am not sure.

I use CT about 3 times a week on portions of my body. Minimize EMF at night (have f.lux on computer) and I use the glasses to ward off blue light. I follow the Leptin Rx diet. Only eat fish and coconut oil these days. I drink a lot of water. We have the RO filter on drinking water. My lab results showed no flourides or bromides.

I following the light cycles. Do not snack between meals and do not eat anything after 6:30 p.m. Sleep at 10:30 p.m and wake up at 5:30 a.m on weekdays and 6:30 a.m on weekends. Sometimes I get up around 1:30 a.m to pee.

I am also following the leaky gut protocol for last 2 weeks.

I would greatly appreciate if someone can help me understand what I am doing wrong.

I use blue blockers at night and even during the day when I am on the computer. I use sheildite for EMF control and EMF are minimal per the measurements from my meter. I have f.lux on my computer and have only red light in the evening. I do CT at least 5 times per week. I even put IR light in my nose. On weekends I am out in the sun most of the time doing gardening and weekdays I try to get at least 15 minutes in the morning and about an hr after work.

Even with all this there has been no improvement in the last 5 months. During the first 4 months I had lost about 20 lbs that is where I continue to be (146 lbs at 5 ' 4").

my homocysteine is at 14 (high)

### av8r — 2015-06-10T15:31:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/angies-journey-of-leptin-reset.12481/post-167392>

**US Amateur Transmitter Power Limits**

At all times, transmitter power must be the minimum necessary to carry out the desired communications. Unless otherwise noted, the maximum power output is 1500 watts PEP. Novice/Technicians are limited to 200 watts PEP on HF bands. Geographical power restrictions apply to the 70 cm, 33 cm and 23 cm bands; see _The FCC Rule Book_ for details.

With all the focus on Wi-Fi and cell towers here is something a little less thought of… amateur radio operators in your neighborhood. They typically use directional antennas and that’s a lot of RF energy (1500 watts) if its pointed in your direction!

[http://www.arrl.org/advanced-call-sign-search](http://www.arrl.org/advanced-call-sign-search) Enter your city and state for a list of amateur radio stations in your area.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-31T21:13:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/acute-pancreatitis-in-a-12-year-old-girl.12485/post-151259>

Chronic pancreatitis (CP) is characterized by recurrent inflammatory attacks that can gradually cause irreversible damage to the pancreas and the surrounding tissue. Contributing risk factors. Exocrine pancreatic function depends on a balance of enzyme activation and inhibition which can be affected by multiple environmental and genetic factors. While alcoholism is thought to be the primary environmental cause of chronic pancreatitis, research points to inherited causes for chronic pancreatitis.

This is tied to three possible epigenetic/genetic defects. Mutations in cystic fibrosis transmembrane conductance regulator (CFTR), in cationic trypsinogen (PRSS1) and in serine protease inhibitor Kazal type 1 (SPINK1) genes have been associated with chronic pancreatitis. _CTRC_ gene is also a new target in this family of disorders.
This pancreatitis can be alcohol related, idiopathic and hereditary.

Hereditary pancreatitis is an autosomal dominant condition that can result from a mutation in the major pancreatic gene, _PRSS1_. At least 17 mutations have been described, R122H being the most common on the _PRSS1_ gene. _SPINK1_ is a modifier gene that acts to inhibit potential trypsin activity in the pancreas. _SPINK1_ mutations impair this protective function and have been identified as risk factors in idiopathic, familial, tropical and alcoholic pancreatitis. At least 14 mutations have been identified, N34S being the most common on the _SPINK1_ gene.

Cystic Fibrosis (CF) is an autosomal recessive disorder that results from mutations in the _CFTR_ gene. CF has an incidence of 1 in 3200 live births in the U.S, with a carrier frequency of 1 in 25 Non-Hispanic Caucasians. Research shows a link between CF and pancreatitis.

Pancreatitis Panel uses gene sequence analysis to test for _PRSS1, SPINK1_ and _CFTR_ mutations. It does not include analysis of _CTRC_.

Individual _PRSS1_ and _SPINK1_ full gene analysis is available.

I wrote about CFTR defects in Tensegrity 1 blog post so you should read about it. Mutations in the CFTR gene represent the major cause of idiopathic chronic pancreatitis in South American patients. It is rare to find mutation in the PRSS1 gene among South American patients suggesting further genetic heterogeneity for hereditary and idiopathic chronic pancreatitis. Interestingly, the most frequent SPINK1 N34S mutation is rare in South America. Moreover, the -253C allele for the SPINK1 gene was significantly more frequent in South America, suggesting where you live is tied to the defect you might have.

There is a webinar upcoming on this disorder @: [https://ambrygen.webex.com/mw0401ls...ef94ff60bf7d8597e3474a5495&&&siteurl=ambrygen](https://ambrygen.webex.com/mw0401lsp11/mywebex/default.do?nomenu=true&siteurl=ambrygen&service=6&rnd=0.19825304026941637&main_url=https%3A%2F%2Fambrygen.webex.com%2Fec0701lsp11%2Feventcenter%2Fevent%2FeventAction.do%3FtheAction%3Ddetail%26confViewID%3D1717287693%26%26EMK%3D4832534b0000000234628be1bde4be08941ff120851b3c20c04574ef94ff60bf7d8597e3474a5495%26%26%26siteurl%3Dambrygen)

### Valerie — 2015-01-01T03:24:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/acute-pancreatitis-in-a-12-year-old-girl.12485/post-151281>

My son got acute pancreatitis from a gall stone that got stuck in the canal (age 19). Turned out he has some form of hemolytic anemia (gall stone had high billirubin content). He actually had some previous episodes of stomach issue years before this. Unfortunately he no longer has a gall bladder.

### Valerie — 2015-01-01T05:13:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/acute-pancreatitis-in-a-12-year-old-girl.12485/post-151289>

Should mention that the best diagnostic and simple test is the reticulocyte count for his form of hemolytic anemia. It shows how hard your bone marrow is producing more red blood cells. Normal values are 0.5 - 2%, where my son's was 5-7%.

Other more obvious test include RBC and hemoglobin, but they could still be in the normal range.

### ssj3 — 2015-01-01T05:54:37+0200

### Dan Payne — 2015-01-06T18:07:52+0200

Fats n6:n3

# Thread: Neurological conditions can cause constipation in newborns?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neurological-conditions-can-cause-constipation-in-newborns.12490/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lucian Toma — 2014-12-29T21:44:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neurological-conditions-can-cause-constipation-in-newborns.12490/post-151152>

My two mounths newborn cannot passing his poo only with a glycerin suppository. Strange that are not hard. But after that, he has some seizures and agitation which last for many hours. He is fed with expresed milk mainly and with soy formula sometimes. Can the gut brain to control the defecation and because my wife has a C section does he not have an apropiate gut flora and his nervos system is overloaded?

What can be done in this case? Thank you

### Jude — 2014-12-30T04:02:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neurological-conditions-can-cause-constipation-in-newborns.12490/post-151163>

Hi Lucian, just hope you have been reading all of the early JK blogs...looks like mum and bub are going to need your knowledge to get out of this difficulty....modern medicine will offer you pharmaceuticals to alleviate condition......but this is the best place to learn how to reverse engineer all health problems and in particular your babes.
Do you have JK's book..The epi-paleo prescription?

Your baby needs tons of breast milk and no soy ...ever....feed mum up on lots of good fatty fish...salmon, sardines,oysters to support good milk supply...JK recommends breastfeeding for 2yrs.
I know it's a lot to take in ...keep asking questions
Lots of folk here with tons of info ...keep us up to date re bub

### nonchalant — 2014-12-30T05:00:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neurological-conditions-can-cause-constipation-in-newborns.12490/post-151166>

Welcome, Lucian. I have to wonder about your baby's circadian cycle. And his mother's, also. We normally have higher levels of cortisol in the morning. The rising sun, bright in our eyes, causes a huge spike in cortisol, which helps set the circadian rhythm. It also increases our energy levels and affects many other hormone levels.

I had some constipation problems a couple years ago, and I found that just looking towards the sun, or even at just blue sky, would help the situation in a few minutes. If you are in the northern hemisphere, your wife might be reluctant to take the herself and the baby outside very often. But I think it would be helpful.

### Penny — 2015-01-01T07:14:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neurological-conditions-can-cause-constipation-in-newborns.12490/post-151295>

Hi Lucian - too many glutamates - like in soy/wheat/dairy/processed food - causes excitotoxicity in the brain (migraines/seizures) - avoid glutamates and it's smooth sailing - you can also use slippery elm to soften the stool - and a good prebiotic and probiotic - your kid didn't get mom's flora - not good - who knows what he picked up in the hospital -

get this probiotic:
[http://www.amazon.com/ProBiota-ProB...id=1420088115&sr=8-32&keywords=seeking+health](http://www.amazon.com/ProBiota-ProBiotic-Absorbs-Quickly-Dairy-Free/dp/B003RY51GO/ref=sr_1_32?ie=UTF8&qid=1420088115&sr=8-32&keywords=seeking+health)
and this prebiotic which has galactooligosacharides that feed bifida bacteria - the predominant bacteria in a newborn:
[http://www.amazon.com/ProBiota-Immu...&sr=8-1-spell&keywords=seeking+health+prebiot](http://www.amazon.com/ProBiota-Immune-grams-Seeking-Health/dp/B004JCL3E2/ref=sr_1_sc_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1420088207&sr=8-1-spell&keywords=seeking+health+prebiot)
also - watch these videos for what horrors glutamates to to us:
[http://unblindmymind.org/](http://unblindmymind.org/)
They also have a glutamate list at the above site - glutamates are put in all processed food because they "tickle" your neurons and make them think the food tastes good and make you want more of it... then the neuron dies...

The opposite of glutamates is GABA - this affects the motility of the colon -

This is why wheat is a cork Happy New Year

Also, if you want to know the exact flora of your child, go to the [www.americangut.org](http://www.americangut.org) project and they will get the DNA of your child's gut flora so you can see what is in there...it's only $99.00 and you can do it from many parts of the globe

Not sure if mom eats glutamates if it transfers to the baby, but look it up and report back to us

# Thread: LDL Cholesterol Level
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ldl-cholesterol-level.12491/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Allin — 2014-12-30T06:43:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ldl-cholesterol-level.12491/post-151172>

I seem to recall Jack said something like, "when LDL dropped below 200 it was not a good thing." I can't find the blog where I read that. I keep reading in the data from the "medical community" that LDL above 100 is not optimal. Of course not many are talking about LDL particle size A and B either. Anyone?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-30T06:59:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ldl-cholesterol-level.12491/post-151175>

[http://jackkruse.com/cellular-depletions-why-should-you-care/](http://jackkruse.com/cellular-depletions-why-should-you-care/) you need a pic up.

### nonchalant — 2014-12-30T16:25:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ldl-cholesterol-level.12491/post-151184>

> [Allin said:](/goto/post?id=151172)
>
> I seem to recall Jack said something like, "when LDL dropped below 200 it was not a good thing." I can't find the blog where I read that. I keep reading in the data from the "medical community" that LDL above 100 is not optimal. Of course not many are talking about LDL particle size A and B either. Anyone?

Hi Allin. If my **total cholesterol** were to drop back below 200 I would be concerned. Lower cholesterol levels (like lower levels of vitamin D, cholesterol's cousin) are bad news and could lead to diseases including cancer.
A better thing for the medical community to say would be that damaged (oxidized) LDL above 100 is not optimal.

# Thread: Happy New Year!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-new-year.12499/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 30
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2014-12-30T22:46:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-new-year.12499/post-151203>

Happy New Year to all from the Southern hemisphere!

It is a rainy overcast day today .... yesterday was gorgeous and I was able to spend a lot of time at the ocean and swimming in the rock pool and laying on the rocks with a very special person .... pure and simple magic! Lots of people chatting to each other and enjoying all the beauty - taking every single moment in.
In my opinion - it doesn't get much better

It is wonderful to see all of us moving forward as JK shines his flashlight for us. It certainly isn't linear sometimes .... but every single day I learn so much about the world around me and about myself.

I feel so energised and hopeful every day ....in spite of all the brain pain .....and the never ending blogs - LOL

My life has changed dramatically - especially the last six months ...and I can look back and see a truly amazing journey the last four years at jackkruse.com.... my whole life has been magic - full stop!

What plans do you have to make this an even more memorable year?

What is on your bucket list??????

Happy New Year Everyone!

Happy New Year Dr. K. to you and yours ...... thanks for everything.....so glad I was smart enough to become quantumly entangled from the very beginning!

### MrPinkies — 2014-12-31T00:01:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-new-year.12499/post-151208>

First, Happy New Year, Audrey! The past two years of your encouragement have been priceless, Thank You! and I agree.. life has certainly and undeniably changed.. I cannot wait for the growth of next year... moving forward.. the idea and actual doing is so grand..

My bucket list...
First and foremost, I want to eat my first oyster.. Oysters around here are very expensive.. but.. I want to do it and feel awesome... I want to surprise Chris with going to a raw bar I found- they have a dollar per oyster happy hour..
I CANNOT wait for the filming of the new movie in February!!!
Chris and I want to buy a house... we have been seriously considering moving south.. just have to find the balls to do it.. we know very few people down there.. and it's somewhere new.. and learning all the ins and outs that is so scary for us- especially finding the Autism resources... and of course... finding jobs..
Chris hopes to go back to school and find a new job that he likes...

Much love to you and yours.. xoxo

Quantum entangled... <-- I like!

### Albert83BCN — 2015-01-02T13:11:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-new-year.12499/post-151366>

The economy is bad, really bad, let's just forget about this

I haven't yet listen to this Q&A but will do, as for the wiki thing, I use a plugin on my site that is prety cool, its called [CM Tooltip for Wordpress](http://www.cminds.com/store/tooltipglossary/?af=170) and it allows to build a glossary on your blog. It requires some effort to kick in but for a blog with a nature such as this one or mine, that is always dealing with biochemistry, biology, QED terminology and the likes, it's cool because once you make an entry for the word it will be always linked every time it's used on a future blog entry. So let's say you create a wiki for DHA and what it does at a quantum level on the brain, it will appear highlighted next time as a popup so it helps a lot clarifying things. Of course you could keep linking to the Wikipedia each time but for very recurrent terms it's a pain in the ass, this way you can also give it the certain meaning that suits your site (ie QED) as well, you cannot control the way wikipedia presents it's info.

I really think it would be worth this site staff to have a look at it

### caroline — 2015-01-03T01:32:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-new-year.12499/post-151414>

Sean started a bit of a blog a while back ....and that is what he did/used - it was great ....and saves so much time. it gets so frustrating to always have to google words when reading a blog.
